Title: Analista de Datos
Description:
Overview

CARRERAS PARA SONREÍR

En PepsiCo, descubrirás un lugar donde nuestra misión es crear sonrisas alrededor del mundo. Con más de 500 marcas queridas, incluyen Gatorade, Lay's y Quaker y nuestro trabajo llega a millones de personas cada día.

En el corazón de nuestra compañía hay un equipo de pensadores y creadores que colaboran para innovar y convertir las ideas en acciones. Impulsados por la innovación y centrados en crear momentos de alegría a través de los alimentos y bebidas, nuestras decisiones se guían por y para el consumidor, creando oportunidades para que nuestros asociados realicen un trabajo significativo y tengan un impacto duradero en las comunidades a las que servimos.

Sea cual sea tu función, formarás parte de una comunidad global que valora tus ideas y te empodera para que tengas impacto en tu carrera y en el mundo que te rodea.

La oportunidad

Tu impacto

El Analista de Datos con Enfoque en Servicio de Ventas es responsable de recopilar, procesar y analizar información clave para apoyar la toma de decisiones comerciales. Su función combina habilidades analíticas con atención al cliente interno brindando soporte operativo al área de ventas mediante reportes, seguimiento de indicadores y gestión básica de documentación utilizando herramientas del paquete Office.

Responsibilities


Recopilar, depurar y analizar datos provenientes de diversas fuentes para generar reportes comerciales y operativos.
Elaborar dashboards e indicadores clave de desempeño (KPIs) que faciliten el monitoreo de resultados del área de ventas.
Brindar soporte directo al equipo comercial en requerimientos de información, cotizaciones, seguimiento a clientes (alta, bajas y Modificaciones)
Mantener actualizadas bases de datos de clientes, productos, ventas y oportunidades.
Mantener un sano unventario de Dispositivos celulares y Impresoras Preparar informes periódicos en Excel, PowerPoint y Word para presentaciones internas.
Detectar tendencias, variaciones y oportunidades de mejora en el comportamiento de ventas.
Dar seguimiento a solicitudes de clientes y coordinar la comunicación con áreas involucradas.
Apoyar en la automatización o simplificación de procesos mediante herramientas básicas del paquete Office.
Conocimientos y Habilidades Análisis de datos: manejo de bases de datos, interpretación de información, análisis estadístico básico, elaboración de reportes.
Ventas y servicio al cliente: comunicación efectiva, seguimiento de clientes, comprensión del ciclo comercial.
Paquete Office (nivel básico–intermedio): Excel: tablas, filtros, gráficos, fórmulas básicas. PowerPoint: elaboración de presentaciones ejecutivas. Word: redacción y formateo de documentos.
Pensamiento crítico orientado a resultados.
Organización, atención al detalle y control documental.
Capacidad para trabajar bajo objetivos y múltiples solicitudes simultáneas.


Qualifications

¿A quién buscamos?


Formación académica en Administración, Negocios, Mercadotecnia, Sistemas o afines.
Experiencia en análisis de datos, soporte comercial o servicio al cliente (Deseable).
Disponibilidad para trabajar de lunes a sábado.
Excel intermedio.
Habilidad para trabajar en equipo, resolver problemas y mantener una comunicación clara con áreas comerciales.
Ubicación: Gudalupe, Zacatecas.


Si esta es una oportunidad que te interesa, te alentamos a postularte aún si no cumples con el 100% de los requisitos.

Qué puedes esperar de nosotros:


Oportunidades para aprender y desarrollarte todos los días a través de una amplia oferta de programas.
Plataformas internas digitales que promueven el self-learning.
Programas de desarrollo de acuerdo con habilidades de Liderazgo.
Entrenamientos especializados de acuerdo con el rol.
Experiencias de aprendizaje con proveedores internos y externos.
Nos encanta celebrar el éxito, por lo que contamos con programas de reconocimiento por antigüedad, comportamientos, liderazgo, momentos de vida, entre otros.
Programas de bienestar financiero que te ayudarán a alcanzar tus metas en todas las etapas de la vida.
Un programa de flexibilidad que te permitirá balancear tu vida personal y laboral, adaptando tu jornada laboral a tu estilo de vida.
Y porque tu familia también es importante para nosotros, ellos también pueden gozar de beneficios como nuestra Línea de Bienestar, miles de Convenios y Descuentos, programas de Becas para tus hijos, Planes de Ayuda para diferentes momentos de vida, entre otros.


En PepsiCo, estamos comprometidos con impulsar un equipo de trabajo diverso al crear un espacio colaborativo, equitativo e incluyente, en donde todos y todas independientemente de cómo nos vemos, de dónde somos o a quién amamos- tengamos una voz.

Ofrecemos oportunidades únicas para contratar personas calificadas y diversas, independientemente de su género, raza, orientación sexual, religión, nacionalidad, edad o discapacidad.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Title: Data Analyst – Analytics & Data Operations

Location: Roswell, GA

Duration: Long Term




Hiring Manager Notes

10+ years of experience

Excellent communication skills

Exceptional stakeholder engagement skills — comfortable managing expectations and communicating with both business and technical audiences

Experience in data analysis and data visualization.

Hands-on experience with at least one BI tool (Power BI, Tableau or similar)

Knowledge of ETL is a plus but not mandatory.




About the Role

We are seeking a Data Analyst who will play a critical role in delivering reliable insights, working closely with stakeholders, and ensuring data accuracy across the organization.

This role goes beyond traditional reporting; the analyst will actively investigate and resolve data issues, collaborate with cross functional teams, and provide operational support for analytics systems.

The ideal candidate is analytically strong, detail oriented, comfortable working with ambiguity, and capable of balancing business analysis with data operations and system support.




Key Responsibilities

Analytics & Business Insights

Partner with business and technical stakeholders to understand objectives and translate them into clear analytical requirements

Perform ad hoc and recurring analyses to support business decision making

Analyse large datasets to identify trends, risks, anomalies, and opportunities

Align and maintain data definitions, metrics, and business rules across teams

Present insights through dashboards, reports, and clear written or verbal summaries to both technical and non-technical audiences




Data Quality, Issue Resolution & Root Cause Analysis

Proactively identify, investigate, and resolve data discrepancies across source systems, pipelines, and downstream reporting

Perform root cause analysis in collaboration with data engineering, platform, application, and business teams

Validate fixes, implement preventative controls, and ensure issues do not recur

Act as a primary point of contact for data related issues, troubleshooting, and inquiries




Data Operations & Systems Support

Monitor data pipelines, dashboards, and jobs for failures or abnormal behaviour

Support analytics and reporting systems during incidents, outages, or data availability issues

Conduct post deployment testing and validation to ensure data accuracy and system stability as needed

Identify opportunities to improve automation, monitoring, alerting, and data workflows




Required Qualifications

7+ years of experience as a Data Analyst or Analytics-driven role

Strong proficiency in SQL or Python (required)

Experience working with databases and large, complex datasets

Strong experience with data visualization or reporting tools (Power BI, Tableau, or similar)

Solid understanding of data validation, data quality, and reconciliation techniques

Demonstrated ability to independently investigate complex data issues from source systems through reporting layers

Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills

Excellent communication skills with the ability to work effectively across technical and non-technical teams




Preferred / Nice to Have Qualifications

Bachelor’s or master’s degree in business, Analytics, Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Math or a related field

Familiarity with cloud data warehouses and ETL/ELT tools

Exposure to data transformation tools (e.g., dbt)

Experience supporting production analytics systems or data platforms

Knowledge of basic statistics and experiment analysis (e.g., A/B testing)

Comfort participating in off peak deployments and releases when needed

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Hybrid 4 days onsite, 1 remote. in Englewood, CO




Our client seeks a junior data analyst to build and maintain operational dashboards in Grafana that support DAA infrastructure, RPD activation workflows, and telemetry from vCMTS devices. The role focuses on data visualization, query authoring across MongoDB aggregation pipelines and SQL systems, and clear documentation. You will collaborate with a senior lead, translate business requirements into usable dashboards, and troubleshoot data issues. Curiosity, problem solving, and comfort with structured data will be important.




Due to client requirements, applicants must be willing and able to work on a w2 basis. For our w2 consultants, we offer a great benefits package that includes Medical, Dental, and Vision benefits, 401k with company matching, and life insurance.




Rate: $30.00 to $40.00/hr. w2




JN -052026-106928




Responsibilities

Build, maintain, and improve Grafana dashboards used by operations and engineering teams.
Write and optimize queries for MongoDB aggregation pipelines and SQL-based data stores such as StarTree Pinot.
Translate business requirements into clear, usable dashboard visualizations.
Troubleshoot data issues and dashboard behavior.
Advise on data structure improvements to support better reporting.
Document dashboard designs, query logic, and data models.




Experience Requirements

Foundational understanding of computer science concepts including data structures, logic, and system interactions.
Experience writing SQL or a similar language.
Comfort working with structured data formats such as JSON and CSV.
Basic understanding of databases including querying, data organization, and joins.
Ability to read and reason about code.
Strong problem-solving approach.
Intellectual curiosity and a desire to understand underlying causes.
Clear written and verbal communication.
Coachable with the ability to apply feedback.
Attention to detail to ensure data accuracy.
Exposure to Grafana or BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, or Power BI (preferred).
Familiarity with MongoDB or NoSQL databases (preferred).
Familiarity with time-series or streaming data such as Kafka or PromQL (preferred).
Exposure to JSON manipulation or data transformation scripting in Python or JavaScript (preferred).
Comfort working in a telecom or infrastructure operations environment (preferred).
Interest in network technology or cable/broadband systems (preferred).
Experience presenting data or insights to non-technical audiences (preferred).




Education Requirements

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Analytics, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.

Title: Data Analyst, Advisory Data & Research
Description:
Primary Talent Partners has a new contract opening for a Data Analyst, Advisory Data & Research with our commercial real estate client in Richardson, TX. This is a 6-month contract with a potential for extension.

Pay: $40.00 - $47.00/hr; W2 contract, no PTO, no Benefits. ACA-compliant supplemental package available for enrollment. Candidates must be legally authorized to work in the United States and must be able to sit on Primary Talent Partners W2 without sponsorship.


Description:
Client's Advisory business runs on data — and the analysts on your team are how that data becomes insight, decisions, and competitive advantage for clients around the world. As a Data Analyst on the Advisory Data & Research team, you'll own analytical workstreams end-to-end, partner closely with a scrum team, and sit at the intersection of data, technology, and the business. You'll be close to the data — and close to the users — shaping what gets measured and why.

This isn't a reporting-factory role. You'll help frame the questions and define the methodology. You will drive the analysis, and be accountable for the insights. You'll partner with UX designers and product managers to embed analytics people actually use, push engineering teams on data quality and pipeline reliability, and represent your findings to business leaders and D&T leadership alike.


What You'll Do:
Own analytical workstreams end-to-end — synthesize input from users, business stakeholders, and D&T leadership into a clear, prioritized set of questions to answer and metrics to track.
Build, maintain, and refine dashboards, reports, and self-service analytics: write clean, well-documented SQL, groom data backlogs, lead refinement sessions with stakeholders, and actively participate in scrum ceremonies, demos, and lead user testing and rollout of analytical products.
Drive data discovery alongside Product and UX partners — conduct user research on analytical needs, run feedback sessions, and champion the end-user perspective in every metric and visualization decision.
Translate business questions into execution by collaborating closely with Engineering and Data Engineering, turning analytical requirements into well-modeled, reliable, queryable data.
Define and deliver specific analyses end-to-end, from problem framing through validation, presentation, and measurement of impact.
Represent your analysis to business leadership and D&T leadership — providing crisp, regular readouts and connecting day-to-day work to broader strategic goals.
Spot data quality problems before they become blockers: proactively investigate anomalies, resolve issues, and communicate with clarity and urgency when it matters.
Model RISE values on your team and in every cross-functional interaction — lead by example, build trust fast, and bring people along.


What You'll Need:
3-5+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field — with a track record of delivering analyses that influenced decisions. A bachelor's degree in a quantitative discipline is preferred; relevant experience counts.
Strong SQL skills with hands-on experience querying large, complex datasets, plus fluency building dashboards and reports in modern BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar). Proficiency in Python or R for analysis and automation is a plus.
Strong instincts for prioritization — you know which questions matter, and you know how to explain why.
The communication skills to work across levels: from a debugging session with a data engineer to a C-suite readout, you adjust your style and land your message.
A bias for the truth — you ask hard questions of the data, validate your assumptions, and let what you find change your mind.
Comfort navigating ambiguity, managing competing priorities, and keeping work focused when requirements shift.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint) and familiarity with modern analytics and data warehousing platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks).
Experience with data modeling, data quality, and data governance — you understand data and how it interacts with products and decisions.
Experience in Commercial Real Estate (nice to have).

Title: Data Annotator (Canada)
Description:
Jobright is a next-generation AI job search platform built to make career navigation faster, smarter, and more personal. They are looking for a Data Annotator to craft the high-quality datasets that teach our AI agents how to read resumes, understand job markets, and guide people toward better careers.




Why Join Us




Your labeling decisions become the ground truth that defines how our AI agents behave in production
Watch agent quality climb week over week as the datasets you build feed directly into the next model iteration
Sit close to the applied AI and research teams, with a clear path to grow into AI engineering or ML roles down the line




Responsibilities




Annotate and review resumes, job postings, agent transcripts, and other text data that shape how AI agents learn and improve
Work through ambiguous cases with judgment and care, surfacing patterns and gaps that help the team tighten guidelines and reduce future errors
Team up with applied AI engineers to investigate where agents are failing, then design targeted labeling efforts that close those gaps
Contribute to the playbooks, quality audits, and reviewer workflows that keep annotation output consistent as the team and dataset grow




Qualifications




Required




Recent grad or early-career professional with 0 to 2 years of experience in annotation, content moderation, research, editorial work, or a similar field
Clear writer and communicator who can articulate why a label is correct and raise good questions when the guidelines don't cover a case
Meticulous eye for detail and steady judgment under ambiguity, paired with a working sense of how labeled data influences AI and ML model behavior




Preferred




Internship or project experience in data annotation, linguistics, qualitative research, or content operations at a tech or AI-focused company
Comfort moving through large volumes of nuanced material without losing accuracy, especially when the subject matter is unfamiliar at first
Familiarity with annotation platforms such as Label Studio or Scale, basic SQL or spreadsheet skills, and hands-on experience reviewing LLM outputs or prompt-based workflows

Title: Data Systems Analyst
Description:
Key Responsibilities:

Provide 24x7 support for client and user‑facing products and services with a strong focus on data quality, system reliability, and seamless integrations. Responsible for mapping and transforming legal practice data to enable downstream processes and analytics.

Design and build custom data integrations using Intapp Integrate (Integration Builder), leveraging Intapp APIs, data models, and workflow tools. Demonstrate expertise integrating with mission‑critical enterprise systems such as Active Directory/Azure AD, HRIS platforms, DMS, CRM, Finance/ERP, matter management, billing/time systems, and data warehouses.

Develop and execute SQL queries for data extraction, reconciliation, quality checks, troubleshooting, auditing, and performance validation, with strong competency in joining complex datasets and supporting integration logic through SQL.

Support automation and custom connector development through scripting—preferably using PowerShell, Python, or C#—to enable pre‑ and post‑processing operations and streamline data workflows.




Deploy, maintain, and troubleshoot core business applications, including configuration, reporting, and system optimization.

Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to define project scope, system objectives, and detailed requirements.

Develop, analyze, prioritize, and document requirement specifications, data mappings, diagrams, and process flows.

Translate complex technical specifications into clear, easy-to-understand non‑technical requirements.

Manage system setup, configuration, and environment preparation.

Define and coordinate testing activities, including test planning, test case development, and quality assurance oversight.

Create and maintain comprehensive documentation for system processes, workflows, and procedures.

Implement maintenance routines, monitor system performance, collect operational metrics, and resolve reported issues.

Design, implement, and upgrade information systems to meet evolving business and user needs.

Perform performance tuning and optimization of integration systems.

Install, upgrade, and administer integration platforms and related components.

Diagnose and troubleshoot integration and system-related errors.

Build and manage database reports, visualizations, and dashboards.

Automate recurring tasks to improve operational efficiency.

Develop and enforce policies, procedures, and standards for system management and governance.

Create and maintain backup and recovery strategies aligned with industry best practices.

Monitor and optimize integration system performance, providing recommendations to enhance scalability and efficiency.

Ensure system security, integrity, and availability, including planning and executing disaster recovery measures.




Qualifications:

Mandatory experience on Integration Builder

3–5+ years of experience delivering enterprise‑grade system integrations

Proficient in SQL including querying, joins, and basic performance tuning for validation, reconciliation, and issue resolution

Strong background in integration delivery and operational support, including:

Deep understanding of REST APIs, authentication methods (OAuth2, API keys, token-based auth), and webhooks

Skilled in working with JSON/XML, data mapping, transformations, and schema validation

Solid knowledge of core data‑integration concepts, such as:

Error handling, dead‑letter queues, monitoring, and alerting

Scheduling, incremental data loads, and change tracking

Experience supporting production integrations with a focus on stability and performance, including:

Managing SLAs, incident response, root‑cause analysis, and long‑term problem management

Proven ability to understand business requirements and translate them into effective technical solutions

Strong knowledge of application setup principles and best practices

Hands-on experience installing, configuring, documenting, testing, training, and deploying new applications and systems

Excellent analytical and problem‑solving skills

Advanced command of SQL and SQL Server toolsets

In‑depth understanding of database security, backup/recovery, and performance monitoring

Familiarity with relational and dimensional data‑modeling techniques

Scripting skills in PowerShell and Unix shell

Exposure to SSAS, SSIS, and SSRS

Exceptional written and verbal communication skills

High level of attention to detail




Compensation:

Exact compensation may vary based on skills and experience and salary range is $135,000 - $160,000.




About Us

At Herbert Smith Freehills Kramer, we align your growth and ambition with ours. We invest in your personal and professional growth and support you to achieve your ambitions. And you share responsibility for playing a part in delivering the firm’s growth and ambition too.




A leading global law firm, with over 6,000 people, we are in the world's largest markets, key financial centers and major growth hubs. We’re recognized leaders in demanding contentious matters, exacting regulatory work and complex public and private transactions.




We’re immersed in the many challenges facing our clients. We’re invested and resourceful. We understand the part technology and digitalization play in the delivery of legal services. We want to make a positive impact wherever in the world we operate. You’ll have the opportunity to engage with an open mind and curiosity.




We are known for our diverse perspectives and renowned for our culture. Being human, bold and outstanding are more than our values: you’ll discover they are our lived experience. And by being ambitious for your growth and ours, we’ll achieve our goals together.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Overall Summary:

The Senior Data Analyst is responsible for the operational integrity of IWCO Direct’s reporting ecosystem and for leading the migration from legacy platforms to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. This role sits at the intersection of hands-on technical delivery and strategic data governance; maintaining the reporting infrastructure the business depends on today while building the governed, self-service data platform it needs tomorrow. The right candidate brings deep SQL expertise, practical experience with modern BI platforms, and the ability to think architecturally about how data flows from source systems to business decisions.




Primary Duties/Responsibilities

Lead the migration of IWCO Direct’s analytics platform from SQL Server–based data warehouse and SSRS/Qlik reporting to Microsoft Fabric, including lakehouse design, data pipelines, and Power BI semantic models.
Define and execute a phased migration roadmap that balances business continuity with modernization progress.
Design and implement data pipelines that replace legacy ETL processes with Fabric-native workflows (Data Factory, dataflows, notebooks).
Architect Power BI semantic models with governance, row-level security, and reusability as core design principles.
Maintain and support existing SSRS reports, Qlik Sense dashboards, and SQL Server stored procedures/views that drive daily business operations.
Triage and resolve data quality issues, report failures, and performance problems across the current reporting stack, while identifying legacy reports and data flows that are candidates for retirement, consolidation, or migration.
Develop, document, and maintain governed data pipelines and curated datasets that internal departments use as the foundation for their own reporting and analysis.
Establish naming conventions, documentation standards, data dictionaries, and lineage tracking practices that make the data platform navigable and trustworthy.
Partner with business SMEs to understand their data needs, then deliver governed data products that enable self-service analytics at scale.
Define data quality standards and monitoring to ensure the reliability of data streams downstream consumers depend on.
Contribute to the department’s technology roadmap, including evaluation and adoption of AI capabilities as IWCO Direct’s data platform needs evolve.
Identify opportunities to apply automation, AI, and modern tooling to reduce manual reporting effort and improve data accessibility.
Stay current with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and broader data platform developments; bring recommendations for adoption back to the team.




Required Skills/Abilities/Competencies:

Advanced ability to problem-solve creatively and analyze processes and systems.
Ability to prioritize with minimal supervision while working independently or in a team environment.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with proficiency in documenting data models and explaining technical decisions to business audiences.
Strong understanding of data governance principles: data quality, documentation, lineage, access control, and naming standards.
Working knowledge of SSRS report development and maintenance.
Advanced SQL skills, comfortable writing and optimizing complex queries, stored procedures, views, and ETL logic against large-scale SQL Server databases.
Local candidates to the Greater Twin Cities area are preferred and prioritized

Education and Experience:

Bachelor’s degree in mathematics, computer science, economics, statistics, data science, engineering, or related quantitative field; equivalent experience considered in lieu of education.
5+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or data engineering roles.
Hands-on experience with Power BI development, including data modeling, DAX, and report/dashboard design.
Experience with data warehouse design concepts: dimensional modeling, star/snowflake schemas, slowly changing dimensions, ETL/ELT patterns.
Demonstrated experience leading or significantly contributing to a BI platform migration or major data infrastructure modernization effort.
Experience gathering business requirements from non-technical stakeholders and translating them into technical data solutions.
Experience with Microsoft Fabric (lakehouses, Data Factory, OneLake) or demonstrated ability to ramp quickly on emerging Microsoft data stack technologies preferred.
Familiarity with Qlik Sense development and administration preferred.
Experience building self-service analytics frameworks, including semantic models, curated datasets, or data products designed for business user consumption preferred.
Exposure to AI/ML concepts in an analytics context, including AI-driven data quality, automated insight generation, or AI assistant tooling preferred.
Experience working in manufacturing, direct mail, or marketing services industries preferred.
Familiarity with Azure DevOps or similar project management and CI/CD tooling preferred.

Physical Requirements:

Ability to work 8 hours consecutively.
Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer

  • Define and execute a phased migration roadmap that balances business continuity with modernization progress.
  • Design and implement data pipelines that replace legacy ETL processes with Fabric-native workflows (Data Factory, dataflows, notebooks).
  • Architect Power BI semantic models with governance, row-level security, and reusability as core design principles.
  • Maintain and support existing SSRS reports, Qlik Sense dashboards, and SQL Server stored procedures/views that drive daily business operations.
  • Triage and resolve data quality issues, report failures, and performance problems across the current reporting stack, while identifying legacy reports and data flows that are candidates for retirement, consolidation, or migration.
  • Develop, document, and maintain governed data pipelines and curated datasets that internal departments use as the foundation for their own reporting and analysis.
  • Establish naming conventions, documentation standards, data dictionaries, and lineage tracking practices that make the data platform navigable and trustworthy.
  • Partner with business SMEs to understand their data needs, then deliver governed data products that enable self-service analytics at scale.
  • Define data quality standards and monitoring to ensure the reliability of data streams downstream consumers depend on.
  • Contribute to the department’s technology roadmap, including evaluation and adoption of AI capabilities as IWCO Direct’s data platform needs evolve.
  • Identify opportunities to apply automation, AI, and modern tooling to reduce manual reporting effort and improve data accessibility.
  • Stay current with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and broader data platform developments; bring recommendations for adoption back to the team.


Required Skills/Abilities/Competencies:

  • Advanced ability to problem-solve creatively and analyze processes and systems.
  • Ability to prioritize with minimal supervision while working independently or in a team environment.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills with proficiency in documenting data models and explaining technical decisions to business audiences.
  • Strong understanding of data governance principles: data quality, documentation, lineage, access control, and naming standards.
  • Working knowledge of SSRS report development and maintenance.
  • Advanced SQL skills, comfortable writing and optimizing complex queries, stored procedures, views, and ETL logic against large-scale SQL Server databases.
  • Local candidates to the Greater Twin Cities area are preferred and prioritized

Education and Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in mathematics, computer science, economics, statistics, data science, engineering, or related quantitative field; equivalent experience considered in lieu of education.
  • 5+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or data engineering roles.
  • Hands-on experience with Power BI development, including data modeling, DAX, and report/dashboard design.
  • Experience with data warehouse design concepts: dimensional modeling, star/snowflake schemas, slowly changing dimensions, ETL/ELT patterns.
  • Demonstrated experience leading or significantly contributing to a BI platform migration or major data infrastructure modernization effort.
  • Experience gathering business requirements from non-technical stakeholders and translating them into technical data solutions.
  • Experience with Microsoft Fabric (lakehouses, Data Factory, OneLake) or demonstrated ability to ramp quickly on emerging Microsoft data stack technologies preferred.
  • Familiarity with Qlik Sense development and administration preferred.
  • Experience building self-service analytics frameworks, including semantic models, curated datasets, or data products designed for business user consumption preferred.
  • Exposure to AI/ML concepts in an analytics context, including AI-driven data quality, automated insight generation, or AI assistant tooling preferred.
  • Experience working in manufacturing, direct mail, or marketing services industries preferred.
  • Familiarity with Azure DevOps or similar project management and CI/CD tooling preferred.

Physical Requirements:

  • Ability to work 8 hours consecutively.
  • Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Why USAA?

At USAA, our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service and trusted advice. We seek to be the #1 choice for the military community and their families.




Embrace a fulfilling career at USAA, where our core values - honesty, integrity, loyalty and service - define how we treat each other and our members. Be part of what truly makes us special and impactful.




We are proud to support active-duty military spouses. USAA roles may offer remote or hybrid flexibility for active-duty military spouses consistent with applicable policy and business needs.







The Opportunity

As a dedicated Senior Decision Science Analyst for Bank Marketing Analytics, you will provide decision support for business areas across Bank marketing, and the Association, to drive member-centric value. Staff in this area will be responsible for applying mathematical and statistical techniques and/or innovative /quantitative analytical approaches to draw conclusions and make 'insight to action' recommendations to answer Bank marketing campaign and strategy business objectives and drive change. The essence of work performed by the Decision Science Analyst involves gathering, manipulating and synthesizing data (e.g., attributes, transactions, behaviors, etc.), models and other relevant information to draw conclusions and make marketing campaign recommendations resulting in implementable, member-centric strategies - Right Offer. Right Channel. Right Time.




We offer a flexible work environment that requires an individual to be in the office 4 days per week. This position can be based in one of the following locations: San Antonio, TX, Plano, TX, Phoenix, AZ, Colorado Springs, CO, Charlotte, NC, or Tampa, FL.




Relocation assistance is not available for this position.




What you'll do:

Leverages advanced business, analytical and technical knowledge to participate or lead discussions with cross functional teams to understand and collaborate highly complex business objectives and influence solution strategies.
Applies advanced analytical techniques to solve business problems that are typically medium to large scale with significant impact to current and/or future business strategy.
Applies innovative and scientific/quantitative analytical approaches to draw conclusions and make 'insight to action' recommendations to answer the business objective and drive the appropriate change.
Translates recommendation into communication materials to effectively present to various levels of management.
Incorporates visualization techniques to support the relevant points of the analysis and ease the understanding for less technical audiences.
Identifies and gathers the relevant and quality data sources required to fully answer and address the problem for the recommended strategy through testing or exploratory data analysis (EDA).
Integrates/transforms disparate data sources and determines the appropriate data hygiene techniques to apply.
Thoroughly documents assumptions, methodology, validation and testing to facilitate peer reviews and compliance requirements.
Understands and adopts emerging technology that can affect the application of scientific methodologies and/or quantitative analytical approaches to problem resolutions.
Succinctly delivers analysis/findings in a manner that conveys understanding, influences various levels of management, garners support for recommendations, drives business decisions, and influences business strategy.
Provides subject matter expertise in operationalizing recommendations.
Remains informed on current data and analytics trends, (Ex: Cloud, Data Mining, Python, Neural Networks, Sensor data, IoT, Streaming/NRT data).
Identifies opportunities to continue to learn in the data and analytics space, whether informal (e.g., Coursera, Udemy, Kaggle, Code Up, etc.) or formal (e.g.Certifications or advanced coursework).
Ensures risks associated with business activities are effectively identified, measured, monitored, and controlled in accordance with risk and compliance policies and procedures.




What you have:

Bachelor's degree in quantitative analytics field such as Economics, Finance, Statistics, Mathematics, Actuarial Sciences, Operations Research, Data and/or Business Analysis, Data Science or other quantitative discipline; OR 4 years of related experience in statistics, mathematics or quantitative analytics (in addition to the minimum years of experience required) may be substituted in lieu of degree.
6 years of data & analytics experience OR a minimum of 4 years of data & analytics experience and up to 2 years of progressive functional business relevant experience within the respective industry of responsibility (i.e.P&C, Bank, Finance, Marketing etc.) for a total of 6 years combined experience OR advanced degree in quantitative analytics field such as Economics, Finance, Statistics, Mathematics, Actuarial Sciences, Operations Research, Data and/or Business Analysis, Data Science or other quantitative discipline and 4 years of experience in data/analytics or functional business experience within the respective industry of responsibility (i.e.P&C, Bank, Finance, Marketing, etc.).
Demonstrates advanced skills in mathematical and statistical techniques and approaches used to drive fact-based decision-making.
Advanced knowledge of data analysis tools, data visualization, developing analysis queries and procedures in SQL, SAS, BI tools or other analysis software, and relevant industry data & methods and ability to connect external insights to business problems.
Experience with new and emerging data sets, and incorporation (data wrangling, data munging) into new insights.




What sets you apart:

US military experience through military service or a military spouse/domestic partner
Big Bank experience supporting Bank Product Marketing (Deposits, Credit Card, Lending) and Membership-based organizations
Big Bank experience with product profitability dynamics
Experience analyzing marketing channel data including digital placements, email, direct mail, paid search, affiliate, and social media
Experience working with Acquisition and Existing Customer Management (ECM) Marketing and cloud-based solutions like Salesforce, Pega, Snowflake, and AWS
Experience in Bank model forecasting, financial profitability, propensity, targeting optimization, next best action, marketing mix modeling, multi-touch attribution, last-touch attribution, and performance tracking
Experience in performing ad-hoc analytics using descriptive, diagnostic, and inferential statistics
Experience and/or general understanding of concepts and technologies associated with classical supervised modeling (linear/logistic regression, discriminant analysis, support vector machines, decision trees, forest models), unsupervised modeling (k-means clustering, hierarchical/agglomerative clustering, neighbors algorithms, DBSCAN)




Compensation range: The salary range for this position is: $114,080 - $218,030.




USAA does not provide visa sponsorship for this role. Please do not apply for this role if at any time (now or in the future) you will need immigration support (i.e., H-1B, TN, STEM OPT Training Plans, etc.).




Compensation: USAA has an effective process for assessing market data and establishing ranges to ensure we remain competitive. You are paid within the salary range based on your experience and market data of the position. The actual salary for this role may vary by location.




Employees may be eligible for pay incentives based on overall corporate and individual performance and at the discretion of the USAA Board of Directors.




The above description reflects the details considered necessary to describe the principal functions of the job and should not be construed as a detailed description of all the work requirements that may be performed in the job.




Benefits: At USAA our employees enjoy best-in-class benefits to support their physical, financial, and emotional wellness. These benefits include comprehensive medical, dental and vision plans, 401(k), pension, life insurance, parental benefits, adoption assistance, paid time off program with paid holidays plus 16 paid volunteer hours, and various wellness programs. Additionally, our career path planning and continuing education assists employees with their professional goals.




For more details on our outstanding benefits, visit our benefits page on USAAjobs.com.




Applications for this position are accepted on an ongoing basis, this posting will remain open until the position is filled. Thus, interested candidates are encouraged to apply the same day they view this posting.




USAA is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran.

Experience working in Agile environments

Title: Estimating Analyst
Description:
About The Role

The Estimating Analyst I is primarily responsible for providing construction labor and material costs associated to all new and existing designs. Included in this responsibility is the use of analytics to support the proposed costs as well as to identify opportunities for cost savings.

What You'll Do

Cost Analysis: New Designs and Elevations


Provide labor and material cost for new and existing projects to meet deadlines and address market needs
Analyze design costs and provide data supporting the proposed costs as well as identifying cost saving opportunities
Coordinate with the Design and Purchasing Departments to identify alternative construction methods


Revise Costs: Existing Designs


Review design changes to existing plans and update costs accordingly
Coordinate with Construction Managers and Vice Presidents of Construction to identify and address concerns


Daily Releases


Review daily reports to organize and manage the release schedule for new home budgets to be built
Provide final approval for new home release budgets



What We're Looking For


High School Diploma or equivalent required; Bachelor's degree preferred
Requires 0 to 1 year of relevant work experience
Some analyst experience preferred
The ability to interpret cost analytics to identify inaccuracies, trends, and saving opportunities
Demonstrated computer skills including a working knowledge of MS Office products, including Outlook, Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.



Why You Will Love Working Here

Competitive compensation and benefits package:


Medical, dental, vision coverage
Financial Planning
Time Off & Life Balance
Family & Lifestyle
Opportunities for growth and development
Culture that is collaborative, inclusive, fast-paced, people-first
Stable company with strong reputation in the market

Title: Data Entry Clerk
Description:
Job Summary 

Our client is seeking a Data Entry Clerk II to support their field operations team in Pine Valley, California! This position is responsible for employee timekeeping administration, records management, document retention, and administrative support activities. The ideal candidate will have strong data entry skills, excellent attention to detail, and experience working with timekeeping, payroll, workforce management, or administrative systems.



Candidates with experience supporting employee timekeeping, payroll processing, administrative operations, HR support, or records management are highly encouraged to apply.


  

This position is W-2 only. We do not work with third-party firms or C2C arrangements for this role.

Core Responsibilities 
Enter and maintain employee timekeeping records within the MyTime system
Review timesheets for accuracy and completeness
Research and resolve data discrepancies and missing information
Maintain Department of Transportation (DOT) records and documentation
Ensure compliance with document retention and recordkeeping requirements
Update and maintain databases, spreadsheets, and internal records
Generate reports and provide administrative support to operational teams
Organize, file, and manage sensitive employee and operational documentation
Support various administrative and data management projects as assigned
Required Skills/Experience (Must-Haves) 
High School Diploma or equivalent required
2+ years of experience in data entry, administrative support, payroll, workforce management, or related functions
Strong attention to detail and accuracy
Excellent organizational and time management skills
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Proficiency with Microsoft Office, including Excel
Preferred Skills/Experience (Nice-to-Haves) 
College coursework or degree preferred
Experience with MyTime, ADP, Kronos, UKG, Workday, or similar timekeeping systems
Payroll or workforce management experience
Records management or document control experience
Utility, construction, transportation, logistics, or field operations support experience
Key Competencies & Behaviors 
Attention to Detail
Organization and Reliability
Communication and Collaboration
Work Environment 
Location: Pine Valley, CA 
Onsite (Opportunity to work remotely 1 day per week after training is completed)
Core hours are 6:30 AM – 3:00 PM
Compensation & Benefits 
Pay Range: The approximate pay range for this position is between $18.00 and $22.00. Please note that the pay range provided is a good faith estimate. Final compensation may vary based on factors including but not limited to background, knowledge, skills, and location. We comply with local wage minimums.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About The Company

CAQH (Council for Affordable Quality Healthcare) is a nationally recognized nonprofit organization dedicated to streamlining healthcare administration processes to improve efficiency and reduce costs. With a focus on innovative technology solutions, CAQH works collaboratively with healthcare providers, payers, and other stakeholders to simplify administrative tasks such as credentialing, enrollment, and claims processing. Our mission is to create a more seamless and transparent healthcare system that benefits all parties involved, ultimately improving patient care and reducing administrative burdens. CAQH prides itself on fostering a culture of innovation, integrity, and excellence, attracting talented professionals committed to making a meaningful impact in the healthcare industry.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Data Analyst to join our team at CAQH. In this role, you will be responsible for analyzing complex healthcare data sets to identify trends, develop insights, and support strategic decision-making. The ideal candidate will possess strong analytical skills, a solid understanding of healthcare processes, and the ability to communicate findings effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. This position offers an exciting opportunity to contribute to projects that directly impact healthcare efficiency and patient outcomes. The Data Analyst will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, including IT, operations, and product development, to ensure data integrity and provide actionable insights that drive organizational improvements.

Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Healthcare Administration, or a related field; Master’s degree preferred.
Proven experience (3+ years) in data analysis, preferably within the healthcare industry.
Proficiency in data analysis tools such as SQL, Python, R, or SAS.
Strong understanding of healthcare data standards, such as HIPAA, HL7, and FHIR.
Experience with data visualization tools like Tableau, Power BI, or similar platforms.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Effective communication skills to present complex data insights clearly and concisely.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.


Responsibilities


Analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies relevant to healthcare administration processes.
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports to monitor key performance indicators and operational metrics.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data requirements and translate business needs into technical solutions.
Ensure data accuracy and integrity through validation and quality assurance procedures.
Provide actionable insights and recommendations to improve workflows, reduce costs, and enhance patient outcomes.
Support the development of data governance policies and best practices for data management.
Participate in ongoing training and development to stay current with industry trends and emerging technologies.


Benefits

CAQH offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support our employees' health, well-being, and professional growth. Employees enjoy competitive health insurance plans, including medical, dental, and vision coverage. We provide generous paid time off, including holidays and personal leave, to promote work-life balance. Our organization supports ongoing learning and development through training programs, conferences, and tuition reimbursement. Additionally, CAQH offers retirement savings plans with company matching, wellness programs, and a collaborative work environment that encourages innovation and teamwork. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where every employee can thrive and contribute meaningfully.

Equal Opportunity

CAQH is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected status under applicable law. All employment decisions are made based on qualifications, merit, and business needs. We encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply and join our mission to transform healthcare administration for the better.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Shift4 (NYSE: FOUR) is boldly redefining commerce by simplifying complex payments ecosystems across the world. As the leader in commerce-enabling technology, Shift4 powers billions of transactions annually for hundreds of thousands of businesses in virtually every industry. For more information, visit www.shift4.com.

As an AWS Cloud Data Engineer, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining robust data infrastructure solutions to support the organization’s data-driven initiatives. We seek self-driven and motivated individuals who take ownership of their projects. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams including project managers, business analysts, and software engineers to ensure the availability, reliability, and scalability of our system.

This role is onsite and can be based at any of the following Shift4 locations: USA: Tampa, FL / Atlanta, GA / Las Vegas, NV / Center Valley, PA

Canada: Vancouver, BC / Toronto, ON

Responsibilities

Technology:


Work hands on within the cloud data platform to deliver new features, fix issues and ensure best practices.
Contribute to the technical design and architecture development for parts of the solution. Ensure that best practices are defined and adhered to
Diagnose and resolve issues with deployed systems (as part of level 3 support).
Become technically solid for the cloud data platform.
Evaluate and resolve technical debt, engage in platform modernization and build reliable features.
Recommend and implement best practices and standards for the cloud data platforms.


People and Leadership


Work closely with multiple engineering teams, product owners and stakeholders to understand needs and meet data requirements.
Interface across multiple engineering teams and engineering groups working on various parts of the platform.
Mentor and develop junior team members.


Process


Ensure efficient operation of Scrum within the team.
Analyze and update tools and processes when required.
Measure key team metrics.


Qualifications


Bachelors or Masters degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field. Experience in lieu of a degree may be considered.
3+ years of experience in cloud data engineering.
2+ years working with AWS services including Redshift, AWS Glue, Lambda, DMS, Kinesis, etc.
1+ years working with ETL tools (MS SSIS or similar).
Significant hands-on experience working with databases, schemas, complex queries, data modeling, stored procedures, etc. (MS SQL Server, Postgres and Redshift preferred).
Excellent communication skills in English, with the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams and stakeholders.


Additional Experience That Is a Plus To Have


AWS Certified Data Engineer – Associate
AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner
AWS Certified AI Practitioner


We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any other federal, state or local protected class.

Description:

If you enjoy working directly with clients, solving problems, and being part of a fast-moving, collaborative team — this role offers hands-on experience and a clear path for career advancement.

Act as a key point of contact supporting client relationships and daily account communication
Build trust with clients through proactive outreach and strong follow-through
Assist in developing and executing client-facing strategies that drive engagement and success
Participate in client meetings, presentations, and strategic discussions
Partner with internal teams to ensure smooth delivery of services and solutions
Track account activity, performance metrics, and client feedback
Identify opportunities to strengthen partnerships and support business growth

Strong verbal and written communication skills
Relationship builder who enjoys working with people
Organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities
Self-starter with a growth mindset and leadership potential




Career Growth and Leadership!

This role is designed as a stepping stone into future leadership and management positions. You’ll receive mentorship, exposure to business strategy, and opportunities to take ownership of projects as you grow.




Working With Boost Company!

Direct exposure to clients and leadership
Competitive compensation and professional development support

Title: Quantitative Researcher
Description:
Top-tier systematic prop trading firm seeking a Sr Quantitative Researcher to lead equities alpha research during their most ambitious growth phase yet, doubling headcount, opening new US offices, and expanding globally in 2026.




Compensation starts at $750,000 (base of 250K) and reaches $1,500,000+ with PnL carry and bonuses. Role is on-site across New York, Chicago, Miami, London, and Singapore.




Role Includes

Own end-to-end alpha pipeline across cross-sectional and alternative equity datasets
Build and refine production-grade signal and factor models with real capital behind
Develop ML-driven approaches to forecast price behavior and market microstructure
Work directly alongside portfolio managers and quant developers in a lean, high-output tea

Must Have

5+ years in systematic equities research at a prop firm, quant fund, or hedge fund
Signals in production, you've seen your work trade live
Deep understanding of alpha decay, capacity constraints, and TO
Background in mathematics, statistics, physics, or engineering
You move fast, think independently, and don't need a committee to start research

HTML:
Top-tier systematic prop trading firm seeking a Sr Quantitative Researcher to lead equities alpha research during their most ambitious growth phase yet, doubling headcount, opening new US offices, and expanding globally in 2026.


Compensation starts at $750,000 (base of 250K) and reaches $1,500,000+ with PnL carry and bonuses. Role is on-site across New York, Chicago, Miami, London, and Singapore.


Role Includes

  • Own end-to-end alpha pipeline across cross-sectional and alternative equity datasets
  • Build and refine production-grade signal and factor models with real capital behind
  • Develop ML-driven approaches to forecast price behavior and market microstructure
  • Work directly alongside portfolio managers and quant developers in a lean, high-output tea

Must Have

  • 5+ years in systematic equities research at a prop firm, quant fund, or hedge fund
  • Signals in production, you've seen your work trade live
  • Deep understanding of alpha decay, capacity constraints, and TO
  • Background in mathematics, statistics, physics, or engineering
  • You move fast, think independently, and don't need a committee to start research

Title: Category Insights Analyst
Description:
Bringing our love of food to families for five generations! Blount Fine Foods is a family-owned and operated manufacturer, marketer, and developer of premium fresh prepared foods. We are an engaging team, bringing restaurant-quality products to America including single-serve grab-n-go fresh soups, mac & cheese, and entrées in grocery stores across the country, as well as for hot bars and restaurants. Help us create the finest food experiences including those with specialty certifications that include organic, gluten-free, and low sodium, among others. Join a proven team for growth, success, and a satisfying career!




Summary: The Associate Category Analyst is tasked with driving the short and long-term health and success of Blount’s owned and licensed CPG brands. Success will be measured by the development and execution of fact-based dashboards, reporting, and analytical models used to enhance decision making across the marketing and sales function to produce desired results. This role will work closely as part of the marketing and sales teams to track key activities, evaluate their success, and identify how they contribute to the sales pipeline and ensure they’re offering an adequate return on investment (ROI). We expect this role to leverage syndicated data sources and weave this quantitative analysis with qualitative information to drive our business forward. Additional internal systems, reporting and portals will be integral to leverage and understand in creating a full view and analysis of the business. The Associate Category Analyst will work proactively with cross-functional teams, including Sales & Marketing, R&D, Communications, Operations, and Customer Service, to understand data points applicable to each group and to advise on areas for tracking data.




Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

Sales and Promotional Analysis
Conducts category reviews and analysis, including category management, space management, product evaluation and promotional planning for strategic accounts.
Analyze retailer and shopper behavior, leveraging data and insights for actionable recommendations.
Identify trends and opportunities for brands and competitors and share within the sales, marketing and forecasting teams.
Manages and analyze syndicated and non-syndicated category data resources.
Develops insights, fact-based selling stories, and best practices for sharing with retailers
Identify opportunities to create analytics communications, reports and dashboards for the marketing and sales teams.
Develop materials and presentations to assist sales team in driving Blount brand across key retailers.
Partner with Sales team to build A/B testing methodologies to better understand promotion incrementality and fatigue.
Trade Management and Performance
For assigned retailers, ensure trade and promo activity is captured and reflects the latest available retailer knowledge.
Manage and monitor retailer-level trade expenditures and forecasts to provide actionable insights for the sales team and other key stakeholders.

Experience/Education:

Bachelor’s degree (B.A.) from a four-year college or university required: MBA preferred; 5+ years of related experience in a brand or category management role with a CPG company being a strong advantage.
Experience using Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, v-lookups and more).
Experience with Tableau, Syndicated data sources (IRI/Nielsen) and Power BI.







Our Total Compensation Package Includes:

Medical, dental and vision benefits.
401k with Company match.
Paid time off including vacation, sick time and holidays.
Education Assistance Program.
Life Insurance and Short-Term Disability.
Discounts on Blount products at Company retail location.
Discretionary Annual Bonus Program.

Title: Sr. Finance Analyst
Description:
At Acorns, our mission is to look after the financial best interests of the up-and-coming, beginning with the empowering, proud step of micro-investing. How do we accomplish our mission? Our values.

Lead With Heart - With compassion, integrity, and tenacity, inspire yourself and others to move past all previous thresholds of possibility

Make Bold Decisions - We are creating a new world. Be optimistic, adventurous, and courageous. Leap fearlessly into the future

Always Build Trust - Say what you mean, mean what you say, and do what you say you’re going to do. We are all owners. We are one team

Never Stop Growing - Change IS the constant. Stay hungry and curious. Be relentless in the pursuit of progress

Find a way - Never settle, no excuses, nothing is impossible. Just make it happen

Our values guide us, and our mission drives us. Come join us and help deliver financial wellness for the whole family, putting the tools of wealth-making into everyone’s hands.

_______________________________________________________________________

Sr. Finance Analyst | Acorns

Acorns is seeking an ambitious Sr. Finance Analyst to enable, inform, and improve strategic and financial decision-making across the company. This role will work directly with the VP of Accounting, Finance and Treasurer as well as teams throughout the organization, including product, engineering, marketing, legal, and operations.

Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA and New York, NY.

What You Will Do At Acorns


Introduce yourself to the organization and develop a deep understanding of the product, business model, and financial outlook.
Equip product managers and department leaders with monthly insights on business trends.
Support short- and long-term financial plans, assist in financial reporting and KPI variance analysis, and help drive key strategic initiatives.
Develop, maintain, and enhance corporate financial models, including annual budgets, forecasts, and consolidated cash flows.
Build KPI frameworks, analyze product line performance, and support high-impact decisions including go-to-market strategy, partnership negotiations, and potential M&A.
Create clear presentations to articulate findings to key stakeholders.


What You Will Bring To Acorns


Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Economics, Finance, Business, or another quantitative field.
Two or more years of relevant industry experience with financial modeling (e.g., investment banking, private equity, corporate finance).
Highly analytical mindset with the ability to execute complex financial modeling, analyze raw data, and draw clear conclusions.
High degree of organization, efficiency, and exceptional attention to detail.
Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across cross-functional teams.
A team-player mindset with a desire to roll up your sleeves and be hands-on in an entrepreneurial, fast-paced environment.
Strong sense of ownership, pride of work, and the ability to self-start.
Highest ethical standards, integrity, authenticity, credibility, and character.
Hunger to deliver game-changing products.
Exceptional drive and precision in delivery.
A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission.
Optimistic about the potential of societal change.
A strong passion for the Acorns’ mission.


You are not expected to have experience with all listed requirements. If you feel passionate about Acorns' mission, vision, and values, please apply.

What We Offer


Competitive salary and stock options.
A comprehensive benefits package for you and your family.
Flexible work location, hours, and paid time off.
401(k) matching.
Monthly Acorns account contribution & GoHenry account for your family.
Mindfulness and Financial Wellness resources, including Headspace and Addition Wealth.
Acorns Career Development Program (ongoing training sessions, development plans, development check-ins, and Cornerstone’s online training platform).
Roots Leadership Program for Emerging Leaders.
Community week onsite gatherings and various virtual events.
Talented and motivated team members who care deeply about one another, our mission, and our customers.
The rare opportunity to create a new world. We inspire one another every day to do meaningful work that solves big societal challenges.


About Acorns

Acorns is a financial wellness app that helps everyday people and families save and invest money for the long term. Since 2014, Acorns has grown into a global company with multiple life-stage products serving the needs of kids, teens, adults, and parents. Named one of Time’s “World’s Best Brands of 2024,” Acorns has helped over 14 million people save & invest over $25 billion dollars, much of it from spare change and small amounts.

Compensation Information

The pay range for this position at commencement of employment is expected to be between $125,000 and $135,000/year; however, base pay offered may vary depending on multiple individualized factors, including market location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. The total compensation package for this position may also include other elements, including a sign-on bonus, equity grants, and discretionary cash bonus awards in addition to a full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits (including 401(k) eligibility and various paid time off benefits, such as vacation, sick time, and parental leave), dependent on the position offered. Details of participation in these benefit plans will be provided if an employee receives an offer of employment.

If hired, the employee will be in an “at-will position” and the Company reserves the right to modify base salary (as well as any other discretionary payment or compensation program) at any time, including for reasons related to individual performance, Company or individual department/team performance, and market factors.

Work Authorization

Acorns participates in E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. For more information about E-Verify, please visit www.e-verify.gov

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Imagine working for one of New Zealand’s most iconic brands, but from a beautiful villa in Malta. That’s SkyCity Online. We’ve traded the traditional corporate grey office for a space that inspires creativity and collaboration. We are a people-first organization where "People & Culture" isn't just a department, it’s our identity. From our induction process to our commitment to work-life balance and high-quality internal assets, we ensure every team member feels at home from day one. Join us as we grow our Maltese presence.

About The Role

The Data Analyst is accountable for:


Deliver high-quality reporting and insights across online gaming products and platforms in regulated markets
Design and implement automated reporting, dashboards, and scalable data solutions to improve efficiency and reliability
Analyse complex datasets to identify trends, risks, and opportunities that inform product, commercial, and operational decisions
Monitor and improve data quality, working across internal teams and external vendors to ensure accuracy, integrity, and compliance


About You

You are a commercially minded and detail‑driven Data Analyst with strong experience working in complex, data‑rich digital environments. You thrive on turning large, messy datasets into clear, actionable insights and take pride in building reliable, scalable reporting solutions.

You are a self‑starter who enjoys working across systems, stakeholders, and vendors, and you bring a high level of rigour to data accuracy, documentation, and governance. You are confident engaging with both technical and non‑technical audiences, and you’re comfortable operating in regulated industries where compliance and precision are critical.

You bring proven experience in building dashboards, automating reporting, and analysing trends to support business decisions, ideally within online gaming or a similar regulated digital environment.

What’s in it for you?


Work on high impact digital initiatives shaping SkyCity’s online future 
A supportive, flexible, and inclusive workplace where you can thrive 
Opportunities for development and career growth 
Great SkyCity benefits


If you’re an experienced Data Analyst ready to elevate reporting, insights, and automation in a fast-moving, regulated digital environment, this role offers a great opportunity to make a meaningful impact. We look forward to hearing from you so please apply online and we’ll be in touch.

At SkyCity, our career opportunities are as diverse as our workforce. We don't just embrace differences; we value and support them. Our hiring is centred on capability and performance, regardless of gender, gender identity, race, origin, age, educational background, disability, or religious beliefs. We're here to empower you to shine. Our willingness to adapt ensures you can chart your path with us! Join us at SkyCity and be extraordinary by being you.  

SkyCity takes its commitment to Host Responsibility seriously. Every member of our team, no matter their role, contributes towards SkyCity's culture of customer care.

At SkyCity, our career opportunities are as diverse as our workforce. We don't just embrace differences; we value and support them. Our hiring is centred on capability and performance, regardless of gender, gender identity, race, origin, age, educational background, disability, or religious beliefs. We're here to empower you to shine. Our willingness to adapt ensures you can chart your path with us! Join us at SkyCity and be extraordinary by being you.  

SkyCity takes its commitment to Host Responsibility seriously. Every member of our team, no matter their role, contributes towards SkyCity's culture of customer care.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

32–40 hours per week | On-site, Malta 

 

Bits Malta Ltd is looking for a commercially-minded, curious Data Analyst; someone who doesn’t just produce charts, but turns data into decisions. An honest admission: our previous Data Analyst delivered many beautiful dashboards. But dashboards don't change a business. Insights do.

 

We're looking for someone who reads every number with the question: "So what?"

 

What you'll do

Own our data warehouse. It's the place where signals surface, and action begins.
Deliver a weekly strategic scorecard to the executive team, with your interpretation and recommendations.
Translate patterns in player behaviour, marketing, compliance and cost into concrete improvements.
Proactively flag what the organisation is missing.
Support marketing, product, customer service and compliance with analyses that actually lead to action.

 

You're strong in

SQL (required), Python or R, Power BI or Tableau, and expert-level Excel.
Commercial thinking. You see data as input for decisions, not as an end in itself.
Working independently, juggling multiple questions in parallel, and owning projects from question to decision.
Excellent English, both verbal and written.
Online gambling / iGaming experience and familiarity with AML / Responsible Gambling is a strong plus.

 

What we offer

Direct exposure to executive decision-making. Your work is seen and used.
International environment in a fast-growing, regulated sector.
Room for autonomy and development.
Competitive salary, strong benefits, private health insurance and performance bonus.
Relocation support for candidates moving to Malta.

 

Screening filter

In your motivation, include one concrete example of an analysis that actually changed a business decision. Without this example, we won't consider your application.

 

Selection process

Introductory interview covering background and motivation.
Data case. You receive an anonymised dataset, formulate three commercial insights with recommendations, and present them in 15 minutes to the management team.
Final interview with the executive team.

 

Practical

On-site position at our Malta office.
Bits Malta Ltd provides operational, compliance and customer service facilitation within the online gambling sector.

 

No agency recruitment, please.

#DataAnalyst #iGaming #Malta #Hiring #BusinessIntelligence #PowerBI #SQL #ResponsibleGambling

Title: Data Insights Analyst
Description:
At bet365, we're one of the world's leading online gambling companies, revolutionising the industry since 2000. Founded by Denise Coates CBE, we now employ over 9,000 people and serve over 100 million customers in 27 languages. Our focus on In-Play betting has solidified our market-leading position, offering an unmatched experience across 96 sports and 700,000 streaming events. With over 750 concurrent sporting fixtures at peak and more live sports streamed than anyone else in Europe, we handle over 6 billion HTTP requests daily and process more than 2 million bets per hour at peak.




We empower our employees to push boundaries and explore new ideas, cultivating a culture that celebrates and rewards creativity. This offers employees a wealth of opportunities for growth, giving them the opportunity to make a real impact in the world of online gambling. As a forward-thinking company, we’re breaking new ground in software innovation too, redefining what’s possible for our customers worldwide.




Job Description




We are seeking a highly analytical and commercially minded Gaming Insights Analyst to join our dynamic Insights team. In this role, you will be a crucial partner to the Casino Gaming and Marketing divisions, transforming raw data into the strategic intelligence that drives our business forward.




You will move beyond surface-level reporting to conduct deep, methodologically sound analyses of our Gaming products, marketing campaigns, and player behaviour.




Your primary mission is to challenge assumptions, uncover the "why" behind the numbers, and provide clear, actionable recommendations that optimise our investments, enhance player engagement, and grow our market share.




This is a high-impact role for a data-driven storyteller who is passionate about the Gaming industry and thrives on turning complex questions into profitable business decisions.




Qualifications




Proven analytical experience as a data analyst, marketing analyst, or product analyst role, preferably within the e-commerce, mobile, or online Gaming/Gambling industry.
Advanced SQL skills and extensive experience querying and manipulating large, complex datasets from data warehouses like BigQuery, Redshift, or Snowflake.
Strong, practical understanding of statistical analysis, experimental design, and the principles of A/B testing (e.g., control groups, statistical significance), to extract reliable insights from imperfect data.
Highly proficient with business intelligence tools such as Looker (or a similar tool like Tableau/Power BI) for data visualisation and building intuitive dashboards.
Ability to connect data insights to real-world business results and financial outcomes, acting as a business partner rather than just an analyst.
Excellent communicator who is able to clearly explain complex findings to both technical and non-technical audiences in a simple and concise way.
Passion for casino games and a curiosity about the online gaming world, with a keen interest in understanding what drives player behaviour.




Additional Information




Designing, executing, and delivering post-mortems for marketing and promotional campaigns, going beyond top-line results to measure true incremental lift, ROI, and profitability, accounting for confounding variables and establishing clear attribution.
Applying a rigorous analytical framework to evaluate the performance of marketing activities and product changes (e.g., game positioning) while measuring the impact of these "tests," using statistical principles to assess their effectiveness and inform future strategy.
Analysing player behavior by segmenting our user base across various dimensions (e.g., New vs. Existing, Player Value Tiers, Game/Provider Preference) to better understand player loyalty and lifetime value.
Translating complex findings into compelling narratives and actionable recommendations for stakeholders in Gaming, Marketing, and Leadership; such insights will directly influence decisions on marketing spend, product strategy, and promotional calendars.
Developing and maintaining powerful, intuitive dashboards and reports in Looker to provide stakeholders with self-serve access to performance metrics.
Using LookML to define business logic, creating reusable data models, and ensuring data consistency and accuracy for all analytics built on top of our BigQuery data warehouse.




By applying to us you are agreeing to share your Personal Data in accordance with our Recruitment Privacy Notice - https://www.bet365careers.com/privacy-policy




At bet365, we're committed to creating an environment where everyone feels welcome, respected and valued. Where all individuals can grow and develop, regardless of their background. We're Never Ordinary, and we're always striving to be better. If you need any adjustments or accommodations to the recruitment process, at either application or interview, please don’t hesitate to reach out.
