Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Description

Position at LeoVegas Group

About The Role

We are seeking a Data Analyst to support P&T initiatives by analyzing data, identifying trends, and providing actionable insights to drive decision-making.This role is a unique opportunity to venture into 'uncharted territories,' focusing on how our products work and analyzing the performance of new features to drive our commercial and product strategy forward.

As a Data Analyst, you will be a key player in investigating customer journeys and website search performance, providing data-driven recommendations that directly impact the user experience. Working in a fast-paced environment in a team based in Malta, Sweden, Poland, and Italy, you will have the chance to build projects from scratch, revamp dashboards, and find areas for improvement within our global gaming product.

YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR:


Product Analysis: Analyze website search performance and player insights to identify "loopholes" and areas for product improvement.
Stakeholder Management: Build strong, mature relationships with product owners, management, and commercial teams to communicate data-driven recommendations.
Customer Journey Investigation: Use behavioral and product analytics to deep-dive into how users interact with our platforms.
Dashboard Development: Collaborate with the team to revamp existing dashboards and create new tracking projects centered on product analytics.
Proactive Reporting: Conduct proactive analysis of new feature performance and provide actionable insights to the wider data team.
Data Unification: Help unify previously separate analysis tasks following the merger of our behavioral and commercial functions.


OUR SUCCESSFUL CANDIDATE WILL HAVE THE FOLLOWING:

Essential Skills


Professional Experience: Minimum of 2+ years of experience in a Data Analyst role, preferably within a product-focused environment.
Advanced SQL: Strong proficiency in SQL for querying and managing complex data sets.
Analytics Expertise: Proven experience in behavioral and product analytics, specifically investigating customer journeys.
Statistical Knowledge: A solid foundation in statistics to ensure the accuracy and reliability of your findings.
Communication Skills: The ability to translate complex data into clear recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.
Proactive Attitude: A self-starter mindset with the maturity to take ownership of projects and build stakeholder trust.
Python Proficiency: Experience using Python for data manipulation or advanced analysis.


NICE TO HAVES


iGaming Experience: Previous experience within the iGaming industry is considered a significant asset.


Who We Are

At the core of LeoVegas Group is Team Leo. Our culture is our foundation and is what enables us to innovate, build, and lead as we trailblaze our way through the igaming industry.

We're a team of over 2000 innovators, initiators, and groundbreakers working in a fast-paced and agile environment across 19 offices worldwide.

Benefits


Hybrid work policy
4 weeks of Workation (T&C apply)
Well-being allowance to support your active lifestyle
Private health insurance
Discounts across a range of retailers, gyms, bars & restaurants
We offer an employee assistance program that can provide help and guidance during challenging moments.


JOIN US!

In our pride, we empower our teammates to find their roar and run with their wildest ideas. We don’t wait for things to happen; we pounce and make it happen!

Would you be a good fit for the Leo Pride - give us a roar!

**As our company working language is English, we’d like to see your CV in English, please**

Title: BI Analyst
Description:
About kwiff:




kwiff isn’t gambling as you know it. We’re redefining the experience with a bold, player-first approach to sports betting and casino, powered by our proprietary tech platform, fully automated sportsbook, and standout UX across web and mobile. What truly sets us apart? Supercharging: our signature feature that allows players’ odds, cash outs, and more to be supercharged at random, creating surprise wins and a thrilling betting experience. ⚡




Role overview:




We're looking for a BI Analyst to join our growing team and play a key role in building a more data-driven business. You'll work with stakeholders across the organisation to turn data into actionable insights, helping us better understand our customers, improve performance, and identify opportunities for growth.




This is a hands-on role with real impact, offering the opportunity to shape our Business Intelligence capability while supporting both operational and strategic decision-making.




We're looking for someone who is naturally curious, analytical, and proactive, with the ability to uncover meaningful insights and communicate them effectively to a range of audiences.




Core responsibilities:




Data & Analytics Leadership: Act as the primary point of contact for data and analytics across the organisation, providing expert guidance and insight to stakeholders at all levels.




BI Development: Support the development and implementation of BI tools, reporting frameworks, and data processes that enable effective analysis and decision-making.




Data Quality: Ensure the accuracy, quality, and integrity of data through robust validation, cleansing, and governance practices.




Financial Planning & Analysis: Deliver financial and operational analysis to support budgeting, forecasting, performance monitoring, and strategic planning.




Commercial Insight: Produce analysis that helps drive revenue growth, improve profitability, and identify commercial opportunities.




Business Partnering: Work closely with operational and commercial teams to understand challenges, measure performance, and provide actionable insights.




Reporting & Recommendations: Translate complex data into clear, meaningful reporting and recommendations that support both operational and strategic decision-making.




Continuous Improvement: Proactively identify emerging reporting and analytical requirements arising from changes in customer behaviour, business processes, or new data sources, and develop scalable solutions to address them.




Dashboard Development: Design, build, and maintain dashboards, reports, and self-service analytics tools that provide stakeholders with timely and reliable information.




Trend Analysis: Identify trends, risks, and opportunities within business data, presenting findings and recommendations to relevant stakeholders.




Skills and qualifications:





Strong analytical background with a degree or equivalent experience in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, or a related quantitative discipline.


Advanced SQL skills, with the ability to write efficient, scalable, and reliable queries for reporting, analysis, and data validation.


Experience in Business Intelligence or Analytics, with at least 3 years' experience delivering insights in a BI, Data, or Analytics environment.


Data management expertise, including working with multiple data sources such as relational databases, APIs, and flat files, with a strong understanding of data quality, governance, and lineage.


BI and visualisation tools, with hands-on experience building dashboards, reports, and data models using tools such as Power BI, Metabase, or similar platforms.


Commercial acumen, with the ability to identify trends, opportunities, and risks, and translate analysis into meaningful business recommendations.


Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, capable of presenting complex findings in a clear and actionable way to both technical and non-technical audiences.


Proactive and curious mindset, with a willingness to challenge assumptions, investigate root causes, and independently identify opportunities for improvement.


Experience working with operational and commercial teams, partnering closely with stakeholders to understand business requirements and drive data-led decision-making.


Interest in sports, gaming, or online casino industries would be advantageous.





Perks and Benefits:




Opportunities for career growth – in a vibrant, inclusive and fast-paced industry.

Private Healthcare – Full coverage, including dental.

Performance Bonuses – Quarterly, based on team goals and business impact.

Well-being Allowance – Budget refreshed every six months to spend on wellness or fitness.

Free Lunch – Enjoy lunch on us when working from the office.

Long Service Rewards – Travel perks to celebrate your commitment.

Team Socials & Office Culture – Regular events, office dogs, offsites, plus ping pong, darts




Why join us?




Kwiff is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.




Join us, and you’ll be part of a team that’s shaping the future of iGaming with cutting-edge technology and a culture of bold innovation!




We aim for equity at all stages of the recruitment process. Please let us know if we can do anything to make the process more accessible to you.

Trend Analysis: Identify trends, risks, and opportunities within business data, presenting findings and recommendations to relevant stakeholders.


Skills and qualifications:



  • Strong analytical background with a degree or equivalent experience in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, or a related quantitative discipline.

  • Advanced SQL skills, with the ability to write efficient, scalable, and reliable queries for reporting, analysis, and data validation.

  • Experience in Business Intelligence or Analytics, with at least 3 years' experience delivering insights in a BI, Data, or Analytics environment.

  • Data management expertise, including working with multiple data sources such as relational databases, APIs, and flat files, with a strong understanding of data quality, governance, and lineage.

  • BI and visualisation tools, with hands-on experience building dashboards, reports, and data models using tools such as Power BI, Metabase, or similar platforms.

  • Commercial acumen, with the ability to identify trends, opportunities, and risks, and translate analysis into meaningful business recommendations.

  • Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, capable of presenting complex findings in a clear and actionable way to both technical and non-technical audiences.

  • Proactive and curious mindset, with a willingness to challenge assumptions, investigate root causes, and independently identify opportunities for improvement.

  • Experience working with operational and commercial teams, partnering closely with stakeholders to understand business requirements and drive data-led decision-making.

  • Interest in sports, gaming, or online casino industries would be advantageous.


Perks and Benefits:


Opportunities for career growth – in a vibrant, inclusive and fast-paced industry.

Private Healthcare – Full coverage, including dental.

Performance Bonuses – Quarterly, based on team goals and business impact.

Well-being Allowance – Budget refreshed every six months to spend on wellness or fitness.

Free Lunch – Enjoy lunch on us when working from the office.

Long Service Rewards – Travel perks to celebrate your commitment.

Team Socials & Office Culture – Regular events, office dogs, offsites, plus ping pong, darts


Why join us?


Kwiff is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.


Join us, and you’ll be part of a team that’s shaping the future of iGaming with cutting-edge technology and a culture of bold innovation!


We aim for equity at all stages of the recruitment process. Please let us know if we can do anything to make the process more accessible to you.

Title: Player Insights Analyst
Description:
Rhino Entertainment is looking for a Player Insights Analyst to join our CRM Team You will be responsible for analyzing customer data and campaign outcomes to enhance engagement at each stage of the customer journey. Reporting to the Head of CRM, you will bridge the gap between commercial insights and operational CRM by designing and implementing strategies that foster business growth and operational efficiency. You will focus heavily on data analysis to make informed decisions regarding optimal offerings, campaign budgets, and player value. By collaborating closely with internal stakeholders, the Casino team, and the Business Intelligence (BI) team, you will ensure our campaigns and game performances are fully optimized to meet regional and global audience needs.

What your role will look like:


Analyze customer data to identify trends, behaviors, and opportunities for enhancing player engagement and retention
Predict, budget, monitor, and recap the impact of CRM activities to optimize campaign spend and effectiveness across various markets
Build models and perform statistical analysis to make predictions and drive informed data-based decision-making
Assess and drive insights to optimize player value, game play activity, and promotional offerings
Collaborate with the BI and Casino teams to analyze game and provider performance across different brands and regions
Identify engagement gaps and generate improvement strategies for CRM campaigns
Utilize tools like SQL and Tableau to develop, automate, maintain, and optimize reports and dashboards
Conduct database exploration in search of hidden potential


Requirements


Experience in this or a similar role within the iGaming industry
2 to 3 years of work experience in commercial or business analysis, including building visualizations and working with complex queries or large data sets
Strong understanding of operational CRM, customer segmentation, lifecycle marketing, and retention strategies
Knowledge of SQL for reporting and experience with visualization tools such as Tableau
Highly analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data and create actionable strategies
Degree in Marketing, Data Analytics, Business Administration, Economics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal
Strong problem-solving skills, a can-do attitude, and the ability to work efficiently under pressure
Knowledge of gamification principles and skill in applying them to boost user engagement is a plus


Benefits

Working for Rhino includes plenty of perks.

What we offer:


Competitive salary based on your knowledge & experience
Quarterly performance bonus split on personal objectives and company performance
€500 Yearly Wellness & Mindfulness allowance to support an active lifestyle
Flexibility & a work-life blend through our Hybrid Working environment
Work from anywhere with our 30 days "overseas working"
Diverse, dynamic, fun environment with happy and helpful colleagues
Learning & Development opportunities to invest in your personal development
Fresh fruit, healthy snacks & food are available at the office everyday - and of course, good tea & coffee
Free parking, table tennis, lounge area, plenty of meeting rooms and sea views at our ergonomic office
Regular social events to strengthen long lasting relationships
Enjoy a "Me Day" on your birthday
Paid time off, including extra days for special life events
AXA Private Hospital & Dental Insurance Coverage
Form an active part of our talent acquisition, with our Referral Bonus
And to top it off, a great culture!


Join us at Rhino Entertainment and unleash your full potential in a vibrant and rewarding career! 🎉

Our Story

Rhino Entertainment Group is a fast-growing online gaming operator that was founded in 2020. Winner of the EGR Rising Operator of the Year Award 2023 and iGaming Idol Employer of The Year Award 2023, the company has a multi-brand strategy and currently operates five brands: Casino Days, Buusti Kasino, Raketti, Big Boost and Lucky Spins. Rhino group is committed to providing its players with the best possible gaming experience supported by a cutting-edge gaming platform.

Our Culture

"We are a community of passionate, committed, driven and fun-loving people who are united by a shared mission to deliver world-class entertainment to our players in a vibrant and dynamic environment. Our culture identity is rooted in our curiosity, our sense of community and unwavering drive for success. We cherish each person's unique qualities and contributions, and we encourage our team members to take ownership, think creatively, find solutions and enjoy the process of achieving our goals!"

DEIB

We are proud to have so many diverse backgrounds, cultures, perspectives, and ideas represented within our Rhino community, which fuels an authentic workplace and a strong sense of belonging. This broad spectrum helps us achieve a supportive working environment where everyone can make the best use of their skills, free from discrimination or harassment. Diversity goes hand in hand with promoting equal opportunities for all current and future employees and at Rhino, we are committed to ensure fairness in our recruitment process.

How We Work

At Rhino we very much believe in supporting flexible working arrangements! By blending working from our vibrant offices and using the latest tech to work from home, through our Hybrid Working environment, we firmly believe that we can create an effective, balanced, and collaborative working experience to help us achieve our goals!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Are you ready to join our digital revolution journey?

At Aleph , we’re not just part of the digital advertising landscape—we’re shaping its future. Representing the world’s leading platforms, including TikTok, Amazon, Google, and nearly 55 others, we operate in 130+ markets across new and existing geographies.

Our mission is to empower advertisers and brands to unlock the full potential of these platforms' advertising capabilities. By fostering long-lasting partnerships, we create limitless opportunities for people and businesses to advertise effectively at both local and global levels.

With a presence spanning continents, Aleph offers you the chance to be part of a fast-growing, innovative team where your work makes a direct impact. If you’re ambitious, forward-thinking, and eager to thrive in a dynamic, global environment, Aleph is the perfect place to build your career.

Role Overview

We are looking for a highly analytical Data Analyst to join our Business Intelligence team. You will act as a critical "backend engine," building the data foundations and analytical models that enable data-driven decisions for both our supply (platforms) and demand (sales) sides.

You will dive deep into complex datasets, build automated reporting pipelines, and uncover actionable insights that drive our strategy with global platforms like TikTok, Microsoft, Google, X, Spotify, and others.

What You 'll Do



Extract and analyze complex data.
Query, clean, and transform large datasets from multiple sources to uncover trends, performance metrics, and market insights that support strategic decision-making across the organization.
Build and maintain BI dashboards.
Design, develop, and maintain automated dashboards and reports using Looker Studio to provide real-time visibility into business performance for executive and partner discussions. Support strategic projects.
Conduct deep-dive analyses for platform RFP responses, Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs), and strategic initiatives that shape Aleph’s partnerships and revenue strategy. Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders.
Work closely with strategists, sales teams, and regional leadership to define data requirements, understand their business challenges, and deliver quantitative solutions.
Translate data into actionable recommendations.
Identify performance anomalies, revenue opportunities, and market risks by packaging raw data into structured, easy-to-consume analytical models and insights.
Ensure data integrity and governance.
Establish clear data validation frameworks, optimize queries, and maintain high standards for data accuracy and documentation internally.



Who You Are



Highly analytical and problem-solving oriented.
You have a strong quantitative background and enjoy tackling complex business questions through rigorous data analysis.
Technically proficient in data tools.
You possess strong SQL skills for data extraction and manipulation, and you are highly proficient in at least one major BI visualization tool (e.g., Power BI, Looker Studio). Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills are a must; experience with Python a strong plus. Business-minded.You don’t just pull numbers; you understand the business context behind the data and can connect your analysis directly to revenue growth, efficiency, and key opportunities.
A clear communicator. You can translate complex technical findings and data models into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders and leadership
Structured and detail-oriented. You are meticulous about data accuracy, organize your code and queries logically, and ensure all deliverables meet a high-quality standard before they reach stakeholders.
Interested in digital media or advertising.
Experience in digital advertising analytics, media agencies, ad-tech, or similar environments is a strong plus, though not strictly required.



What You'll Love About Us



We care about your individuality by giving you freedom to grow and create within the company, regardless of your position.
Work in a dynamic team in a fast-paced industry of an international company with multi-cultural teams around the world .
Learn from the best: Our state of the art workshops guarantee the latest insights into digital advertising.
In line with the EU Pay Transparency Directive, we are committed to fair and open compensation practices. For this role, the minimum gross annual base salary is 35,000 EUR.



In addition to base salary, this role includes a variable component, paid annually. The final offer will reflect your experience, skills, and the scope of the role.

#ALEPH

Why Join Us?

Aleph is a place of many cultures, perspectives, and talents. We support each other, creating an environment of giving and receiving. We value partnership and communication because we believe it takes a group of people to achieve great things. We are energized by our ever-changing industry, our curiosity keeps us learning and seeking out new opportunities.

We stay flexible and adaptable, and believe in moving with the speed of change. We encourage everyone to strive for more, providing our internal talent with growth opportunities and the ability to learn together.

#ALEPH

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
About Cityswift

CitySwift is the world’s leading performance optimisation platform for public transport, enabling data-driven decision-making for some of the largest transport networks globally. Backed by over €15 million in venture capital funding, we partner with major public transport operators and government authorities, including Go-Ahead Group, Transport for London, and many more. CitySwift is experiencing accelerated growth, with 117% year-over-year revenue growth in 2025.

Our mission is to increase the adoption of sustainable public transport worldwide. Today, our platform optimises more than 3 billion passenger journeys annually, and our target is to reach 10 billion journeys by the end of 2026.

At CitySwift, you’ll have the opportunity to take ownership, make a measurable impact, and grow professionally as part of a collaborative, mission-driven team. Join us in shaping the future of public transport by driving the next phase of CitySwift’s growth.

About The Role

As an Analytics Engineer, your mission is to ensure that every data user at CitySwift is working from a single, high-quality source of truth. You will apply software engineering best practices—like version control, testing, and continuous integration—to the world of data warehousing.

In the Data Analytics team, we sit in the information layer between our tech stack and our customers. You will play a critical role in scaling our Modern Data Information Layer. You are building scalable data models that enable our platform to deliver real-time insights to the world’s largest transport operators. If you thrive on seeing the impact of data on business decisions, if you're curious about solving business problems at scale, if you want to be at the centre of AI adoption and if you love working in a creative data-led environment, then this role is for you.

Key Responsibilities


Data modelling & architecture: Build & create data models that help solve business problems. Follow team conventions and best practices for data architecture design. Understand Git workflows and raise PRs for review.
Semantic Layer & BI development: Define, build and edit semantic layer views that power customer-facing analytics such as User Apps, Dashboards, and AI tools. Create and build scalable and reusable dashboards.
Engineering collaboration & deployment: Understand the essentials of how analytics are surfaced within products. Participate in cross-team meetings and follow agreed deployment processes.
Commercial & stakeholder partnership: Understand the core commercial metrics and KPIs of the business. Respond to data requests from commercial teams. Build awareness of how data outputs are used in decision-making.
Team enablement & technical leadership: Seek support from senior colleagues, be curious and ask challenging questions. Share learnings with data peers. Follows established team processes and documentation standards.



Desired Qualifications


Experience: 3+ years in a data-centric role (Analytics, Data Engineering, or BI)
Business Acumen: Experience with solving real-world problems with data and translating data insights into outcomes.
Tooling Mastery: Advanced-level SQL (window functions, CTEs, optimisation). Deep familiarity with BigQuery or a similar cloud data warehouse (Snowflake, Redshift, etc.).
Engineering Mindset: Proficient with Git and the command line. You understand why modularity, DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principles, and documentation are non-negotiable.
Problem Solving: Experience handling large-scale datasets. You know how to handle time-series anomalies, null values, and complex joins without breaking the pipeline.
Communication: Ability to explain complex data to stakeholders and justify architectural decisions based on both technical performance and business needs.
Education: Degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Mathematics, or a related field—or equivalent experience scaling a modern data stack.



We value, recognise and reward our people


A role with real scope, where your impact is visible, your salary is reviewed as we grow, and there are career progression opportunities as the company scales and internationalises.
Comprehensive health and life insurance through Laya Healthcare in Ireland and Aviva in the UK.
Flexible working with hybrid working as the default in Ireland, core hours of 10am to 4pm, remote options depending on role and a work abroad programme of up to 6 weeks.
25 days annual leave from day one, increasing by half a day per year of service, plus company days off, a half day before public holidays and an end of year shutdown.
Enhanced family leave, including 6 months fully paid maternity leave and 3 weeks paid paternity leave after 18 months’ service (and wedding leave!)
And on top of all of this, the usuals done well. A matching pension scheme that increases with service, paid sick leave, bike to work scheme, wellbeing supports, and an employee referral programme with rewards of up to €4,000 (or equivalent) per referral.

  • Engineering Mindset: Proficient with Git and the command line. You understand why modularity, DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principles, and documentation are non-negotiable.
  • Problem Solving: Experience handling large-scale datasets. You know how to handle time-series anomalies, null values, and complex joins without breaking the pipeline.
  • Communication: Ability to explain complex data to stakeholders and justify architectural decisions based on both technical performance and business needs.
  • Education: Degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Mathematics, or a related field—or equivalent experience scaling a modern data stack.


We value, recognise and reward our people

  • A role with real scope, where your impact is visible, your salary is reviewed as we grow, and there are career progression opportunities as the company scales and internationalises.
  • Comprehensive health and life insurance through Laya Healthcare in Ireland and Aviva in the UK.
  • Flexible working with hybrid working as the default in Ireland, core hours of 10am to 4pm, remote options depending on role and a work abroad programme of up to 6 weeks.
  • 25 days annual leave from day one, increasing by half a day per year of service, plus company days off, a half day before public holidays and an end of year shutdown.
  • Enhanced family leave, including 6 months fully paid maternity leave and 3 weeks paid paternity leave after 18 months’ service (and wedding leave!)
  • And on top of all of this, the usuals done well. A matching pension scheme that increases with service, paid sick leave, bike to work scheme, wellbeing supports, and an employee referral programme with rewards of up to €4,000 (or equivalent) per referral.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
The Customization Group

Business Data Analyst

Build the analytics layer for a network shipping 2.5M custom products a day.


Riga, Latvia (Hybrid)
Full-time
€2,500-3000 gross/month


We're looking for a business analyst who wants to build, not just report. Someone who gets excited about defining the metrics that matter, has the instinct to push back when the data disagrees with the room, and uses AI as a natural part of how they work.

About TCG

The Customization Group is a global leader in mass customization and print-on-demand e-commerce. We manufacture up to 2.5 million unique, personalized products daily across production sites in Germany, Poland, the USA, and Latvia. Millions of customers find us through our 20+ online shops and 1,000+ partner stores. Our 750+ team members come from 40+ nationalities and work at the intersection of manufacturing, e-commerce, and AI.

At our scale, operational data is a goldmine of insight - but only if someone knows how to read it. That's the role we're hiring for.

The Role

You'll own the analytics layer for our sales, finance, and profitability data. You define the KPIs, build the Power BI reporting leaders actually use, and turn raw sales and cost data into the recommendations that account managers, shop managers, and top-level leadership execute on. You'll partner with sales, marketing, e-commerce, and production teams to translate business questions into analyses, surface the insights that matter, and drive process improvements with the data to back them up.

In a nutshell: Own the analytics and reporting layer. Extract, transform, visualize, interpret. Drive the process improvements the data points to.

What You'll Be Working On


→Manage daily operations and development of TCG analytics and reporting tools.
→Taking the sales forecasting process from manual to modelled - statistical methods, automation, the works.
→Redesigning sales and profit reporting so group managers can see which products, channels, and shops are actually profitable.
→Building Power BI dashboards leaders actually use, with confident DAX and a clean data model behind them.
→Defining the KPIs that matter across sales, marketing, e-commerce, and production -and getting them adopted.
→Migrating the reporting layer onto our new microservices architecture - clean, fast, scalable.
→Using AI tools to accelerate exploration and automate the repeatable parts of analysis


Who We're Looking For

Sounds like you?


→3-5 years in business analysis inside a data-rich operational environment - e-commerce, manufacturing, or similar.
→Power BI to a depth where you've built dashboards people actually depend on, with confident DAX and data modelling.
→Track record of defining KPIs and turning analysis into adopted process changes - not just reports that get sent.
→You've built your own analytics workflows or automations using AI - it's part of how you work, not something you're planning to explore.
→Working SQL and exposure to statistical methods for forecasting.
→Hands-on experience with financial or profitability data in a previous role - costing, margin analysis, P&L reporting, or similar.
→Fluency in English.


Probably not a fit if...


→You prefer fully-defined requirements over figuring out what the business should be measuring in the first place.
→You see analytics as building reports on request, rather than partnering on decisions.
→You see AI as a future investment rather than a current tool.


What We Offer


→ €2,500-3000 gross/month, depending on level. Additional performance-based annual bonus on top of base salary.
→International experience in our globally spread Teams.
→The flexibility that suits your lifestyle: In-house, hybrid way of working.
→Private Health insurance with a compensation package after the probation period.
→Additional leave on special occasions.
→Variety of packed breakfast, lunch, and snacks in the office.
→Valuable knowledge and excellent experience for future development.
→Connection with your colleagues through Team building and company events.


Interested?

Send us your CV. No cover letter needed - if you want to add a few lines on a dashboard or KPI you're proud of, we'd read it.

The Customization Group


Riga, Latvia
thecustomizationgroup.com We welcome applications from all backgrounds. What matters is what you can build.

Title: Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst
Description:
Over 50,000 customers globally trust our end-to-end, cloud-driven networking solutions and rely on our top-rated services and support to accelerate their digital transformation efforts and deliver progress like never before and with double digit growth year over year, no provider is better positioned to deliver better outcomes on scale, than Extreme.

Come become part of something big with us! We are a global leader, with hubs in Europe, North America, South America, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East.

Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst

About The Role

We are seeking a “Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst” to join our Extreme Networks Global Operations organization, where you'll turn complex operational data into clear, actionable insight that drives decisions and automation across our Global Supply Chan and Order Management teams. This role sits at the intersection of analytics, AI, and supply chain strategy, partnering with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, build analytical products, and tell the stories behind the numbers. The ideal candidate is a curious, technically strong analyst who is equally comfortable writing SQL, using AI tools, framing a business problem, managing a project, and presenting findings to stakeholders. You should be excited about the rapid evolution of AI and agentic workflows and how they can be applied to supply chain decision-making and you should be actively using AI tools in your work today.

A Note On Experience

This role requires commercially delivered analytical work. We will ask you at interview to walk through a specific project from problem through to business outcome that you owned and delivered in a professional setting. Academic or course-based projects do not qualify. If your experience is primarily from an academic programme, this role is not the right fit at this time

Key Responsibilities


Partner with supply chain stakeholders to scope problems and translate them into structured, measurable, automated workstreams.
Build and maintain python/SQL/Snowflake-based data models, dashboards, and self-service analytics for our global operations teams
Help operationalize AI and agent-based solutions to automate recurring analyses, augment decision support, and supply chain processes.
Apply statistical methods to inform data driven decisions across our global operations function.
Manage your projects end-to-end: scoping, stakeholder alignment, data discovery, modelling, validation, and rollout.
Translate analytical findings into compelling narratives (written, visual, and verbal) tailored to operational, business, and technical audiences.
Define and track KPIs that measure supply chain performance and flag interventions when metrics drift.
Contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality, documentation, and analytical standards across the team.


Required Skills & Qualifications


3-5 years experience in business analytics, data analytics, or a comparable quantitative role.
Demonstrated use of AI tools such as Claude, MS Copilot, or ChatGPT – in an analytical workflow, with a genuine appetite to develop agentic and automation capabilities in role.
Strong python skills and hands-on experience with Snowflake (or a comparable cloud data warehouse such as BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks).
Demonstrated project management ability, owning deliverables across multiple stakeholders and timelines.
Solid grounding in descriptive statistics, and analytics fundamentals.
Strong data storytelling skills: the ability to distil complexity into clear visuals and narratives that drive action.
Supply chain domain knowledge: familiarity with demand and supply planning, inventory optimization, order management, logistics, procurement, or manufacturing operations.
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).


Technical Skills Required


Programming for analytics: Python or R for modelling, automation, and analytics workflows.
BI and visualization tools: Tableau, Sigma, Power BI, Looker, or similar — both for building and for design literacy.
ERP / source systems: Exposure to Oracle Fusion Cloud or similar transactional systems.


Preferred / Additional Skills: These are skills that would meaningfully strengthen a candidate for this role:


Practical experience applying agentic workflows (e.g., LLM-based assistants, retrieval-augmented analytics, automated reasoning agents) to analytical or operational problems.
Master's or PhD in a quantitative field (Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).
Forecasting and optimization: time-series methods as well as other optimization techniques.
Machine learning fundamentals: classification, regression, clustering, and an understanding of when ML is and isn't the right tool.
Process improvement methodologies: Lean, Six Sigma, or equivalent structured problem-solving frameworks.
Stakeholder management and influencing: navigating ambiguity, managing competing priorities, and building trust with non-technical partners.


What Success Looks Like


Within 3 months: deeply understand our supply chain data landscape, key stakeholders, and top-priority business questions.
Within 6 months: deliver at least one high-impact analytical product or AI-enabled workflow that changes how a decision is made.
Within 12 months: become a trusted analytics partner to supply chain stakeholders and a contributor to the broader analytics team's standards and practices.


Salary Is Based On Qualifications And Experience Between 60-70K

Extreme Networks Benefits

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Banking is not just about finance - it is a dynamic industry offering career paths for individuals of diverse backgrounds and experiences. Whether your interests lie in finance, technology, compliance, risk management, marketing, legal, ESG, HR or customer support, there are opportunities for you.




At APS Bank, we are committed to offering a banking experience that is personal and simple for our customers, while providing our employees with the support and resources they need to succeed.




Our mission is inspired by a commitment to social, economic, and environmental progress, providing all our stakeholders with an opportunity to grow. This is what APS is all about. This is what we’ve always been about.




🔝 We aspire to elevate every effort towards achieving excellence.

🤗 We value authenticity, forming genuine connections and creating meaningful impact.

💚 We are passionate in everything we do. We own it, love it, lead it!

🙌 We unite in diversity to foster inclusion.

✅ We are committed to being contemporary. We strive and thrive.




Job summary

Customer Intelligence is a unit within the Strategy Department that focuses on leveraging customer data. A data analyst is needed to support in day-to-day data requests and the compilation of monthly and ad-hoc reports. The CI Analyst will also be responsible for enhancing the customer segmentation, and the coordination of the Banks market research activities. They will also be supporting the product development team and contribute to the overall bank strategy by promoting data driven decision making.




The analyst will need to bridge the gap between operations and IT and be able to understand both sides of the business. Therefore, the ideal candidate would have both technical IT knowledge and a good understanding of the banking industry.




Main Responsibilities

Take ownership of monthly and ad-hoc reporting related to products and customers.
Prioritise customer data requests from Marketing and other departments.
Collaborate in projects with other departments that need data guidance.
Identify and develop reports related to customer trends.
Effectively communicate findings through dashboards and KPIs.




Requirements

Have excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Be meticulous, takes initiative and possesses excellent analytical skills.
Be reliable, organised, and able to work under pressure.
Be team player while still being able to work independently when needed.
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Excel and data manipulation.
Familiar with SQL queries and database concepts.
At least 2 years’ experience in an analyst position.
Certification in IT/Statistics/Finance.




Benefits at APS Bank

Join our team and enjoy a competitive remuneration package and a range of employee benefits, including:

🎯 Performance bonus

📈 Preferential interest rates on home and personal loans

💶 Private pension plan

🏥 Health insurance

🧠 Ongoing professional training

👩 🏫 Opportunities for career growth

🏃 Sports and fitness activities' subsidy

🏋️ Free gym facilities at APS Centre

😀 A dynamic and friendly work environment




Closing date

30 June 2026




Diversity, equity, and inclusion are central to our mission of fostering an inclusive work environment where differences are celebrated, enabling individuals to achieve and contribute to their highest potential. At APS Bank, our commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion is deeply embedded in our culture, guiding our interactions with customers, employees, students, suppliers, and communities.

Title: Data Analyst - Internal Audit
Description:
Company Description

Wise is a global technology company, building the best way to move and manage the world’s money.

Min fees. Max ease. Full speed.

Whether people and businesses are sending money to another country, spending abroad, or making and receiving international payments, Wise is on a mission to make their lives easier and save them money.

As part of our team, you will be helping us create an entirely new network for the world's money.

For everyone, everywhere.

Job Description

More about our mission and what we offer.

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join the Internal Audit branch of the Compliance & Internal Audit Analytics team at Wise.

You’ll partner with Internal Audit (IA) to develop and implement the analytics strategy across the function.

Internal Audit serves as an independent 3rd line of defence that oversees and provides an independent assessment and reasonable assurance on the effectiveness of the company’s corporate governance structure + internal controls.

The IA Analytics team supports IA by using analytics to provide more coverage, efficiency and accuracy to audit testing, as well as building innovative tools and using automation to support continuous testing and other IA activities beyond the annual audit plan.

This is an IC2 role. For more information on our Analytics Career Map and levelling structure, click here.

What You’ll Be Doing


Partnering with stakeholders across the IA function covering all regions, products and domains of the business (including Financial Crime, Ops, Risk, Finance and Technology)
Identifying & implementing opportunities to do the most impactful analytics within audits and influencing and upskilling your partners in IA to do the same
Working with analytics, engineering, and business specialists to seek out and understand interesting and varied datasets, perform analytics using modern approaches, and develop a strong understanding of operations at Wise
Championing innovation and data-led insights, not just within audits but across all IA activities where analytics can add value
Working on automation and tooling projects to increase efficiency across IA including workflow automation, continuous auditing, leveraging AI, and building MI dashboards


Qualifications

What you’ll bring:


3+ years of experience in data analytics
1+ years of data analytics experience within an audit, risk, or compliance related role
Advanced stakeholder management skills; you know how to leverage multiple different teams to deliver high-impact change including analytics, engineering, and business specialists
Extensive experience using SQL; you can build and maintain complex data sources and can leverage visualisation tools to surface insights


Nice To Have


Experience of working in regulated environments, Fintech or Financial Services.
Knowledge of financial crime.
Experience using dbt, Superset, or Looker.
Knowledge of advanced analytics techniques, including the use of Python and visualisation techniques.


Additional Information

For everyone, everywhere. We're people building money without borders — without judgement or prejudice, too. We believe teams are strongest when they are diverse, equitable and inclusive.

We're proud to have a truly international team, and we celebrate our differences.

Inclusive teams help us live our values and make sure every Wiser feels respected, empowered to contribute towards our mission and able to progress in their careers.

If you want to find out more about what it's like to work at Wise visit Wise.Jobs.

Keep up to date with life at Wise by following us on LinkedIn and Instagram.
Show more

Title: Data Analyst – Marketing & Campaign Analytics
Description:
If you need support in completing the application or if you require a different format of this document, please get in touch with at UKI.recruitment@tcs.com with the subject line: “Application Support Request”.




Careers at TCS: It means more

TCS is a purpose-led transformation company, built on belief. We do not just help businesses to transform through technology. We support them in making a meaningful difference to the people and communities they serve - our clients include some of the biggest brands in the UK and worldwide. For you, it means more to make an impact that matters, through challenging projects which demand ambitious innovation and thought leadership.

Work closely with the range of teams within the business to bring ideas to life.
Develop and maintain a unified strategy that aligns operational efficiency with marketing and growth objectives.
Work with customers and create high-impact customer experiences for our business.




The Role

We are seeking a Data Analyst to support Marketing and Distribution teams within the Financial Services sector, with a particular focus on Asset Management, Life Insurance, and Retirement. This role is hands‑on and delivery‑focused, supporting marketing campaigns through data extraction, audience segmentation, campaign list pulls, reporting, and performance analysis in a regulated environment.







Your responsibilities:

Support end to end marketing campaign delivery by providing accurate and timely data outputs, including:

-- Campaign list pulls and audience extracts

-- Segment creation and validation

-- Post campaign response and performance analysis

-- Ensuring all activity complies with data governance, and internal usage controls.




Use SQL and Python to extract, transform, and prepare data from enterprise data platforms to support campaign execution, targeting activities, and ongoing marketing analytics.
Work closely with marketing, CRM, and distribution teams to translate campaign briefs into clear data requirements, validating logic and outputs prior to campaign deployment.
Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports to support:

-- Campaign and channel performance tracking

-- Audience and segment analysis

-- Trend and insight reporting for marketing stakeholders

-- Reports should be clear, accurate, and suitable for non technical business users




Access and analyze data hosted on AWS and Azure platforms, working within established cloud, security, and compliance frameworks typical of Financial Services organizations.
Support the application of segmentation and propensity outputs produced by advanced analytics or data science teams, helping operationalize insights into campaign execution.
Assist with data inputs and reporting related to marketing technology and signal based platforms (e.g. 6Sense, Bombora, or similar), without responsibility for model development.
Perform basic data quality checks and validation, identifying inconsistencies or anomalies and working with data engineering or platform teams to resolve issues.
Maintain awareness of regulatory and organizational constraints associated with customer and advisor data, including appropriate handling of sensitive financial information




Your Profile

Essential skills/knowledge/experience:

This role is suited to candidates with approximately 3–6 years of experience in Data Analytics, Marketing Analytics, or a related discipline, ideally gained within Financial Services or another regulated industry.
The position will appeal to individuals who enjoy hands‑on campaign support, working closely with marketing teams, and turning business requirements into reliable, well‑governed data outputs, while continuing to build technical skills and industry knowledge.




Rewards & Benefits

TCS is consistently voted a Top Employer in the UK and globally. Our competitive salary packages feature pension, health care, life assurance, laptop and access to extensive training resources and discounts within the larger Tata network.

We offer health & wellness initiatives and sports events; we are the proud sponsor of the London Marathon and partner with our local communities in Ireland.




Diversity, Inclusion and Wellbeing

Tata Consultancy Services UK&I is committed to meeting the accessibility needs of all individuals in accordance with the Ireland Employment Equality Acts 1998-2011 (as amended) and the Equal Status Acts 2000-2012 (as amended).




We welcome and embrace diversity in race, nationality, ethnicity, disability, neurodiversity, gender identity, age, physical ability, gender reassignment, sexual orientation. We are a disability inclusive employer and encourage disabled people to apply for this role.

As a Disability Confident Employer, we offer an interview to applicants with disabilities or long-term conditions who meet the minimum criteria for the role. Please email us at UKI.recruitment@tcs.com if you would like to opt in.




If you are an applicant who needs any adjustments to the application process or interview, please contact us at UKI.recruitment@tcs.com with the subject line: “Adjustment Request” or email rebecca.mclelland@tcs.com to request an adjustment. We welcome requests prior to you completing the application and at any stage of the recruitment process.




Beware of Fraudulent offers

This is to notify you that TCS does not ask for any sort of payment or security deposit from candidates at any stage of the recruitment process. The firm never sends out job offers from free internet email services like Gmail, Yahoo Mail, and so on. TCS has not authorised any third-party company to collect money on their behalf. As a vigilant job seeker, beware of fraudulent recruitment activity and protect your interests! You can write to UKI.recruitment@tcs.com to report any fraudulent activity.




Due to the high volume of applications, we will be unable to contact each applicant individually on the status of their application. If you have not received a direct response within 30 days, then it should be deemed unsuccessful on this occasion.




Join us and do more of what matters. Apply online now.

Title: Digital Analyst Technical
Description:
We are B2Spin, and we are dedicated to delivering the best Free-to-Play Social Casino and gaming experiences by creating captivating, engaging, and unique products that are accessible to everyone. We have built a team of talented professionals from all over the globe, who are working to continually improve the experiences of our players. You'll be the tracking mastermind ensuring our data collection is bulletproof across web and mobile platforms.

What You Will Be Doing


Partner with developers, product, marketing and data teams on tracking strategy, implementation and quality assurance
Configure, optimize and audit analytics tools while setting up monitoring alerts to maintain data accuracy

Sharp analytical mindset with exceptional attention to detail
Experience with tracking implementation and QA processes
Understanding of privacy regulations in digital analytics

Working with us means diving deep into the exciting world of gaming analytics where your technical expertise directly impacts millions of players' experiences. You'll collaborate with brilliant minds across the globe, have access to cutting-edge tools, and play a crucial role in revolutionizing the social casino industry. Plus, you'll be part of a team that values innovation, precision, and the thrill of turning complex data into game-changing insights.

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Title: Technical Data Analyst - Data Warehouse Services
Description:
Established in 2004, we are a tech pioneer offering world-class adult entertainment and games on some of the internet’s safest and most popular platforms. With the support of an international team of dynamic and collaborative innovators, we are on a mission to enable safe user experiences and empower our communities by celebrating diversity, inclusion, and expression — all while maintaining robust trust-and-safety protocols.

We embrace the best of both worlds! Local talent can thrive in our collaborative office space with the flexibility of a hybrid work environment, while remote team members play an integral role in shaping our dynamic culture from afar. We have offices in Montreal (Quebec), Austin (Texas) and Nicosia (Cyprus).


A select number of positions require full-time in office attendance*


The Data Warehouse Services team plays a vital role in our organization. They are responsible for storing vast quantities of data from many sources and responsible for disseminating data across the organization to various different groups. They are one of the backbones of data at our organization and they are seeking a Senior Data Analyst to join their team.

What you'll be doing:


Collaborate with internal stakeholders including; Directors, Analysts, and Developers to understand business objectives and requirements, and help define the strategy, content, and features for our reporting environments.
Engage with business partners at all levels to discuss and clarify data analysis, needs and requirements.
Analyze data structures and content from data marts to support business intelligence initiatives.
Thrive in a fast-paced environment, where multitasking and adaptability are essential.
Participate in brainstorming sessions to define functional requirements, organize content, map process flows, and specify report features.
Develop and optimize queries to investigate data issues or new reporting structures for various business units.
Work closely with Database Administrators and Developers to estimate time and effort for report projects and refining these estimates throughout the project.
Collaborate with Project Managers to track project status, identify potential risks,and resolve issues as they arise.
Become a subject matter expert in relevant business domains and reporting tools.
Assist in coordinating and maintaining development roadmaps to ensure alignment with business goals.


What you'll need to be successful:

Must Haves:


5+ years of experience in a similar role.
Knowledge of tools to create, extract, and analyze from SQL Server / Excel / PowerBI / Fabric / Cubes.
Strong customer support experience.
Understanding Data flow Concepts.
Understanding Business Model Concepts.


As an equal opportunity employer, we celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees

In this role you may be exposed to adult content

Title: Reporting Engineer
Description:
We are looking for a Reporting Engineer to join our Data Warehouse team. In this role, you will develop and maintain regulatory and in-house reporting solutions that ensure compliance, data accuracy, and reliable insights for both internal stakeholders and B2B clients.

You will work closely with software developers and the DWH team to maintain and improve our reporting ecosystem, troubleshoot existing queries, and ensure reporting evolves alongside product and data changes. This role plays an important part in maintaining data integrity, supporting regulatory requirements, and continuously improving reporting performance in a fast-paced environment.




Core responsibilities

Develop high-performing SQL queries and reporting solutions on large datasets.
Build and maintain regulatory, in-house, and B2B client reporting.
Troubleshoot and optimize existing reports, improving performance and data integrity.
Ensure data consistency by analyzing dependencies between reports.
Maintain and improve documentation for reporting logic, KPIs, and data transformations.
Identify and help reduce technical debt in reporting solutions.
Support the DWH team in maintaining and improving the reporting ecosystem.
Participate in agile ceremonies such as sprint planning and retrospectives.
Collaborate with both technical teams and business stakeholders as needed.
Identify and contribute to process and reporting improvements.




Experience and key competencies

Strong experience writing efficient SQL queries on large datasets.
Good understanding of relational databases (PostgreSQL) and database architecture.
Experience in reporting, data analysis, or data engineering roles.
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently.
Ability to adapt to changing priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent spoken and written English.
Python or JavaScript/TypeScript knowledge and experience with tools like Prefect or Telerik is a plus.

Coolbet operates in a highly regulated environment. This role may require a gaming license in any jurisdiction in which the company operates, and this position may require the ability to secure and maintain a license in all relevant jurisdictions. A gaming license application utilizes personal data, a criminal and credit check, and fingerprints. Coolbet will cover all costs and provide access to an Identity Protection program.




Our Benefits, Your Happiness

Enjoy 28 days of vacation with the option to use your balance on business days only, effectively giving you up to 10 extra days off. Combined with flexible working hours, you can make the most of your time off.
Elevate your well-being with monthly sports/health compensation, full sick leave coverage for the first 8 days, our in-house gym, and a chill games room to unwind.
Unlock a generous Supplemental Training Program that includes training and company-wide knowledge-sharing opportunities to fuel your growth.
Our office is a cozy (and dog-friendly) haven right in the heart of the city. Enjoy complimentary fruits and beverages and free parking. It's the perfect place to be productive and feel right at home.
Be part of the most legendary events and make amazing connections with colleagues from across the entire company.
Comprehensive relocation support for a smooth transition.
We foster innovation and independence while celebrating success in a multicultural and diverse company, where you'll work alongside people from around the globe.
..plus, if you're not already a pro, we'll teach you how to play poker!




Recruitment flow

Interview - Technical Interview - Police record, and background check - Offer
Start date: ASAP
Recruiting from within Estonia
Applications are open until we find the one - take the leap and apply now!




We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status.




Compensation

At Coolbet, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining your salary, including market benchmarks, specific job family, background, location, skills, and experience. The typical range for this role is 3000€-3800€ gross per month. This reflects total compensation.




Location

Tallinn, Estonia




Coolbet is an award-winning gaming company delivering both B2C and B2B platforms across international markets. We offer sportsbook, casino, and poker experiences to players in 8 countries across 3 continents and provide advanced B2B solutions to operators in the US. Part of the Sega Sammy Creation gaming segment, our core team of 500+ experts collaborates across borders to create world-class experiences for both players and operators. With a strong presence at our Tallinn HQ, home to a diverse team representing over 50 nationalities, we bring different perspectives together to shape the future of gaming through continuous product development, a collaborative culture, and a strong commitment to responsible play. Find out more about Coolbet!

The Data Analyst of the Casino Marketing Department which is responsible for Casino related Activations and Promotions. He/she is responsible for performing analysis, prepare reports and presentations for upper management and support operational units with related insights. Furthermore, he/she is involved in the maintenance, integrity, and the security of the membership database.

PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES:


Key Roles & Responsibilities Operational
Supports operational teams with tasks and requests
Generates and maintains reports and database administration
Designs and supports promotions, stage games and ad hoc games.
Analyzes data from the database and performs segmentation analysis for Marketing Bus
Monitors KPIs and delivers relevant reports
Creates Proforma Analysis
Develops campaigns, SMS and outbound sales lists
Assists in Marketing plan and budget Systems
Assists in Casino Management System configuration & maintenance
Performs UAT in cooperation with BU’s of all new casino systems through the Casino Management System SOP
Liaises with Business Processes and assists Marketing Compliance to maintain and prepare the Departmental Operational Manual and SOP of Casino Marketing
Establishes the operational procedures for Database Administration Team to ensure the membership database is well maintained and accurate KEY PERFORMANCE INDICATORS:
Quality of reports and data delivered
Achieves deadlines
Follows and develops workflow and SOPs
Training and UATs
Proper system configuration Page 2 QUALIFICATIONS: Education
Bachelor’s degree in a STEM (Science-Technology-Engineering-Mathematics) field, BI, Data Analysis, or any other relevant field
Post-graduate degree will be considered an advantage Skills / Competencies
Excellent knowledge of MS Excel in Data Analysis, Graphs & Charts, and Pivot Tables. Experience with VBA and macros is preferred
Knowledge of SQL. Additionally, knowledge of MS Access, Tableau, or other relevant software will be considered a plus
Knowledge and experience in Data Analysis and/or Machine Learning in Python and/ or R will be considered a plus.
Knowledge and understanding of casino games’ rules and relevant gaming regulations will be considered a plus
Strong problem-solving capability
Comprehensive planning and communication skills
Committed to self-development and the development of staff
Attention to detail
Fluent in spoken & written English


Perpersonal Competencies


Achieves agreed objectives and accepts accountability for results
Displays a high commitment to delivering results
Leads others to achieve business objectives
Communicates effectively
Displays the highest level of integrity
Ability to maintain discretion
Self-motivated
Approachable

Title: BUSINESS DATA ANALYST
Description:
HELLO! 👋

We are KIKA GROUP – a rapidly growing group of companies with 33 years of market leadership, consisting of:

🏠 KIKA LT – a company managing the KIKA retail chain in Lithuania and Estonia, as well as wholesale operations in Lithuania.

🐾 AKVATERA – a specialized pet food manufacturing company exporting its products to more than 60 countries worldwide.

🥽 AKVATERA W – a modern canned pet food manufacturing facility focused on the highest quality and innovation to ensure the best nutrition for your pets.

🐕 TAURO KENNEL – a dog breeding kennel winning awards at international dog shows.

✨ TAURO GROOMING ACADEMY – premium pet SPA salons and a professional training academy.

🌐 KIKA USA LLC – the group's division in the United States of America.

Your responsibilities:


Analyze large volumes of data and provide insights to support business decision-making.
Develop and maintain reports and visualizations using Power BI.
Work with databases, write and optimize SQL queries.
Create and improve semantic data models.
Apply machine learning methods for forecasting and analysis.
Integrate Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools into analytical processes (e.g., forecasting, segmentation, anomaly detection).
Automate data analysis processes.
Collaborate with other teams to improve data quality and accessibility.


Requirements:


Higher education in Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Statistics, or a related field.
Excellent knowledge of Python and SQL.
Experience working with Power BI.
Ability to create semantic data models.
Understanding of machine learning principles and their application.
Experience working with Big Data solutions.
Experience applying AI tools.
Strong analytical thinking, ability to solve problems independently, attention to detail, and responsibility for data accuracy.
Experience analyzing business needs and providing data-driven insights.
Excellent English language skills.


KIKA offers:

📈 A position in the leading pet products retail and manufacturing company in the Baltic States.

🤝 Dynamic work in an international company alongside a team of experienced and ambitious managers and colleagues.

🏡 Freedom to drive change and opportunities to achieve your professional goals.

💰 A supportive, friendly, and professional team that is looking forward to welcoming you!

🌿 Additional health and accident insurance.

🛒 Additional benefits package (Perks.lt and KIKA discounts for employees and their four-legged companions).

Salary:

€3,300–4,500 gross/month.

We will gladly discuss the specific compensation package during the interview, taking into account your experience and qualifications!

Title: Product Data Analyst II
Description:
At F5, we strive to bring a better digital world to life. Our teams empower organizations across the globe to create, secure, and run applications that enhance how we experience our evolving digital world. We are passionate about cybersecurity, from protecting consumers from fraud to enabling companies to focus on innovation.

Everything we do centers around people. That means we obsess over how to make the lives of our customers, and their customers, better. And it means we prioritize a diverse F5 community where each individual can thrive.

The Opportunity

We believe that behind every great product feature is an incredible story told by data. As a Product Data Analyst II within our Product Data Group, you won’t just be pulling numbers—you’ll be the critical bridge between Product Management, Engineering, and our Data Group. By diving deep into product telemetry and logs, you'll help us decode user behavior and directly shape the future of our products.

What You’ll Do


Design for Impact: Collaborate closely with Product Engineering to design data instrumentation that captures genuine user behavior, events, and meaningful outcomes.
Translate & Transform: Act as the ultimate translator—turning high-level business questions and new feature requests into crisp, technical data requirements.
Uncover Insights: Analyze rich product datasets to guide the development of pipelines and new features, working hand-in-hand with Data Engineers and Data Scientists.
Define Success: Help define and track the core metrics and KPIs that truly reflect product health and performance.
Guard Data Quality: Conduct rigorous data quality analyses to spot gaps before they become issues, and contribute to the design of robust monitoring and alerting systems.



What You’ll Bring To The Table


Solid Experience: 2-4 years shaping data into insights, with a focus on statistical analysis, dashboarding, and data quality/profiling.
Technical Chops: Strong proficiency in SQL—you know how to navigate, query, and tame large, complex datasets.
Data Storytelling: Strong ability to shape narratives using data, providing visualization and reports that illuminate the import of the data
Domain Knowledge: Familiarity with product telemetry and observability (knowing how products "talk" to us).
Collaboration: Stellar communication skills. You know how to tell a compelling story with data and collaborate effortlessly across varied teams.



Bonus Points (Nice to Have)


Experience navigating a software development organization, including familiarity with version control (git), the SDLC, and testing frameworks.
Hands-on experience with modern data stack tools like dbt, Databricks, and Tableau.



The Job Description is intended to be a general representation of the responsibilities and requirements of the job. However, the description may not be all-inclusive, and responsibilities and requirements are subject to change.

Please note that F5 only contacts candidates through F5 email address (ending with @f5.com) or auto email notification from Workday (ending with f5.com or @myworkday.com).

Equal Employment Opportunity

It is the policy of F5 to provide equal employment opportunities to all employees and employment applicants without regard to unlawful considerations of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, sensory, physical, or mental disability, marital status, veteran or military status, genetic information, or any other classification protected by applicable local, state, or federal laws. This policy applies to all aspects of employment, including, but not limited to, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, discipline, and termination. F5 offers a variety of reasonable accommodations for candidates. Requesting an accommodation is completely voluntary. F5 will assess the need for accommodations in the application process separately from those that may be needed to perform the job. Request by contacting accommodations@f5.com.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
About Block Labs

Block Labs is a premier technology studio operating at the bleeding edge of Web3, Artificial Intelligence, and iGaming. We don't just ship features; we engineer high-scale, production-grade platforms that power the next generation of digital products.

We are a collective of senior engineers, product strategists, and builders who refuse to compromise on architecture. Whether we are designing autonomous multi-agent AI systems, building decentralized financial infrastructure, or architecting high-frequency iGaming platforms, our standard is excellence.

We move fast, but we build for the long term. If you are looking to work alongside a team that values deep technical expertise, thoughtful system design, and product ownership, Block Labs is where you belong.

About The Role

We’re looking for a data-obsessed BI Analyst to turn blockchain and product data into crisp insights that drive growth, retention, and risk controls across our crypto-enabled iGaming products. You’ll own the end-to-end analytics stack for wallet funnels, wagering behavior, lifecycle marketing, and compliance—using SQL, DBT, Python, Amplitude and Tableau as your primary tools. In the future we planning transition from Tableau to other BI tools alternatives like Metabase or Apache Superset or others.

Key Responsibilities


Drive the business forward through proactive analytics - don't just fulfill dashboard requests; spot anomalies, frame the right question yourself, and bring actionable recommendations to Product / CRM / Risk before they have to ask..
Build, maintain, and iterate dashboards surfacing KPIs (GGR/NGR, ARPU, LTV, churn, deposit→wager conversion, fraud loss rate).
Write production-grade SQL (CTEs, window functions, incremental models) to model clean datasets from Clickhouse and on-chain tables.
Build and maintain AI-agent workflows that automate recurring analytics - anomaly investigation, root-cause loops etc.
Use Python (pandas, seaborn, sklearn, scipy) for analysis, data quality checks, lightweight ETL/backfills, API pulls (e.g., blockchain providers), and automation.
Work with on-chain data: parse EVM logs, token transfers (ERC-20/721), join labeled address datasets, analyze exchange/bridge flows, gas/fee dynamics, and wallet clustering.
Partner with Product, Marketing/CRM, Risk/Compliance, and Engineering to define event schemas and tracking plans; ensure data quality, lineage, and documentation.
Design and analyze experiments, cohort analyses, and attribution within iGaming and regional compliance constraints.
Investigate anomalies (bonus abuse, botting, arbitrage, suspicious wallet rings) and recommend mitigations.
Automate recurring reporting and alerting for KPI movements.


Must-haves


2+ years in BI or Data Analytics; crypto or iGaming exposure prefered.
Ambitions about building great analytics infrastructure.
Good SQL: complex joins, window functions, query optimization, and dimensional modeling.
Solid AI-collaboration craft — prompt & context engineering, spec-driven task framing, and eval-driven iteration. Comfortable decomposing business questions into precise, agent-executable steps and designing the right tools for the agent to call.
BI Versatility: Expert knowledge of Tableau (LODs, parameters, performance tuning) combined with a strong interest or experience in modern, open-source BI tools like Metabase or Apache Superset.
Proficient Python for analytics & data engineering: pandas, modular scripts/notebooks, API integrations, scheduling small jobs, and writing maintainable code.
Solid statistics and probability fundamentals for experimentation - hypothesis testing, sample size and power, confidence intervals, and the discipline to distinguish real signal from noise.
Practical understanding of crypto wallets and transaction flows.


Nice-to-haves


dbt, Great Expectations/Monte Carlo for data quality; orchestration with Airflow/Prefect.
Built or contributed to production AI agents for analytics — ReAct loops, RAG over a knowledge base , MCP servers for data sources, multi-agent orchestration. Knows how to curate domain knowledge for agents so they stop hallucinating.
Ability to produce on-chain analytics (Dune/Flipside/Goldsky/Nansen tables or self-indexed data; contract ABIs; entity labeling).
Warehouse ecosystems knowledge Clickhouse, AWS, S3 buckets.
Data governance & privacy (GDPR, data minimization, retention policies).
Understanding of iGaming economics & KPIs: deposits, wagers, hold, GGR/NGR, RTP, bonus efficiency, RG metrics, and common fraud/AML patterns.
Experience with product analytics services like Amplitude, Mixpanel, Google Analytics.


What kind of culture can I expect?

Mature, mission-driven, and low-ego. We value clarity over noise, outcomes over theatrics, and pace without chaos. If you’re one of the smartest minds in your craft and want to build with other experts, you’ll feel at home here.
