Title: Data Lead (Growth & Analytics)
Description:
Mid-senior position | Part-time or Full-time | On-site @Ljubljana




About the role

We're building a real-world social platform. Matched meetups that get people out of their homes and turn strangers into friends. It works. Now we need someone to build the data infrastructure that helps us scale it.




As our Data Lead (Growth & Analytics) you'll own everything data: event tracking, pipelines, the dashboards that drive our decisions. If a metric matters to the business, you built the system that surfaces it.




We're an AI-first team - that includes BI. We're building AI-powered dashboards and we want someone who finds that exciting and has done this in the past.




You'll be one of our first 10 team members. Early enough to shape how we instrument, what we trust, and how decisions get made. If you've spent years in a SaaS or subscription business and you intuitively know which numbers lie and which ones matter, you'll fit right in.




It's fast. It's sometimes messy. The problems are real and the impact is direct.




You’ll be a great fit if you:

Work best without permission
Default to "how could I systemize this"
Had weird teenage hobbies
Spend your Sundays on side projects
Shipped something this week
Are good at chess, poker or Monopoly
Forget to grab lunch because you’re in the zone
Get the best ideas while showering
Have left a job because the pace was too slow
Are more motivated by winning than by being right
Find bureaucracy physically painful




What you’ll be doing

Data infrastructure

Own data architecture decisions and tooling selection across marketing, revenue, finance, product, and operations
Set up and maintain a data warehouse
Set up experimentation tooling
Ensure data accuracy, freshness, and consistency
Implement data validation, anomaly detection, and monitoring

Tracking

Build attribution tracking for Meta Ads, Google Ads, email and other channels
Measure reliable data at every step of the acquisition funnel
Diagnose and resolve tracking discrepancies across platforms
Work with other teams to implement and QA tracking changes

Data analysis

Translate data into clear, actionable recommendations for leadership
Build forecasting models for revenue, churn, and growth scenarios
Identify leading indicators and growth constraints
Support strategic decisions around pricing, roadmap, and scale strategy
Implement cohort dashboards, churn prediction tools, and LTV/CAC reporting
Segmentation by geographies, niches, channels, etc
User feedback analysis
Operational efficiency analysis

Experimentation

Set success metrics and guardrails for experiments
Run A/B tests on landing pages, onboarding funnels, pricing pages, upsells, etc
Evaluating A/B tests and suggesting new ones

Product & business insights

Analyze user behavior across onboarding, activation, engagement, and retention
Identify friction points and drop-offs in core user journeys
Partner with product, marketing, and ops to drive improvements




Requirements

Essential:

Fluent in English (written and spoken)
At least 3 years of relevant experience
Experience working with a subscription/SaaS business (familiar with metrics such as MRR, churn, cash conversion cycle,...)
Comfortable working with SQL, data modeling, BI, retention/LTV analysis, attribution, and forecasting.
AI-first mindset - ability to heavily rely on AI tools and build in such a way that we can keep leveraging AI in the future
Strong revenue strategy mindset: understands monetisation, pricing, payback logic, and CAC/LTV trade-offs.
Comfortable working in ambiguity, adapting to changes and wearing multiple hats
Ability to identify ideal software and integrate it into the company’s workflow
Attention to detail and the ability to work independently without constant oversight
A lot of drive, energy, and time.
Ability to work from our office in Ljubljana city center

Bonus points:

Previous startup experience (especially early-stage)
Experience building teams from scratch
You are based close to Ljubljana (or willing to move)




About us

We help people meet new friends through real-life events all around the world.




We’re building something ambitious. Small team. Global startup. Serious brains. Real responsibility. Fast growth. Mobile app / B2C SaaS.




We are past product-market fit and are now in fast-scaling mode.




If you want to ship fast, work hard, own systems end-to-end, and grow with the company - this is for you.




Founded by Jani Pravdič and Matic Jesenovec – entrepreneurs who've together built multiple businesses impacting hundreds of thousands of people and generating well over eight figures in revenue. We're now building something that we believe has the potential to grow much larger and have a greater impact than anything we’ve done before.




What’s in it for you

Working closely with the founders
Working in a high-performance team
It takes us hours instead of weeks to launch/implement something
We test things and we listen to the numbers, not who is the loudest in the room
Failure is part of the process; we accept it and learn from it
Opportunity to grow into a higher role
The chance to genuinely impact many thousands of lives globally
A front-row seat in an exciting, fast-growing start-up
Competitive salary
Performance bonuses




Our values

Strategic Boldness: We do what it takes to deliver faster, farther, and louder.
Agility > Perfection: We value action over polish, effectiveness over effort. Ship fast, refine later.
Scrappy & Curious: We test, learn, and adapt constantly. Resourceful beats resource-rich.
Ambition + Humility: We learn from the best, then push beyond. Inspiration fuels our innovation.
Long Game Thinking: We’re impatient with action, patient with results. Correct quickly, stay the course.




Next steps

We’re looking for someone to start as soon as possible. So if the role sounds exciting, please follow these steps:

Fill out the application form.
Our team carefully reviews every application, so the selection process may take some time.
The best candidates will receive an additional test assignment.
Those who perform best on this task will be invited to an extensive interview.




If you do not hear from us within 14 days of submitting your application, it means we have decided to continue the process with other candidates at this time.

HTML:
Mid-senior position | Part-time or Full-time | On-site @Ljubljana


About the role

We're building a real-world social platform. Matched meetups that get people out of their homes and turn strangers into friends. It works. Now we need someone to build the data infrastructure that helps us scale it.


As our Data Lead (Growth & Analytics) you'll own everything data: event tracking, pipelines, the dashboards that drive our decisions. If a metric matters to the business, you built the system that surfaces it.


We're an AI-first team - that includes BI. We're building AI-powered dashboards and we want someone who finds that exciting and has done this in the past.


You'll be one of our first 10 team members. Early enough to shape how we instrument, what we trust, and how decisions get made. If you've spent years in a SaaS or subscription business and you intuitively know which numbers lie and which ones matter, you'll fit right in.


It's fast. It's sometimes messy. The problems are real and the impact is direct.


You’ll be a great fit if you:

  • Work best without permission
  • Default to "how could I systemize this"
  • Had weird teenage hobbies
  • Spend your Sundays on side projects
  • Shipped something this week
  • Are good at chess, poker or Monopoly
  • Forget to grab lunch because you’re in the zone
  • Get the best ideas while showering
  • Have left a job because the pace was too slow
  • Are more motivated by winning than by being right
  • Find bureaucracy physically painful


What you’ll be doing

Data infrastructure

  • Own data architecture decisions and tooling selection across marketing, revenue, finance, product, and operations
  • Set up and maintain a data warehouse
  • Set up experimentation tooling
  • Ensure data accuracy, freshness, and consistency
  • Implement data validation, anomaly detection, and monitoring

Tracking

  • Build attribution tracking for Meta Ads, Google Ads, email and other channels
  • Measure reliable data at every step of the acquisition funnel
  • Diagnose and resolve tracking discrepancies across platforms
  • Work with other teams to implement and QA tracking changes

Data analysis

  • Translate data into clear, actionable recommendations for leadership
  • Build forecasting models for revenue, churn, and growth scenarios
  • Identify leading indicators and growth constraints
  • Support strategic decisions around pricing, roadmap, and scale strategy
  • Implement cohort dashboards, churn prediction tools, and LTV/CAC reporting
  • Segmentation by geographies, niches, channels, etc
  • User feedback analysis
  • Operational efficiency analysis

Experimentation

  • Set success metrics and guardrails for experiments
  • Run A/B tests on landing pages, onboarding funnels, pricing pages, upsells, etc
  • Evaluating A/B tests and suggesting new ones

Product & business insights

  • Analyze user behavior across onboarding, activation, engagement, and retention
  • Identify friction points and drop-offs in core user journeys
  • Partner with product, marketing, and ops to drive improvements


Requirements

Essential:

  • Fluent in English (written and spoken)
  • At least 3 years of relevant experience
  • Experience working with a subscription/SaaS business (familiar with metrics such as MRR, churn, cash conversion cycle,...)
  • Comfortable working with SQL, data modeling, BI, retention/LTV analysis, attribution, and forecasting.
  • AI-first mindset - ability to heavily rely on AI tools and build in such a way that we can keep leveraging AI in the future
  • Strong revenue strategy mindset: understands monetisation, pricing, payback logic, and CAC/LTV trade-offs.
  • Comfortable working in ambiguity, adapting to changes and wearing multiple hats
  • Ability to identify ideal software and integrate it into the company’s workflow
  • Attention to detail and the ability to work independently without constant oversight
  • A lot of drive, energy, and time.
  • Ability to work from our office in Ljubljana city center

Bonus points:

  • Previous startup experience (especially early-stage)
  • Experience building teams from scratch
  • You are based close to Ljubljana (or willing to move)


About us

We help people meet new friends through real-life events all around the world.


We’re building something ambitious. Small team. Global startup. Serious brains. Real responsibility. Fast growth. Mobile app / B2C SaaS.


We are past product-market fit and are now in fast-scaling mode.


If you want to ship fast, work hard, own systems end-to-end, and grow with the company - this is for you.


Founded by Jani Pravdič and Matic Jesenovec – entrepreneurs who've together built multiple businesses impacting hundreds of thousands of people and generating well over eight figures in revenue. We're now building something that we believe has the potential to grow much larger and have a greater impact than anything we’ve done before.


What’s in it for you

  • Working closely with the founders
  • Working in a high-performance team
  • It takes us hours instead of weeks to launch/implement something
  • We test things and we listen to the numbers, not who is the loudest in the room
  • Failure is part of the process; we accept it and learn from it
  • Opportunity to grow into a higher role
  • The chance to genuinely impact many thousands of lives globally
  • A front-row seat in an exciting, fast-growing start-up
  • Competitive salary
  • Performance bonuses


Our values

  • Strategic Boldness: We do what it takes to deliver faster, farther, and louder.
  • Agility > Perfection: We value action over polish, effectiveness over effort. Ship fast, refine later.
  • Scrappy & Curious: We test, learn, and adapt constantly. Resourceful beats resource-rich.
  • Ambition + Humility: We learn from the best, then push beyond. Inspiration fuels our innovation.
  • Long Game Thinking: We’re impatient with action, patient with results. Correct quickly, stay the course.


Next steps

We’re looking for someone to start as soon as possible. So if the role sounds exciting, please follow these steps:

  1. Fill out the application form.
  2. Our team carefully reviews every application, so the selection process may take some time.
  3. The best candidates will receive an additional test assignment.
  4. Those who perform best on this task will be invited to an extensive interview.


If you do not hear from us within 14 days of submitting your application, it means we have decided to continue the process with other candidates at this time.

Title: Analytic Support Analyst
Description:
Cencora, previously known as AmerisourceBergen, is a leading global pharmaceutical solutions organization centered on improving lives. Ranked #21 on the Global Fortune 500, our team members are united in our responsibility to create healthier futures.

Our Shared Service Center in Lithuania is experiencing rapid growth and we have many diverse and exciting roles in Customer Service, Operations, IT, Finance and HR. Join us and make a positive impact on human and animal health.

Job Details

We are seeking an experienced Analyst to join our Analytic Support team under the guidance of the Analytic Support Manager. This role offers the opportunity to lead impactful projects, work across departments to deliver analytics solutions, and support data-driven decision-making across the organization.

This is a dynamic role with opportunities for professional growth, including advanced analytics, project management, and leadership development.

Responsibilities


Operate as the first point of contact for World Courier employees by keeping track of reporting incidents.
Ensure the resolution of basic incidents, and/or escalate to senior team members based on incident complexity.
Support business requests related to reporting system access and reporting system usage.
Create, update and debug Alteryx workflows.
Perform initial assessment of SQL stored procedures to identify potential data errors.
Assist in administration of Alteryx Server and Alteryx Gallery environments.
Actively review queues and monitor tool usage.
Run periodic reports in Excel. Ensure timely report delivery to stakeholders.
Support data extraction from SQL database based on requests from different business segments.
Serve as a resource for interpreting data and information.
Create and/or update documentation, self-service guides reporting tools.
Work closely with all other teams in Analytics to coordinate cross-functional activities and ensure deliverables are met.
Support senior team members on enhancements to existing tools and processes.


Education


Bachelor’s degree in a relevant discipline (e.g., Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics).


Work Experience


Minimum 3+ years of experience in analytics and reporting, ideally within logistics or a related field.


Skills And Knowledge


Planning, analytical, and conceptual skills to evaluate business problems to identify and apply appropriate solutions.
Knowledge of Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Excel (essential).
Ability to write and understand Structure Query Language (SQL).
Experience with relational databases (e.g. Microsoft SQL) and data management (desirable).
Ability to create and/or understand one of the following - Alteryx Designer, Power BI (desirable).
Experience with ServiceNow or any other ticket management system (desirable).
Ability to effectively participate in cross-functional global teams.
Intellectual curiosity and a self-starter mindset.
Strong organizational skills with attention to detail.
Ability to build strong relationships and collaborate across teams.


Salary: 2,135.94 - 3,051.37 eur gross/month

What Cencora offers

We offer a competitive annual bonus, life insurance from Day 1, a best-in-class health insurance package, and up to 6 fully paid benefit days a year. As a Cencora employee, you have the benefit of our referral bonus scheme, our boundless learning opportunities and our global Employee Assistance Program. We have a wonderful office location in Quadrum, equipped with everything you need for a small break at work and fresh snacks at all times. Become part of our purpose-driven, multicultural team now and help us create healthier futures

Full time

Gross Salary Range (Monthly)

Affiliated Companies

Affiliated Companies: World Courier (Lithuania) UAB

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re a team of 500+ professionals who develop cutting-edge proxy and web data scraping solutions for thousands of the world’s best known businesses, including Fortune 500 companies.

What’s in store for you:

You’ll be developing complex products with high coding standards, maintaining our own infrastructure, handling petabytes of data, and solving challenges on a daily basis. We got you covered with a team of strong professionals to support you, a well-built tech stack, and loads of ownership.

In this role, you’ll:


Develop a solid understanding of the relationships between the behaviour of our customers and our growth drivers through data analysis and modelling
Create and maintain self-service dashboards for stakeholders across business, using PowerBI
Utilize analytical tools (SQL, Python, Google Sheets) to prepare and analyze data, supporting key decision-making processes
Carry out ad-hoc analysis to address stakeholder questions and effectively communicating your findings
Ensure data integrity and accuracy by performing regular data audits and validation
Collaborate with business users to determine KPIs and critical metrics to monitor for new business lines
Build forecast models to support business development
Deliver actionable insights and data-driven recommendations



Your skills & experience:


2+ years of experience working on business problems in a Data Analyst/Business Analyst role
Strong knowledge of SQL
Hands-on experience building effective, easy-to-navigate dashboards, ideally with PowerBI (or other data visualization tools such as Tableau, Qlik Sense, Looker, Grafana)
Fluency in English and Lithuanian
Ability to generate actionable insights and recommendations from data analysis with an understanding of how these insights can drive business impact
Problem solving and being a team player.
Pragmatic and impact-focused - you will choose Excel over Python if it is a better solution for the problem and vice-versa
Proven ability to formulate and test hypotheses about business performance
Strong communication skills to effectively collaborate with stakeholders and present findings



Tech Stack:


Google Cloud Platform (GCS, BQ) for storage and warehousing;
dbt and SQL for data modelling;
Python for automation;
PowerBI for data visualisation;
Airflow for orchestration;
Any tool you like for ad-hoc analysis
We don't expect you to master or know all of these technologies today



Salary & Benefits:


Gross salary: 3200 - 4500 EUR/month. Keep in mind that we are open to discussing a different salary based on your skills and experience
Growth & Learning: 40+ internal learning options, external conferences, mentorship, and year-round knowledge-sharing
Health & Well-being: Private health insurance, psychotherapy, on-site well-being consultants, 24/7 gym access, and a wellness app
Celebration & Community: Team events, an overseas workation, quarterly team-building budgets, and plenty of ways to mark milestones together
Plus bonus vacation days, paid life-moment days off, barista coffee, and all the tools you need. See the full list of benefits here: https://career.oxylabs.io/blog/our-work-benefits/



Up for the challenge? Let’s talk!

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Product Data Analyst II
Description:
At F5, we strive to bring a better digital world to life. Our teams empower organizations across the globe to create, secure, and run applications that enhance how we experience our evolving digital world. We are passionate about cybersecurity, from protecting consumers from fraud to enabling companies to focus on innovation.

Everything we do centers around people. That means we obsess over how to make the lives of our customers, and their customers, better. And it means we prioritize a diverse F5 community where each individual can thrive.

The Opportunity

We believe that behind every great product feature is an incredible story told by data. As a Product Data Analyst II within our Product Data Group, you won’t just be pulling numbers—you’ll be the critical bridge between Product Management, Engineering, and our Data Group. By diving deep into product telemetry and logs, you'll help us decode user behavior and directly shape the future of our products.

What You’ll Do


Design for Impact: Collaborate closely with Product Engineering to design data instrumentation that captures genuine user behavior, events, and meaningful outcomes.
Translate & Transform: Act as the ultimate translator—turning high-level business questions and new feature requests into crisp, technical data requirements.
Uncover Insights: Analyze rich product datasets to guide the development of pipelines and new features, working hand-in-hand with Data Engineers and Data Scientists.
Define Success: Help define and track the core metrics and KPIs that truly reflect product health and performance.
Guard Data Quality: Conduct rigorous data quality analyses to spot gaps before they become issues, and contribute to the design of robust monitoring and alerting systems.



What You’ll Bring To The Table


Solid Experience: 2-4 years shaping data into insights, with a focus on statistical analysis, dashboarding, and data quality/profiling.
Technical Chops: Strong proficiency in SQL—you know how to navigate, query, and tame large, complex datasets.
Data Storytelling: Strong ability to shape narratives using data, providing visualization and reports that illuminate the import of the data
Domain Knowledge: Familiarity with product telemetry and observability (knowing how products "talk" to us).
Collaboration: Stellar communication skills. You know how to tell a compelling story with data and collaborate effortlessly across varied teams.



Bonus Points (Nice to Have)


Experience navigating a software development organization, including familiarity with version control (git), the SDLC, and testing frameworks.
Hands-on experience with modern data stack tools like dbt, Databricks, and Tableau.



The Job Description is intended to be a general representation of the responsibilities and requirements of the job. However, the description may not be all-inclusive, and responsibilities and requirements are subject to change.

Please note that F5 only contacts candidates through F5 email address (ending with @f5.com) or auto email notification from Workday (ending with f5.com or @myworkday.com).

Equal Employment Opportunity

It is the policy of F5 to provide equal employment opportunities to all employees and employment applicants without regard to unlawful considerations of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, sensory, physical, or mental disability, marital status, veteran or military status, genetic information, or any other classification protected by applicable local, state, or federal laws. This policy applies to all aspects of employment, including, but not limited to, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, discipline, and termination. F5 offers a variety of reasonable accommodations for candidates. Requesting an accommodation is completely voluntary. F5 will assess the need for accommodations in the application process separately from those that may be needed to perform the job. Request by contacting accommodations@f5.com.

Title: Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst
Description:
Over 50,000 customers globally trust our end-to-end, cloud-driven networking solutions and rely on our top-rated services and support to accelerate their digital transformation efforts and deliver progress like never before and with double digit growth year over year, no provider is better positioned to deliver better outcomes on scale, than Extreme.

Come become part of something big with us! We are a global leader, with hubs in Europe, North America, South America, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East.

Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst

About The Role

We are seeking a “Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst” to join our Extreme Networks Global Operations organization, where you'll turn complex operational data into clear, actionable insight that drives decisions and automation across our Global Supply Chan and Order Management teams. This role sits at the intersection of analytics, AI, and supply chain strategy, partnering with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, build analytical products, and tell the stories behind the numbers. The ideal candidate is a curious, technically strong analyst who is equally comfortable writing SQL, using AI tools, framing a business problem, managing a project, and presenting findings to stakeholders. You should be excited about the rapid evolution of AI and agentic workflows and how they can be applied to supply chain decision-making and you should be actively using AI tools in your work today.

A Note On Experience

This role requires commercially delivered analytical work. We will ask you at interview to walk through a specific project from problem through to business outcome that you owned and delivered in a professional setting. Academic or course-based projects do not qualify. If your experience is primarily from an academic programme, this role is not the right fit at this time

Key Responsibilities


Partner with supply chain stakeholders to scope problems and translate them into structured, measurable, automated workstreams.
Build and maintain python/SQL/Snowflake-based data models, dashboards, and self-service analytics for our global operations teams
Help operationalize AI and agent-based solutions to automate recurring analyses, augment decision support, and supply chain processes.
Apply statistical methods to inform data driven decisions across our global operations function.
Manage your projects end-to-end: scoping, stakeholder alignment, data discovery, modelling, validation, and rollout.
Translate analytical findings into compelling narratives (written, visual, and verbal) tailored to operational, business, and technical audiences.
Define and track KPIs that measure supply chain performance and flag interventions when metrics drift.
Contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality, documentation, and analytical standards across the team.


Required Skills & Qualifications


3-5 years experience in business analytics, data analytics, or a comparable quantitative role.
Demonstrated use of AI tools such as Claude, MS Copilot, or ChatGPT – in an analytical workflow, with a genuine appetite to develop agentic and automation capabilities in role.
Strong python skills and hands-on experience with Snowflake (or a comparable cloud data warehouse such as BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks).
Demonstrated project management ability, owning deliverables across multiple stakeholders and timelines.
Solid grounding in descriptive statistics, and analytics fundamentals.
Strong data storytelling skills: the ability to distil complexity into clear visuals and narratives that drive action.
Supply chain domain knowledge: familiarity with demand and supply planning, inventory optimization, order management, logistics, procurement, or manufacturing operations.
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).


Technical Skills Required


Programming for analytics: Python or R for modelling, automation, and analytics workflows.
BI and visualization tools: Tableau, Sigma, Power BI, Looker, or similar — both for building and for design literacy.
ERP / source systems: Exposure to Oracle Fusion Cloud or similar transactional systems.


Preferred / Additional Skills: These are skills that would meaningfully strengthen a candidate for this role:


Practical experience applying agentic workflows (e.g., LLM-based assistants, retrieval-augmented analytics, automated reasoning agents) to analytical or operational problems.
Master's or PhD in a quantitative field (Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).
Forecasting and optimization: time-series methods as well as other optimization techniques.
Machine learning fundamentals: classification, regression, clustering, and an understanding of when ML is and isn't the right tool.
Process improvement methodologies: Lean, Six Sigma, or equivalent structured problem-solving frameworks.
Stakeholder management and influencing: navigating ambiguity, managing competing priorities, and building trust with non-technical partners.


What Success Looks Like


Within 3 months: deeply understand our supply chain data landscape, key stakeholders, and top-priority business questions.
Within 6 months: deliver at least one high-impact analytical product or AI-enabled workflow that changes how a decision is made.
Within 12 months: become a trusted analytics partner to supply chain stakeholders and a contributor to the broader analytics team's standards and practices.


Salary Is Based On Qualifications And Experience Between 60-70K

Extreme Networks Benefits

HTML:
Over 50,000 customers globally trust our end-to-end, cloud-driven networking solutions and rely on our top-rated services and support to accelerate their digital transformation efforts and deliver progress like never before and with double digit growth year over year, no provider is better positioned to deliver better outcomes on scale, than Extreme.

Come become part of something big with us! We are a global leader, with hubs in Europe, North America, South America, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East.

Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst

About The Role

We are seeking a “Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst” to join our Extreme Networks Global Operations organization, where you'll turn complex operational data into clear, actionable insight that drives decisions and automation across our Global Supply Chan and Order Management teams. This role sits at the intersection of analytics, AI, and supply chain strategy, partnering with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, build analytical products, and tell the stories behind the numbers. The ideal candidate is a curious, technically strong analyst who is equally comfortable writing SQL, using AI tools, framing a business problem, managing a project, and presenting findings to stakeholders. You should be excited about the rapid evolution of AI and agentic workflows and how they can be applied to supply chain decision-making and you should be actively using AI tools in your work today.

A Note On Experience

This role requires commercially delivered analytical work. We will ask you at interview to walk through a specific project from problem through to business outcome that you owned and delivered in a professional setting. Academic or course-based projects do not qualify. If your experience is primarily from an academic programme, this role is not the right fit at this time

Key Responsibilities

  • Partner with supply chain stakeholders to scope problems and translate them into structured, measurable, automated workstreams.
  • Build and maintain python/SQL/Snowflake-based data models, dashboards, and self-service analytics for our global operations teams
  • Help operationalize AI and agent-based solutions to automate recurring analyses, augment decision support, and supply chain processes.
  • Apply statistical methods to inform data driven decisions across our global operations function.
  • Manage your projects end-to-end: scoping, stakeholder alignment, data discovery, modelling, validation, and rollout.
  • Translate analytical findings into compelling narratives (written, visual, and verbal) tailored to operational, business, and technical audiences.
  • Define and track KPIs that measure supply chain performance and flag interventions when metrics drift.
  • Contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality, documentation, and analytical standards across the team.

Required Skills & Qualifications

  • 3-5 years experience in business analytics, data analytics, or a comparable quantitative role.
  • Demonstrated use of AI tools such as Claude, MS Copilot, or ChatGPT – in an analytical workflow, with a genuine appetite to develop agentic and automation capabilities in role.
  • Strong python skills and hands-on experience with Snowflake (or a comparable cloud data warehouse such as BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks).
  • Demonstrated project management ability, owning deliverables across multiple stakeholders and timelines.
  • Solid grounding in descriptive statistics, and analytics fundamentals.
  • Strong data storytelling skills: the ability to distil complexity into clear visuals and narratives that drive action.
  • Supply chain domain knowledge: familiarity with demand and supply planning, inventory optimization, order management, logistics, procurement, or manufacturing operations.
  • Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).

Technical Skills Required

  • Programming for analytics: Python or R for modelling, automation, and analytics workflows.
  • BI and visualization tools: Tableau, Sigma, Power BI, Looker, or similar — both for building and for design literacy.
  • ERP / source systems: Exposure to Oracle Fusion Cloud or similar transactional systems.

Preferred / Additional Skills: These are skills that would meaningfully strengthen a candidate for this role:

  • Practical experience applying agentic workflows (e.g., LLM-based assistants, retrieval-augmented analytics, automated reasoning agents) to analytical or operational problems.
  • Master's or PhD in a quantitative field (Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).
  • Forecasting and optimization: time-series methods as well as other optimization techniques.
  • Machine learning fundamentals: classification, regression, clustering, and an understanding of when ML is and isn't the right tool.
  • Process improvement methodologies: Lean, Six Sigma, or equivalent structured problem-solving frameworks.
  • Stakeholder management and influencing: navigating ambiguity, managing competing priorities, and building trust with non-technical partners.

What Success Looks Like

  • Within 3 months: deeply understand our supply chain data landscape, key stakeholders, and top-priority business questions.
  • Within 6 months: deliver at least one high-impact analytical product or AI-enabled workflow that changes how a decision is made.
  • Within 12 months: become a trusted analytics partner to supply chain stakeholders and a contributor to the broader analytics team's standards and practices.

Salary Is Based On Qualifications And Experience Between 60-70K

Title: Data Analyst - Internal Audit
Description:
Company Description

Wise is a global technology company, building the best way to move and manage the world’s money.

Min fees. Max ease. Full speed.

Whether people and businesses are sending money to another country, spending abroad, or making and receiving international payments, Wise is on a mission to make their lives easier and save them money.

As part of our team, you will be helping us create an entirely new network for the world's money.

For everyone, everywhere.

Job Description

More about our mission and what we offer.

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join the Internal Audit branch of the Compliance & Internal Audit Analytics team at Wise.

You’ll partner with Internal Audit (IA) to develop and implement the analytics strategy across the function.

Internal Audit serves as an independent 3rd line of defence that oversees and provides an independent assessment and reasonable assurance on the effectiveness of the company’s corporate governance structure + internal controls.

The IA Analytics team supports IA by using analytics to provide more coverage, efficiency and accuracy to audit testing, as well as building innovative tools and using automation to support continuous testing and other IA activities beyond the annual audit plan.

This is an IC2 role. For more information on our Analytics Career Map and levelling structure, click here.

What You’ll Be Doing


Partnering with stakeholders across the IA function covering all regions, products and domains of the business (including Financial Crime, Ops, Risk, Finance and Technology)
Identifying & implementing opportunities to do the most impactful analytics within audits and influencing and upskilling your partners in IA to do the same
Working with analytics, engineering, and business specialists to seek out and understand interesting and varied datasets, perform analytics using modern approaches, and develop a strong understanding of operations at Wise
Championing innovation and data-led insights, not just within audits but across all IA activities where analytics can add value
Working on automation and tooling projects to increase efficiency across IA including workflow automation, continuous auditing, leveraging AI, and building MI dashboards


Qualifications

What you’ll bring:


3+ years of experience in data analytics
1+ years of data analytics experience within an audit, risk, or compliance related role
Advanced stakeholder management skills; you know how to leverage multiple different teams to deliver high-impact change including analytics, engineering, and business specialists
Extensive experience using SQL; you can build and maintain complex data sources and can leverage visualisation tools to surface insights


Nice To Have


Experience of working in regulated environments, Fintech or Financial Services.
Knowledge of financial crime.
Experience using dbt, Superset, or Looker.
Knowledge of advanced analytics techniques, including the use of Python and visualisation techniques.


Additional Information

For everyone, everywhere. We're people building money without borders — without judgement or prejudice, too. We believe teams are strongest when they are diverse, equitable and inclusive.

We're proud to have a truly international team, and we celebrate our differences.

Inclusive teams help us live our values and make sure every Wiser feels respected, empowered to contribute towards our mission and able to progress in their careers.

If you want to find out more about what it's like to work at Wise visit Wise.Jobs.

Keep up to date with life at Wise by following us on LinkedIn and Instagram.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
About Cityswift

CitySwift is the world’s leading performance optimisation platform for public transport, enabling data-driven decision-making for some of the largest transport networks globally. Backed by over €15 million in venture capital funding, we partner with major public transport operators and government authorities, including Go-Ahead Group, Transport for London, and many more. CitySwift is experiencing accelerated growth, with 117% year-over-year revenue growth in 2025.

Our mission is to increase the adoption of sustainable public transport worldwide. Today, our platform optimises more than 3 billion passenger journeys annually, and our target is to reach 10 billion journeys by the end of 2026.

At CitySwift, you’ll have the opportunity to take ownership, make a measurable impact, and grow professionally as part of a collaborative, mission-driven team. Join us in shaping the future of public transport by driving the next phase of CitySwift’s growth.

About The Role

As an Analytics Engineer, your mission is to ensure that every data user at CitySwift is working from a single, high-quality source of truth. You will apply software engineering best practices—like version control, testing, and continuous integration—to the world of data warehousing.

In the Data Analytics team, we sit in the information layer between our tech stack and our customers. You will play a critical role in scaling our Modern Data Information Layer. You are building scalable data models that enable our platform to deliver real-time insights to the world’s largest transport operators. If you thrive on seeing the impact of data on business decisions, if you're curious about solving business problems at scale, if you want to be at the centre of AI adoption and if you love working in a creative data-led environment, then this role is for you.

Key Responsibilities


Data modelling & architecture: Build & create data models that help solve business problems. Follow team conventions and best practices for data architecture design. Understand Git workflows and raise PRs for review.
Semantic Layer & BI development: Define, build and edit semantic layer views that power customer-facing analytics such as User Apps, Dashboards, and AI tools. Create and build scalable and reusable dashboards.
Engineering collaboration & deployment: Understand the essentials of how analytics are surfaced within products. Participate in cross-team meetings and follow agreed deployment processes.
Commercial & stakeholder partnership: Understand the core commercial metrics and KPIs of the business. Respond to data requests from commercial teams. Build awareness of how data outputs are used in decision-making.
Team enablement & technical leadership: Seek support from senior colleagues, be curious and ask challenging questions. Share learnings with data peers. Follows established team processes and documentation standards.



Desired Qualifications


Experience: 3+ years in a data-centric role (Analytics, Data Engineering, or BI)
Business Acumen: Experience with solving real-world problems with data and translating data insights into outcomes.
Tooling Mastery: Advanced-level SQL (window functions, CTEs, optimisation). Deep familiarity with BigQuery or a similar cloud data warehouse (Snowflake, Redshift, etc.).
Engineering Mindset: Proficient with Git and the command line. You understand why modularity, DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principles, and documentation are non-negotiable.
Problem Solving: Experience handling large-scale datasets. You know how to handle time-series anomalies, null values, and complex joins without breaking the pipeline.
Communication: Ability to explain complex data to stakeholders and justify architectural decisions based on both technical performance and business needs.
Education: Degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Mathematics, or a related field—or equivalent experience scaling a modern data stack.



We value, recognise and reward our people


A role with real scope, where your impact is visible, your salary is reviewed as we grow, and there are career progression opportunities as the company scales and internationalises.
Comprehensive health and life insurance through Laya Healthcare in Ireland and Aviva in the UK.
Flexible working with hybrid working as the default in Ireland, core hours of 10am to 4pm, remote options depending on role and a work abroad programme of up to 6 weeks.
25 days annual leave from day one, increasing by half a day per year of service, plus company days off, a half day before public holidays and an end of year shutdown.
Enhanced family leave, including 6 months fully paid maternity leave and 3 weeks paid paternity leave after 18 months’ service (and wedding leave!)
And on top of all of this, the usuals done well. A matching pension scheme that increases with service, paid sick leave, bike to work scheme, wellbeing supports, and an employee referral programme with rewards of up to €4,000 (or equivalent) per referral.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our Barbora Data Analytics team. If you’re passionate about turning data into actionable insights and want to work in a motivating, friendly, and ambitious environment, this could be the perfect opportunity for you!

What you'll be doing:


Building data models (AWS Redshift) and Power BI reports. We strive to automate as much as possible.
Delivering ad‑hoc deep dives (monthly/weekly/urgent) to explain data/anomalies and recommend concrete actions.
Examining sales trends, analyzing digital channel performance across the website and app, collaborating closely with the marketing and sales data analysts.
Measuring performance across Lithuania, Latvia, and Estonia by tracking core KPIs, assessing website and app improvements, evaluating the impact of marketing and other initiatives.
Using your research materials to identify the right opportunities for business growth and branding.
Owning end‑to‑end digital channel analytics: events tracking, funnel analytics, customer behaviour.
Communicating insights clearly, keeping reporting reproducible.


What you'll need to succeed:


2+ years of experience in business analytics. Experience with digital data, including website and app events, is a plus but not required.
Experience with BI tools (Power BI, Qlik, Tableau, Quicksight, etc.). Power BI is a plus.
Proficiency in SQL. Experience with Python, R, Matlab is a plus.
Excellent English (written/spoken).
Ownership and autonomy in a fast‑moving, result driven environment.
Collaborative and organized working style. We want to show you the ropes. It is great if we can learn from you as well.


What's in it for you:


Opportunity to accelerate your career by joining the #1 e-commerce grocery company in the Baltics. 🚀
Comprehensive coverage via private health insurance.
Access to Barbora.lt and partners’ discounts.
Your choice of hardware (Mac or Windows) to suit your work style.
Hybrid work model.
A high degree of collaboration and autonomy, motivated colleagues and access to cutting-edge technologies.
Fully equipped kitchen with coffee, cold drinks, and snacks.
Company events and team-buildings!


Salary:

Salary for this position ranges from 3500 EUR to 4200 EUR GROSS and depends on your experience.

Title: Duomenų analitikas (-ė)
Description:
„IKI“ – tai vieta, kur žmonės, nepriklausomai nuo to, ar dirba biure, parduotuvėse, gamyboje ar logistikoje, aistrą pirmauti verčia ambicingais pasiekimais! Pirmieji Lietuvoje pristatėme prancūziškus kruasanus ir bagetes, atidarėme autonominę parduotuvę, įdiegėme savitarnos kasas ir elektronines kainų etiketes. Šie žingsniai atspindi mūsų nuolatinį siekį būti žingsniu priekyje ir kurti ateities mažmeninę prekybą – inovatyvią bei tvarią. Jei ieškai dinamiškos, idėjoms atviros aplinkos, kurioje galėtum augti ir kurti naujoves, gali būti, kad darbas „IKI“ yra arčiau tavęs!

Šiame darbe tu:


Analizuosi duomenis įvairiuose projektuose (dalyvavimas projektų komandose, reikalingų duomenų išgavimas ir apdorojimas) bei verslo atvejų skaičiavimuose;
Dalyvausi rodiklių (KPI) stebėsenoje (nustatyti kasdienės veiklos ir projektų įgyvendinimo KPI rodiklius bei užtikrinti jų matavimą);
Teiksi pagalbą biudžeto sudarymo ir kontrolės procesuose;
Dalyvausi tiekimo grandinės parametrų nustatyme siekiant optimizuoti visuminius tiekimo grandinės kaštus.


Tikimės, kad tu:


Turi aukštąjį universitetinį išsilavinimą kiekybinėje srityje, tokioje kaip matematika, informatikos mokslai, programų inžinerija, ekonomika, fizika ar panašioje srityje.;
Turi 2+ metų darbo patirties duomenų analizės srityje;
Turi gerus MS Excel ir darbo su PowerBI įgūdžius;
Gali pasigirti stipriais analitiniais ir loginio mąstymo gebėjimais;
Turi programavimo kalbų įgūdžių (SQL, Python ir kt.) – būtų privalumas.


„IKI“ pasiūlymas tau:

Atlyginimas ir finansinė gerovė


atlyginimas: 2200–3400 €/mėn. (neatskaičius mokesčių, priklausomai nuo tavo patirties ir įgūdžių),
galėsi pigiau apsipirkti „IKI“ prekybos centruose,
turėsi nuolaidas „IKI“ asortimento naujienoms,
daugiau nei 300 MELP ir partnerių nuolaidų kone visiems gyvenimo atvejams.  


Darbo aplinka ir lankstumas


hibridinis darbo modelis,
lankstumas pasirenkant darbo valandas,
įkvėpimui – darbostogos iki 30 d. per metus.


Karjera ir tobulėjimas


karjeros galimybės (tiek Lietuvoje, tiek užsienyje),
profesinio tobulėjimo galimybės (tiek su kolegomis, tiek ir individualiai).


Sveikata ir emocinė gerovė


papildomas sveikatos draudimas, kurį galėsi rinktis net iš 3 planų,
nemokamos sveikatos patikros biure,
dalyvavimas „Walk15“, vidinėse badmintono, dviračių, žygių mėgėjų sporto grupėse,
streso valdymo ir kiti emocinės gerovės mokymai, galimybė gauti anonimines psichologo konsultacijas telefonu.     


Stipri bendruomenė


stiprybėmis grįsta kultūra, kurioje vertinamos visų darbuotojų unikalios savybės ir pasiekimai,
kolegas pagal laisvalaikio veiklas kviečiame burtis į „Hobby Spotlight” klubus,
darbuotojų fondas „VeIKIme drauge“,
esame aktyvūs bendruomenės nariai – savanoriaudami ir finansine parama prisidedame prie „Maisto banko“ ir kitų organizacijų.  


Inovacijos ir dinamiškumas


modernūs darbo įrankiai ir programos,
inovatyvūs sprendimai prekyboje, tvarumo ir kitose srityse,
mūsų komandos projektus ir pasiekimus kviečiame pažinti čia: Tvarumas | IKI. Arčiau Tavęs!


Skamba kaip darbas arčiau tavęs ir tavo tikslų?

Pasidalink savo CV arba susisiek su mūsų atrankų specialiste Dovile telefonu +37065696339 arba LinkedIn platformoje.

  • Dalyvausi tiekimo grandinės parametrų nustatyme siekiant optimizuoti visuminius tiekimo grandinės kaštus.

Tikimės, kad tu:

  • Turi aukštąjį universitetinį išsilavinimą kiekybinėje srityje, tokioje kaip matematika, informatikos mokslai, programų inžinerija, ekonomika, fizika ar panašioje srityje.;
  • Turi 2+ metų darbo patirties duomenų analizės srityje;
  • Turi gerus MS Excel ir darbo su PowerBI įgūdžius;
  • Gali pasigirti stipriais analitiniais ir loginio mąstymo gebėjimais;
  • Turi programavimo kalbų įgūdžių (SQL, Python ir kt.) – būtų privalumas.

„IKI“ pasiūlymas tau:

Atlyginimas ir finansinė gerovė

  • atlyginimas: 2200–3400 €/mėn. (neatskaičius mokesčių, priklausomai nuo tavo patirties ir įgūdžių),
  • galėsi pigiau apsipirkti „IKI“ prekybos centruose,
  • turėsi nuolaidas „IKI“ asortimento naujienoms,
  • daugiau nei 300 MELP ir partnerių nuolaidų kone visiems gyvenimo atvejams.  

Darbo aplinka ir lankstumas

  • hibridinis darbo modelis,
  • lankstumas pasirenkant darbo valandas,
  • įkvėpimui – darbostogos iki 30 d. per metus.

Karjera ir tobulėjimas

  • karjeros galimybės (tiek Lietuvoje, tiek užsienyje),
  • profesinio tobulėjimo galimybės (tiek su kolegomis, tiek ir individualiai).

Sveikata ir emocinė gerovė

  • papildomas sveikatos draudimas, kurį galėsi rinktis net iš 3 planų,
  • nemokamos sveikatos patikros biure,
  • dalyvavimas „Walk15“, vidinėse badmintono, dviračių, žygių mėgėjų sporto grupėse,
  • streso valdymo ir kiti emocinės gerovės mokymai, galimybė gauti anonimines psichologo konsultacijas telefonu.     

Stipri bendruomenė

  • stiprybėmis grįsta kultūra, kurioje vertinamos visų darbuotojų unikalios savybės ir pasiekimai,
  • kolegas pagal laisvalaikio veiklas kviečiame burtis į „Hobby Spotlight” klubus,
  • darbuotojų fondas „VeIKIme drauge“,
  • esame aktyvūs bendruomenės nariai – savanoriaudami ir finansine parama prisidedame prie „Maisto banko“ ir kitų organizacijų.  

Inovacijos ir dinamiškumas

  • modernūs darbo įrankiai ir programos,
  • inovatyvūs sprendimai prekyboje, tvarumo ir kitose srityse,
  • mūsų komandos projektus ir pasiekimus kviečiame pažinti čia: Tvarumas | IKI. Arčiau Tavęs!

Skamba kaip darbas arčiau tavęs ir tavo tikslų?

Pasidalink savo CV arba susisiek su mūsų atrankų specialiste Dovile telefonu +37065696339 arba LinkedIn platformoje.

Title: Data Analyst – Marketing & Campaign Analytics
Description:
If you need support in completing the application or if you require a different format of this document, please get in touch with at UKI.recruitment@tcs.com with the subject line: “Application Support Request”.




Careers at TCS: It means more

TCS is a purpose-led transformation company, built on belief. We do not just help businesses to transform through technology. We support them in making a meaningful difference to the people and communities they serve - our clients include some of the biggest brands in the UK and worldwide. For you, it means more to make an impact that matters, through challenging projects which demand ambitious innovation and thought leadership.

Work closely with the range of teams within the business to bring ideas to life.
Develop and maintain a unified strategy that aligns operational efficiency with marketing and growth objectives.
Work with customers and create high-impact customer experiences for our business.




The Role

We are seeking a Data Analyst to support Marketing and Distribution teams within the Financial Services sector, with a particular focus on Asset Management, Life Insurance, and Retirement. This role is hands‑on and delivery‑focused, supporting marketing campaigns through data extraction, audience segmentation, campaign list pulls, reporting, and performance analysis in a regulated environment.







Your responsibilities:

Support end to end marketing campaign delivery by providing accurate and timely data outputs, including:

-- Campaign list pulls and audience extracts

-- Segment creation and validation

-- Post campaign response and performance analysis

-- Ensuring all activity complies with data governance, and internal usage controls.




Use SQL and Python to extract, transform, and prepare data from enterprise data platforms to support campaign execution, targeting activities, and ongoing marketing analytics.
Work closely with marketing, CRM, and distribution teams to translate campaign briefs into clear data requirements, validating logic and outputs prior to campaign deployment.
Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports to support:

-- Campaign and channel performance tracking

-- Audience and segment analysis

-- Trend and insight reporting for marketing stakeholders

-- Reports should be clear, accurate, and suitable for non technical business users




Access and analyze data hosted on AWS and Azure platforms, working within established cloud, security, and compliance frameworks typical of Financial Services organizations.
Support the application of segmentation and propensity outputs produced by advanced analytics or data science teams, helping operationalize insights into campaign execution.
Assist with data inputs and reporting related to marketing technology and signal based platforms (e.g. 6Sense, Bombora, or similar), without responsibility for model development.
Perform basic data quality checks and validation, identifying inconsistencies or anomalies and working with data engineering or platform teams to resolve issues.
Maintain awareness of regulatory and organizational constraints associated with customer and advisor data, including appropriate handling of sensitive financial information




Your Profile

Essential skills/knowledge/experience:

This role is suited to candidates with approximately 3–6 years of experience in Data Analytics, Marketing Analytics, or a related discipline, ideally gained within Financial Services or another regulated industry.
The position will appeal to individuals who enjoy hands‑on campaign support, working closely with marketing teams, and turning business requirements into reliable, well‑governed data outputs, while continuing to build technical skills and industry knowledge.




Rewards & Benefits

TCS is consistently voted a Top Employer in the UK and globally. Our competitive salary packages feature pension, health care, life assurance, laptop and access to extensive training resources and discounts within the larger Tata network.

We offer health & wellness initiatives and sports events; we are the proud sponsor of the London Marathon and partner with our local communities in Ireland.




Diversity, Inclusion and Wellbeing

Tata Consultancy Services UK&I is committed to meeting the accessibility needs of all individuals in accordance with the Ireland Employment Equality Acts 1998-2011 (as amended) and the Equal Status Acts 2000-2012 (as amended).




We welcome and embrace diversity in race, nationality, ethnicity, disability, neurodiversity, gender identity, age, physical ability, gender reassignment, sexual orientation. We are a disability inclusive employer and encourage disabled people to apply for this role.

As a Disability Confident Employer, we offer an interview to applicants with disabilities or long-term conditions who meet the minimum criteria for the role. Please email us at UKI.recruitment@tcs.com if you would like to opt in.




If you are an applicant who needs any adjustments to the application process or interview, please contact us at UKI.recruitment@tcs.com with the subject line: “Adjustment Request” or email rebecca.mclelland@tcs.com to request an adjustment. We welcome requests prior to you completing the application and at any stage of the recruitment process.




Beware of Fraudulent offers

This is to notify you that TCS does not ask for any sort of payment or security deposit from candidates at any stage of the recruitment process. The firm never sends out job offers from free internet email services like Gmail, Yahoo Mail, and so on. TCS has not authorised any third-party company to collect money on their behalf. As a vigilant job seeker, beware of fraudulent recruitment activity and protect your interests! You can write to UKI.recruitment@tcs.com to report any fraudulent activity.




Due to the high volume of applications, we will be unable to contact each applicant individually on the status of their application. If you have not received a direct response within 30 days, then it should be deemed unsuccessful on this occasion.




Join us and do more of what matters. Apply online now.

Title: Risk Analyst
Description:
Role Overview

EEZE is expanding rapidly across multiple live casino studios and international offices and as a result, we are seeking a Risk Analyst to join our growing team. The Risk Analyst plays a key role in monitoring, assessing, and managing the integrity of live games and gaming environments. The primary goal of the role is to proactively identify and mitigate fraudulent activities affecting game integrity, ensuring prompt detection, analysis, and resolution.

Key Responsibilities


Investigate and assess game performance and player data for signs of advantage play, fraud, collusion and game malfunctions.
Conduct detailed analyses of player data and game metrics to identify risks and trends that may impact gaming integrity.
Provide support and assistance to Risk Analysts in assessing game performance and identifying potential risks.
Collaborate with GIRM Team and the Product team to assess game performance and provide insights for improvements.
Maintain familiarity with the games at a facility and with strategies and techniques used by players at such games.
Assist in implementing and maintaining systems and tools for continuous risk monitoring.
Provide reporting and documentation on identified risks and ongoing investigations.
Report any abnormal issues or situations to the Head of Gaming Integrity and Risk Management.
Perform additional duties as assigned by the manager in relation to the position.


Qualifications & Skills


Higher education or ongoing studies.
Previous experience in related field.
A mathematical or quantitative academic background, relevant professional experience, or demonstrable expertise in data analysis.
Excellent knowledge of English (both written and spoken).
Mathematical and analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data.
Solid understanding of gaming security, fraud prevention, and risk management practices.
Advanced knowledge of MS Office, especially Excel and Word.
Key personal traits: critical thinking, detail oriented and capable of working independently.


What’s in it for you?


Experience a dynamic and team-orientated work environment.
Opportunities for personal growth and learning.
An open, inclusive and supportive team where you will be valued, and your suggestions will be welcome.
24 days paid holiday per year. This is in addition to local public holidays.
Hybrid Working (4 days office/1 day home).
Life Assurance (2x annual salary).
Private Medical Insurance.
€400 annual wellness allowance.
Team Building Opportunities.
€80 Monthly lunch allowance
Parking (limited).
Local discounts and more.

Title: Staff Data Analyst
Description:
About Analog Devices

Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: ADI ) is a global semiconductor leader that bridges the physical and digital worlds to enable breakthroughs at the Intelligent Edge. ADI combines analog, digital, and software technologies into solutions that help drive advancements in digitized factories, mobility, and digital healthcare, combat climate change, and reliably connect humans and the world. With revenue of more than $9 billion in FY24 and approximately 24,000 people globally, ADI ensures today's innovators stay Ahead of What's Possible™. Learn more at www.analog.com and on LinkedIn and Twitter (X).

Staff Data Analyst, Engineering Analytics

Location: Limerick, Ireland

Overview

As a Staff Data Analyst on the Engineering Enablement team, you will own the development and evolution of analytics dashboards that surface insights on product development and engineering tool usage. These dashboards synthesize large amounts of engineering data from various sources and are broadly used by both engineering teams and executive leadership. You will rely on sound judgment, curiosity, and strong communication skills to translate ambiguous engineering workflows into well-defined metrics and clear, meaningful reports.

Responsibilities


Engage directly with engineering domain teams (design, verification, IP, AI etc.) to understand workflows, identify high-value data sources, and define key telemetry and productivity metrics.
Define data schemas, grain, and transformation logic needed for each data source, and communicate those requirements clearly to the Central Data team for implementation.
Design and maintain a Power BI semantic model (data model, relationships, DAX measures) that serves as a shared analytical foundation across multiple thin reports.
Build, publish, and iterate on Power BI dashboards and reports that deliver clear insights to engineering managers and leadership.
Collaborate with Central Data team to scope, prioritize, and validate pipeline work; serve as the functional owner of data correctness and metric definitions.
Document metric definitions, data lineage, and report logic to ensure institutional knowledge is durable and auditable.
Identify opportunities to expand impact — new data sources, new engineering personas, new use cases — and drive those through requirements definition and delivery.


Qualifications


8+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or a related field.
Degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Business Analytics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field.
Expert-level skills in Power BI or similar tool (Qlikview, Tableau, DataStudio, etc.), including data modeling, DAX, report design, workspace/deployment pipeline management.
Strong SQL skills; ability to write and review transformation logic even when not directly authoring pipelines.
Demonstrated experience working directly with non-technical stakeholders to define metrics and translate domain knowledge into data requirements.
Strong written and verbal communication skills; comfortable presenting findings and influencing priorities with engineering and executive leadership.
Experience owning an analytics product end-to-end — from requirements through delivery and ongoing maintenance.


Additional Qualifications (nice To Have)


Familiarity with consuming data from Snowflake, dbt, or similar modern data stack components.
Certifications in Power BI or related Microsoft data tools.
Experience with Python for data validation, exploration, or lightweight transformation.
Experience in the semiconductor industry or familiarity with EDA tools and flows.


For positions requiring access to technical data, Analog Devices, Inc. may have to obtain export licensing approval from the U.S. Department of Commerce - Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the U.S. Department of State - Directorate of Defense Trade Controls. As such, applicants for this position – except US Citizens, US Permanent Residents, and protected individuals as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3) – may have to go through an export licensing review process.

Analog Devices is an equal opportunity employer. We foster a culture where everyone has an opportunity to succeed regardless of their race, color, religion, age, ancestry, national origin, social or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, pregnancy, parental status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, military or veteran status, union membership, and political affiliation, or any other legally protected group.

Job Req Type: Experienced

Required Travel: Yes, 10% of the time

Shift Type: 1st Shift/Days

Title: Big Data Analyst
Description:
At ReversingLabs, our software supply chain security and threat intelligence solutions have become essential to advancing cybersecurity maturity around the globe. We're on a journey to expand adoption and accelerate growth by hiring top talent across the security industry.

Notable breaches such as SolarWinds, CircleCI, and 3CX have elevated software supply chain security as a top initiative across every organization developing or purchasing software. Only ReversingLabs delivers the software package analysis speed and intelligence needed to protect against this critical risk area.

Our vision is clear - arming every company with the insight to ensure every piece of software built or bought is safe and every threat detected. An innovative vision requires innovative talent to help forge this transformational journey at ReversingLabs. ReversingLabs is recognized by the cybersecurity industry as a leader in innovation. The $56M investment from Crosspoint Capital will support our growth and innovation efforts to build scalable, automated, and intelligent software security solutions.

We are looking for a passionate Big Data Analyst with a keen eye for detail and the ability to understand data. The successful candidate will turn data into information, information into insight, and insight into business decisions.

You will be responsible for developing and managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role, you need a keen eye for detail, some experience as a data analyst, and an understanding of popular data analysis tools and databases.

We offer file reputation records for over 50 billion samples, making our database the largest reputation database. Our customers receive the information through more than 50 REST APIs and Feeds. By performing hands-on data research, you will provide insights, uncover potential issues, and enhance the decision-making process. You will perform complex analysis on large data sets using appropriate data analysis techniques and tools with a strong focus on quality.

Responsibilities


Gathering data from various sources and then cleansing, organizing, processing, and analyzing it to extract valuable insights and information
Working with various database types to handle large amounts of data in structured, semi-structured, and unstructured form
Developing reports and dashboards (charts, tables, ...) with data analysis results to help different audiences make better decisions
Identifying, analyzing, and interpreting trends or patterns in complex data sets
Understanding sources and lineage of data to control the quality of our data sets
Collaborating with team members to prioritize business and information requirements
Ensure data quality by implementing data validation and cleansing processes
Performing routine analysis tasks to support day-to-day business
Applying statistical analysis methods for consumer data research and analysis purposes
Maintain and improve existing data pipelines
Closely collaborating with both the IT teams and customer-facing teams to accomplish company goals and prove value to our customers


Requirements


Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and disseminate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy
Experience with data visualization tools
Solid understanding of data warehousing concepts and ETL/ELT processes
Experience with some programming languages (Python, Java, ...)
Experience with databases and writing queries
Working proficiency in written and spoken English
Enthusiasm for teamwork, constant learning, and adapting to new circumstances


Desired Skills


Knowledge of statistics and experience with statistical packages for analyzing datasets
Proficiency in creating complex data models, database design, and data mining techniques
Expertise in using techniques like clustering, association rule mining, and customer segmentation to uncover valuable insights
Proven ability to understand and apply data insights to solve real-world business problems
Experience in creating concise and informative analysis reports and summaries that effectively communicate key findings and recommendations


Benefits


Hybrid work options (paid accommodation & transportation to Zagreb during onboarding for remote employees)
Flexible working hours
Generous compensation and a bonus system based on annual performance
Hefty personal education budget and possibility to attend leading conferences and seminars in the field
Company library and possibility to order books of choice via Amazon
Permanent contract in a fast-growing global company with Fortune 500 & governmental agencies as clients
Challenging projects in a dynamic, collaborative team
Great career advancement opportunities - clear goals & internal promotions
Employee referral bonus program, EUR 1,060 net for junior position, EUR 2,123 net for mid to senior positions, and EUR 2,654 net for principal/managerial positions
Multisport card, annual health checkup, newborn child allowance, rent-cost, and 3rd pillar pension benefits
Wellness Weekends - quarterly, company-wide three day weekend, starting with a company paid Friday off for all employees
Fully covered car garage in Radnička for all employees

Title: Digital Analyst
Description:
Are you a true analytical generalist who can code, analyse, visualise, and present your findings?

In Swedbank you have the opportunity to:


Build, maintain and develop reports and dashboards for internal stakeholders;
Dig in data and help organisation make factual based decisions;
Get involved in testing, implementation, and automation of digital channels data collection
Consult project teams across Bank on various developments;
Help organisation implement new digital analytics tools, guide stakeholders as they use them, run trainings.



What is needed in this role:


Quick adaptation into a team of technical analysts working on data framework implementations using JavaScript, Adobe Analytics and Experience Platform;
Basic skills to develop and perform code modifications, testing and deployment where appropriate using IDE (IntelliJ, etc) and following version control usage principles in an Agile workstream;
Foundational knowledge of Big Query, SQL, Python, PowerBI, Looker;
Logical thinking and business-minded interpretation skills;
Exceptional data visualisation skills to transform complex data into simple narrative;
Strong communication skills and capability to inspire colleagues in English and in a local market language (EE/LV/LT).



With us, you can experience:


Personal and professional growth through self-leadership and continuous development.
Meaningful work that positively impacts our workplace, our customers, and society.
An open and collaborative culture that encourages cross-functional teamwork and provides networking opportunities.
A supportive and inclusive environment that promotes a balanced and sustainable work-life, with flexible working conditions when suitable for the role.
Benefits such as our share based reward program Eken, company pension plan, employee offer for banking products, health insurance.



" Join our team and...

help Swedbank deliver the best internet bank and app experience in Baltics." Henri Sepp, your future leader

We look forward to your application at 19.06.2026 the latest.

We may begin the selection during the application period, so we welcome your application as soon as possible.

If you are to be employed in Latvia, please note that the salary offered for this position ranges from 2,800–4,200 € gross i.e. before taxes. Read more here.

If you are to be employed in Lithuania, please note that the salary offered for this position ranges from 3,350–5,050 € gross i.e. before taxes. Read more here.

If you are to be employed in Estonia, please note that the salary offered for this position ranges from 3,150–4,750 € gross i.e. before taxes. Read more here!

We have made our choice regarding recruitment media and therefore kindly decline contact with ad sellers or sellers of other recruitment services.

Swedbank does not discriminate anybody based on gender, age, sexual orientation or sexual identity, ethnicity, religion or disability – everybody is welcome.
