Title: Data Lead (Growth & Analytics)
Description:
Mid-senior position | Part-time or Full-time | On-site @Ljubljana




About the role

We're building a real-world social platform. Matched meetups that get people out of their homes and turn strangers into friends. It works. Now we need someone to build the data infrastructure that helps us scale it.




As our Data Lead (Growth & Analytics) you'll own everything data: event tracking, pipelines, the dashboards that drive our decisions. If a metric matters to the business, you built the system that surfaces it.




We're an AI-first team - that includes BI. We're building AI-powered dashboards and we want someone who finds that exciting and has done this in the past.




You'll be one of our first 10 team members. Early enough to shape how we instrument, what we trust, and how decisions get made. If you've spent years in a SaaS or subscription business and you intuitively know which numbers lie and which ones matter, you'll fit right in.




It's fast. It's sometimes messy. The problems are real and the impact is direct.




You’ll be a great fit if you:

Work best without permission
Default to "how could I systemize this"
Had weird teenage hobbies
Spend your Sundays on side projects
Shipped something this week
Are good at chess, poker or Monopoly
Forget to grab lunch because you’re in the zone
Get the best ideas while showering
Have left a job because the pace was too slow
Are more motivated by winning than by being right
Find bureaucracy physically painful




What you’ll be doing

Data infrastructure

Own data architecture decisions and tooling selection across marketing, revenue, finance, product, and operations
Set up and maintain a data warehouse
Set up experimentation tooling
Ensure data accuracy, freshness, and consistency
Implement data validation, anomaly detection, and monitoring

Tracking

Build attribution tracking for Meta Ads, Google Ads, email and other channels
Measure reliable data at every step of the acquisition funnel
Diagnose and resolve tracking discrepancies across platforms
Work with other teams to implement and QA tracking changes

Data analysis

Translate data into clear, actionable recommendations for leadership
Build forecasting models for revenue, churn, and growth scenarios
Identify leading indicators and growth constraints
Support strategic decisions around pricing, roadmap, and scale strategy
Implement cohort dashboards, churn prediction tools, and LTV/CAC reporting
Segmentation by geographies, niches, channels, etc
User feedback analysis
Operational efficiency analysis

Experimentation

Set success metrics and guardrails for experiments
Run A/B tests on landing pages, onboarding funnels, pricing pages, upsells, etc
Evaluating A/B tests and suggesting new ones

Product & business insights

Analyze user behavior across onboarding, activation, engagement, and retention
Identify friction points and drop-offs in core user journeys
Partner with product, marketing, and ops to drive improvements




Requirements

Essential:

Fluent in English (written and spoken)
At least 3 years of relevant experience
Experience working with a subscription/SaaS business (familiar with metrics such as MRR, churn, cash conversion cycle,...)
Comfortable working with SQL, data modeling, BI, retention/LTV analysis, attribution, and forecasting.
AI-first mindset - ability to heavily rely on AI tools and build in such a way that we can keep leveraging AI in the future
Strong revenue strategy mindset: understands monetisation, pricing, payback logic, and CAC/LTV trade-offs.
Comfortable working in ambiguity, adapting to changes and wearing multiple hats
Ability to identify ideal software and integrate it into the company’s workflow
Attention to detail and the ability to work independently without constant oversight
A lot of drive, energy, and time.
Ability to work from our office in Ljubljana city center

Bonus points:

Previous startup experience (especially early-stage)
Experience building teams from scratch
You are based close to Ljubljana (or willing to move)




About us

We help people meet new friends through real-life events all around the world.




We’re building something ambitious. Small team. Global startup. Serious brains. Real responsibility. Fast growth. Mobile app / B2C SaaS.




We are past product-market fit and are now in fast-scaling mode.




If you want to ship fast, work hard, own systems end-to-end, and grow with the company - this is for you.




Founded by Jani Pravdič and Matic Jesenovec – entrepreneurs who've together built multiple businesses impacting hundreds of thousands of people and generating well over eight figures in revenue. We're now building something that we believe has the potential to grow much larger and have a greater impact than anything we’ve done before.




What’s in it for you

Working closely with the founders
Working in a high-performance team
It takes us hours instead of weeks to launch/implement something
We test things and we listen to the numbers, not who is the loudest in the room
Failure is part of the process; we accept it and learn from it
Opportunity to grow into a higher role
The chance to genuinely impact many thousands of lives globally
A front-row seat in an exciting, fast-growing start-up
Competitive salary
Performance bonuses




Our values

Strategic Boldness: We do what it takes to deliver faster, farther, and louder.
Agility > Perfection: We value action over polish, effectiveness over effort. Ship fast, refine later.
Scrappy & Curious: We test, learn, and adapt constantly. Resourceful beats resource-rich.
Ambition + Humility: We learn from the best, then push beyond. Inspiration fuels our innovation.
Long Game Thinking: We’re impatient with action, patient with results. Correct quickly, stay the course.




Next steps

We’re looking for someone to start as soon as possible. So if the role sounds exciting, please follow these steps:

Fill out the application form.
Our team carefully reviews every application, so the selection process may take some time.
The best candidates will receive an additional test assignment.
Those who perform best on this task will be invited to an extensive interview.




If you do not hear from us within 14 days of submitting your application, it means we have decided to continue the process with other candidates at this time.

Title: Datu analītiķis/-e
HTML:
Rimi Baltic piegādes tīkla un loģistikas departaments meklē analītikas profesionāli, kurš/-a novērtē izaicinājumus, spēj iedziļināties sarežģītās tēmās un panāk rezultātus, radot dažādas sarežģītības pakāpes analītiskos risinājumus. Kā piegādes tīkla Datu analītiķim/-ei Tev būs nozīmīga loma, esot vienam no enerģisku, radošu un uz rezultātiem orientētu cilvēku loka, līdzdarbojoties biznesam vērtību nesošu risinājumu radīšanā.

Ja Tev ir svarīgi:

  • Dinamisks un pieredzi bagātinošs darbs vienā no vadošajiem mazumtirdzniecības uzņēmumiem Baltijā;
  • Iespēja pielietot savas zināšanas, prasmes un kompetences daudzveidīgos uzdevumos, jaunu risinājumu radīšanā un uzlabojumu veikšanā;
  • Sava darba rezultātā sasniegt taustāmus, vērtību radošus risinājumus;
  • Profesionālās attīstības iespējas, pilnveidojot gan tehniskās, gan citas analītiskās prasmes;
  • Izbaudīt kompetentu un profesionālu kolēģu atbalstu draudzīgā darba vidē;
  • Plašas karjeras iespējas visā Rimi Baltic grupā;
  • Draudzīga darba vide un dažādi komandas pasākumi;
  • Elastīga darba vides izvēle, kombinējot darbu birojā ar darbu no mājām;
  • Konkurētspējīgs atalgojums, sociālās garantijas, veselības apdrošināšana, sporta zāle birojā, mūsdienīgs un ergonomisks birojs un citi darbinieku bonusi.

Tavā atbildībā ietilps:

  • Vajadzība detalizēti iedziļināties sarežģītās tēmās un datu struktūrās;
  • Iegūt datus no dažādiem datu avotiem un transformēt tos kolēģiem ērti lietojamos un saprotamos “front end” risinājumos;
  • Dažādas sarežģītības pakāpes risinājumu, atskaišu, rīku izstrāde un uzturēšana piegādes tīkla vajadzībām, lai nodrošinātu nepieciešamos biznesa lēmumus, atbalstītu efektīvus procesus un sasniegtu biznesa mērķus;
  • Datu pārvēršana biznesa ieskatos: iekšējo un ārējo datu analīze un interpretācija, lai sniegtu datos balstītas rekomendācijas potenciālajiem attīstības virzieniem;
  • Piedalīties liela mēroga Rimi projektos, pārstāvot piegādes tīkla analītisko kompetenci;
  • Atbalstīt un konsultēt vadītājus un kolēģus par datu izmantošanu un secinājumu veidošanu;
  • Apmācību vadīšana savas kompetences un pārvaldīto risinājumu ietvaros, lai nodrošinātu zināšanu pārnesi visā organizācijā;
  • Pastāvīgi uzlabot un palīdzēt ieviest jaunas analītiskās metodes organizācijā.

Tu esi, ko meklējam, ja Tev ir:

  • Jaudīgas tehniskās datu analītikas zināšanas un šī joma ir tas, kur saredzi savu tālāku attīstību;
  • Spēja saskatīt datu korelācijas un savstarpējās saistības;
  • Orientācija uz detaļām un spēja nodrošināt augstu precizitātes līmeni;
  • Padziļinātas MS Excel un datu apstrādes zināšanas;
  • Tev ir laba pieredze datu iegūšanā no relāciju datu bāzēm un labi pārvaldi SQL valodu;
  • Pārvaldi kādu no programmēšanas valodām (Python, R, Java vai citām);
  • Pieredze prasību definēšanā (requirement engineering);
  • Spēja strādāt dinamiskā vidē un pielāgoties pārmaiņām;
  • Pozitīva personība ar stratēģisku skatu, fokusējoties uz risinājumiem un rezultātiem;
  • Augstākā izglītība un vismaz 1 gada pieredze analītikā, biznesa inteliģencē vai līdzīgā sfērā;
  • Teicamas latviešu un angļu valodas zināšanas mutiski un rakstiski, lai sazinātos ar kolēģiem Baltijas līmenī.

Vispār super, ja Tev ir pieredze ar:

  • Analītikas un biznesa inteliģences rīkiem (Power BI / SAP Business Objects / SAP BW / SAP Data Warehouse Cloud / SAP Analytics Cloud vai citiem);
  • Darbu mazumtirdzniecības un/vai loģistikas nozarē datu analītikas sfērā.

Atalgojums no 2300 - 2900 EUR mēnesī pirms nodokļu nomaksas. Atalgojuma piedāvājums tiks izvērtēts, balstoties uz kandidāta kompetenču, pieredzes un prasmju novērtējumu atbilstoši amata prasībām.

Pateicamies par Tavu interesi mūsu vakancē!

SIA Rimi Baltic, Reģistrācijas numurs 40003592957

Rimi Latvia ir viens no vadošajiem pārtikas mazumtirdzniecības uzņēmumiem valstī. Šobrīd pircējiem visā Latvijā ir atvērti vairāk nekā 140 Rimi veikali. Rimi Latvia strādā vairāk kā 5 000 darbinieku.

Title: DATU ANALĪTIĶIS
Description:
Storent Europe ir viens no nedaudziem Latvijas uzņēmumiem, kas veiksmīgi paplašinājies gan Ziemeļeiropā, gan Amerikas Savienotajās Valstīs. Attīstot savu biznesu, aicinām pievienoties komandai datu analītiķi, kurš palīdzēs nodrošināt kvalitatīvu datu analīzi un sniegs biznesam būtiskus ieskatus starptautiskā mērogā.

Šī loma ir vērsta uz biznesa un finanšu datu analīzi, datu kvalitāti un lēmumu pieņemšanas atbalstu.

Mēs piedāvājam:

Karjeras iespējas

un pastāvīgu darba vietu stabilā, starptautiskā uzņēmumā

Spēcīgu komandu līdzās

apmācības un komandas atbalstu darbu veikšanā

Labumu paketi

sociālās garantijas, veselības apdrošināšana, sporta un izklaides pasākumi

Stabilu atalgojumu

2500 - 3500 EUR mēnesī atkarībā no pieredzes

Darbu ar lieliem datu apjomiem

un ar reālu iespēju veikt ietekmi uz biznesa procesiem un lēmumiem

Elastīgu darba režīmu

darbu hibrīda modelī ar iespēju pašam veidot savu darba dienu

Galvenie pienākumi:


Apkopot, analizēt un validēt datus no dažādām sistēmām (ERP, Microsoft Dynamics 365 u.c.)
Nodrošināt datu kvalitāti, identificēt neatbilstības un ierosināt uzlabojumus
Sagatavot regulāras un ad hoc atskaites par KPI, rentabilitāti un biznesa rezultātiem
Analizēt finanšu un operatīvos datus, salīdzināt tos ar budžetu un prognozēm
Izstrādāt un uzturēt datu modeļus un atskaites (Excel vidē un citos rīkos)
Sniegt rekomendācijas biznesa efektivitātes uzlabošanai, balstoties uz datu analīzi
Sadarboties ar finanšu, IT un citām biznesa komandām
Dokumentēt datu loģiku un struktūras


Prasības kandidātam:


Vismaz 3 gadu pieredze datu analīzē, finanšu analīzē (FP&A) vai līdzīgā jomā
Labas SQL zināšanas un pieredze darbā ar datiem no dažādām sistēmām
Ļoti labas Excel prasmes (datu analīze, modeļi, formulas)
Izpratne par datu struktūrām un biznesa datu loģiku
Zināšanas par datu verifikācijas metodēm un prasme pārbaudīt datu pareizību
Spēja analizēt lielus datu apjomus un identificēt būtiskāko
Labas angļu valodas zināšanas, kas nepieciešamas programmu lietošanai un saziņai ar kolēģiem grupas līmenī


Prasmes un kompetences:


Spēcīga analītiskā domāšana un problēmu risināšanas prasmes
Spēja interpretēt datus un pārvērst tos skaidros secinājumos
Spēja vienkārši izskaidrot sarežģītus datus biznesa lietotājiem
Augsta precizitāte un atbildība darbā ar datiem
Labas komunikācijas un sadarbības prasmes


Par priekšrocību tiks uzskatīta:


Pieredze darbā ar ERP vai finanšu sistēmām
Pieredze ar Power BI, QlikView vai līdzīgiem rīkiem
Pieredze starptautiskā vidē
Izpratne par budžetēšanu un finanšu analīzi


Vēlies saņemt vairāk info?

Sazinies ar mūsu personāla atlases speciālisti.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Strong SQL Skills Required




The Data Analyst will report to the Director Data Intelligence and will use internal and external data to provide actionable insights. This individual will work closely with data scientists, data engineers, software developers and SME’s within the business to ensure the delivery of accurate analytics and reporting.




The Data Analyst will derive understanding and insights via detailed analytics that will lead to increased operational efficiency and effectiveness by enabling proactive data-driven decisions across the organization.




Key Responsibilities:

Dissect and solve fleet related business problems using analytical and quantitative approaches to identify patterns and correlations in large, complex datasets.
You will provide data backed solutions to questions such as the below:
What is the optimal length of time to hold a vehicle?
How can we reduce vehicle out of service time?
What is the driving factor behind increased vehicle damage rates?
Provide actionable insight to decision-makers to influence strategy and performance.
Integrate and curate data from a wide range of internal and external sources to develop detailed reports.
Use data visualization tools to explain your results simply and succinctly to technical, non-technical, and senior audiences.




Required Education

A bachelor's degree or higher in a quantitative discipline such as statistics, mathematics, physics, engineering or computer science.




Required Skills and Experience

Minimum of 3 years hands-on experience in a data analyst role.
Minimum of 2 years hands on experience with Databricks (or equivalent).
Strong capabilities in SQL and experience working with structured and unstructured data.
Strong problem solving and critical thinking skills with a proven record for identifying and diagnosing problems, and solving complex problems with simple, logical solutions.
Strong experience with Data Visualization tools such as Tableau
Outstanding verbal, written, presentation, facilitation, and interaction skills, including
[Nice to Have] Python scripting skills.







Hertz is an equal opportunity employer, all applicants must have the legal right to work in Ireland (Valid EU Passport / Relevant Visa).

Title: Digital Analyst
Description:
Are you a true analytical generalist who can code, analyse, visualise, and present your findings?

In Swedbank you have the opportunity to:


Build, maintain and develop reports and dashboards for internal stakeholders;
Dig in data and help organisation make factual based decisions;
Get involved in testing, implementation, and automation of digital channels data collection
Consult project teams across Bank on various developments;
Help organisation implement new digital analytics tools, guide stakeholders as they use them, run trainings.



What is needed in this role:


Quick adaptation into a team of technical analysts working on data framework implementations using JavaScript, Adobe Analytics and Experience Platform;
Basic skills to develop and perform code modifications, testing and deployment where appropriate using IDE (IntelliJ, etc) and following version control usage principles in an Agile workstream;
Foundational knowledge of Big Query, SQL, Python, PowerBI, Looker;
Logical thinking and business-minded interpretation skills;
Exceptional data visualisation skills to transform complex data into simple narrative;
Strong communication skills and capability to inspire colleagues in English and in a local market language (EE/LV/LT).



With us, you can experience:


Personal and professional growth through self-leadership and continuous development.
Meaningful work that positively impacts our workplace, our customers, and society.
An open and collaborative culture that encourages cross-functional teamwork and provides networking opportunities.
A supportive and inclusive environment that promotes a balanced and sustainable work-life, with flexible working conditions when suitable for the role.
Benefits such as our share based reward program Eken, company pension plan, employee offer for banking products, health insurance.



" Join our team and...

help Swedbank deliver the best internet bank and app experience in Baltics." Henri Sepp, your future leader

We look forward to your application at 19.06.2026 the latest.

We may begin the selection during the application period, so we welcome your application as soon as possible.

If you are to be employed in Latvia, please note that the salary offered for this position ranges from 2,800–4,200 € gross i.e. before taxes. Read more here.

If you are to be employed in Lithuania, please note that the salary offered for this position ranges from 3,350–5,050 € gross i.e. before taxes. Read more here.

If you are to be employed in Estonia, please note that the salary offered for this position ranges from 3,150–4,750 € gross i.e. before taxes. Read more here!

We have made our choice regarding recruitment media and therefore kindly decline contact with ad sellers or sellers of other recruitment services.

Swedbank does not discriminate anybody based on gender, age, sexual orientation or sexual identity, ethnicity, religion or disability – everybody is welcome.

Title: Web BI Analyst / VBET
Description:
About the role

Gathering, analysing, and interpreting data to help the organization make informed business decisions. Taking ownership of complex data projects, designing advanced BI solutions, and actively shaping business strategies with data-driven insights.




Responsibilities

● Analyze and interpret complex datasets to identify trends, opportunities, and areas for improvement.

● Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using BI tools (e.g., Metabase).

● Collaborate with stakeholders to gather business requirements and deliver tailored BI solutions.

● Monitor and maintain data quality, ensuring accuracy and consistency across systems.

● Develop and manage data pipelines and workflows to streamline data integration and processing.

● Track key performance indicators (KPIs) and provide actionable recommendations to improve business performance.

● Support senior analysts and data engineers on larger projects, contributing to strategic initiatives.




Qualifications

● University degree in Math, Statistics, Computer Science, Economics or a related fields;

● At least two years of experience in a relevant position (iGaming, online products, e-commerce);

● Strong analytical skills and ability to analyze large datasets, knowledge in data discovering methodology;

● Understanding of metrics related to online business (web, product, CRM, general financial);

● Experience analyzing CRM campaigns and player retention metrics;

● Understanding of customer segmentation, engagement strategies, and promotional

analytics.

● SQL upper-intermediate skills;

● Experience with Tableau and other BI instruments;

● Upper-Intermediate English (spoken and written);

● Proactive communication and presentation skills to communicate with team

members, external data providers, C level;

● Ability to work independently and as a part of a team.




Soft skills:

● Strong communication skills, clear and adaptable;

● Solution-oriented, proactive problem solver, builds partnerships, fosters teamwork;

● Confident, persuasive yet collaborative.




Competences

● Analytical Thinking: Ability to derive meaningful insights from complex data.

● Attention to Detail: Commitment to accuracy and precision in data work.

● Collaboration: Strong interpersonal skills to work effectively with

cross-functional teams.

● Adaptability: Comfortable navigating fast-paced environments and evolving

priorities.

● Business Acumen: Understanding of business operations and how data

informs strategic decisions.

● Proactivity: Initiative to identify and act on opportunities for improvement.




Sounds interesting? Apply and let's get to know each other better.

Title: Intern, Business Intelligence & Operations - Fall Semester 2026
Description:
Boston Celtics Job Posting




 




Job Title: Business Intelligence & Operations Intern




Department: Business Intelligence & Operations




Supervisor: VP, Business Strategy and Operations




Job Classification: Non-Exempt




Job Type: Part-Time, 29 Hours per Week (September - December)




















Summary:




 




The mission of the Boston Celtics internship program is to educate its participants in all aspects of operating a professional sports organization. Through observation and practical experience, interns learn how to function in a professional environment that requires a high energy level, the ability to perform under pressure and the utilization of essential time management skills. Through these developments, the Boston Celtics strive to successfully prepare their interns to compete for positions in the professional sports industry. 












 Responsibilities: 




 





Assist in the development of SQL scripts used to build Tableau server data sources and validate data accuracy


Build and validate data visualizations in Tableau, summarizing data in a way that is straightforward and informative


Assist with data accuracy audits in CRM system, including day-to-day maintenance of data


Update and analyze leads in email marketing database


Assist in tagging and categorization of social media posts


Assist Ticket Operations in a variety of Archtics related ticketing projects such as pricing structure, data maintenance, financial monitoring, and third-party integrations


Process and report on ticket donations for Shamrock Dreams program to Community Engagement


Field inbound fan questions related to tickets and troubleshoot technical issues


Support various fan engagement programs and solving in-person ticketing issues at the TD Garden Box Office during home games


Research and present best practices relating to business analytics and operations in the sports industry


Other collaborative duties as assigned by the Director of Business Intelligence













Qualifications: 













Strong critical thinking, a growth mindset, an deductive problem-solving


Detail-oriented and thoughtful approaches to ensure accuracy and understanding of projects


Interest in developing ideas and projects from the ground up and being a self-starter


Enthusiasm to understand data, database management, and reporting and analytics


Ability to communicate and work with multiple departments and senior management













Learning Opportunities:













How to build data visualizations in Tableau, including how to tell stories and how to use data to drive insights to key stakeholders


Writing effective and efficient SQL scripts used to pull, validate, and manipulate data


A macro-level understanding of the role data plays in an organization, including how data flows out of and into systems used for ticketing, CRM, and email marketing


The ins and outs of a CRM system that manages hundreds of thousands of customer records


Foundation training and regular use of Ticketmaster’s Archtics ticketing system, the most commonly used ticketing system in professional sports













Requirements:













Must be a junior or senior undergraduate student or graduate student


Academic Credit


Ability to work scheduled home games and events













The expected range of annual salary/hourly rate and potential commission, if applicable, is included below. Where an employee or prospective employee is paid within this/these range(s) will depend on, among other factors, actual ranges for current/former employees in the subject position, market and industry considerations, budgetary considerations, tenure, performance, and standing with the company (applicable to current employees), as well as the employee’s/applicant’s background, pertinent experience, and qualifications. 




Hourly Rate Range: $15.00 




Banner Seventeen, LLC is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. All employees and applicants for employment are afforded equal opportunity in every area of hiring and employment without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy (and pregnancy related conditions), gender identity or expression, citizenship status, national origin, ancestry, sexual orientation, age, physical or mental disability, genetic disposition or carrier status, marital status, familial status, status as a victim of domestic violence, military or veteran status, or any other category protected under applicable federal, state, or local law.




 




About Company: 




Throughout the Boston Celtics’ storied history, they have long stood for equality and respect, including drafting the first African American player, hiring the first African American coach, and playing the first all-black starting five. The Celtics have won a record 18 NBA Championships spanning six decades, including eight in a row from 1959-1966, and winning their most recent in 2024. In addition, 51 former Celtics players, management, coaches, or staff have been inducted into the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame. The team has been operating under local ownership since 2002.

Title: SQL Data Analyst
Description:
Department: Finance

Employment Type: Permanent

Location: Burlington, Massachusetts

Compensation: $100,000 - $110,000 / year

Description

Highwire, a Veriforce company, is the Contractor Success Platform for asset owners, general contractors, and facilities managers. The world’s most admired organizations use Highwire to ensure their contractors and subcontractors deliver great work, on budget and on schedule, by collaborating with them to dynamically mitigate safety, financial, quality, and sustainability risks during capital projects and facilities maintenance.

To support our growth, we’re looking for a technically minded, detail-obsessed Data Analyst to own the integrity of our internal data. This role is perfect for someone who’s a SQL expert and eager to work directly with complex datasets.

Your mission is to ensure the data that powers Highwire — from customer renewal rates and campaign performance to financial reporting — is accurate, complete, and decision-ready. You’ll take ownership of the end-to-end accuracy of key datasets, using SQL to investigate anomalies, validate transformations, and deliver reliable data to teams across Product, Engineering, Finance, and other business functions. Your work will enable the entire company to move quickly and make informed decisions based on trustworthy data.

What You'll Do


Write and improve SQL queries that power reporting and analysis. You’ll support business teams by extracting and transforming data, building reusable queries, and collaborating with engineering to enhance data models and pipelines.
Deliver clean, trusted data. You’ll ensure that the data used across Finance, Product, Sales, and Engineering is accurate and reliable, enabling fast and confident decision-making and reducing rework and second-guessing.
Identify and fix root-cause issues. You’ll proactively identify bugs, inconsistencies, or oddities in the data that powers our reports, and collaborate with Engineering and Product to determine the root cause of the issue.
Enable real-time reporting. Department heads will rely on you for recurring and ad-hoc reports that are both timely and accurate. You’ll build reports that scale as our teams grow.
Continuously improve data quality. You won’t wait for others to flag data issues. Instead, you’ll take initiative to audit, investigate, and improve datasets before problems reach stakeholders.


Who You Are


SQL Expert. You’re comfortable querying relational databases, navigating schemas, writing joins, and thinking in data logic.
Detail-oriented. You notice inconsistencies that others miss and take pride in spotting the one broken row in a dataset of thousands.
Owner’s mindset. You feel a strong sense of responsibility for the quality of your work and the integrity of the data others rely on. If it’s broken, you’ll fix it.
Adaptable. You’re comfortable working with imperfect or incomplete data, and you adjust quickly to changing product requirements or business needs.
Analytical. You excel at analyzing large datasets, identifying patterns and anomalies, and transforming complex or ambiguous information into actionable insights.
Proactive collaborator. You don’t wait for someone else to raise a flag; you pull in the right people to get the right answer, fast.


This is an ideal role for someone who is confident in writing SQL to explore, transform, and troubleshoot data in a relational database. You might come from a background in data analysis, analytics engineering, operational support, or even Excel/Sheets-heavy reporting work, but the key is that you’ve used SQL hands-on, and you’re excited to deepen that skill and partner closely with leaders across our organization to ensure our data is accurate, accessible, and trustworthy.

Attention to detail is a critical competency for success in this Data Analyst role. To help us identify candidates who read thoroughly, you’ll see a mystery question during the application process. The correct answer is: attention to detail.

What You’ll Get In Return

Our benefits go well beyond the basics. To support your work-life integration, we built a benefits program that takes care of the whole person, including physical, mental, financial, and emotional health.


Medical, Dental, & Vision Insurance. We offer health, dental, and vision insurance to all full-time employees. Best part? You’ll be eligible on day one.
401k. We offer a 401k and a 3% company contribution (even better than a match) to help you get ready to retire no matter how far off that seems.
Parental leave. We take care of new parents by offering paid time off for births, adoptions, and fostering.
Paid Time Off. Outstanding time off benefits.
Mentorship. Our office-based culture fosters meaningful learning, growth, and mentorship for our team.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking to hire a data analyst to join our data team. You will take responsibility for managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role you need a very fine eye for detail, experience as a data analyst, and a deep understanding of the popular data analysis tools and databases. Data Analyst Responsibilities: Managing master data, including creation, updates, and deletion. Managing users and user roles. Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Our client is seeking an experienced data analyst to support our Digital Transformation program under the Workday Data workstream.

This is engagement is focused on data analysis, cleansing, profiling, and definition work.

We are looking for someone with a blend of hands-on data skills, strong business acumen, and the ability to engage effectively with cross-functional stakeholders across IT, Data, and Business teams.

Location: Boston area - ability to get to the Boston office when needed.




Keys to this role:

Perform data analysis, cleansing, and mapping across source systems to support rationalization and data cleansing efforts
Conduct data quality analysis on transactional and master data to identify critical data improvement opportunities
Facilitate working sessions with business and technical stakeholders to inform data analysis work
Support content creation for data governance documentation and working group meetings
Propose and apply approved AI tools and methodologies to accelerate data conversion and analysis efforts

Must Haves:

Strong business analysis and data analysis capabilities
Demonstrated experience with data quality, cleansing, mapping, and governance processes
Advanced SQL skills
Proficiency with Google Big Query and Looker preferred
Comfortable working with AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, and/or Claude) to perform traditional data analysis responsibilities
Comfort operating in a collaborative, cross-functional environment with both technical and non-technical audiences

Strong Nice to Haves:

Prior experience with Workday Finance and HCM modules
Experience working with financial data; chart of accounts, trial balances, and p&l data preferred

Title: QMS and Reporting Specialist
Description:
R&D Partners is seeking to hire a QMS and Reporting Specialist in Cambridge, MA.




Your main responsibilities as a QMS and Reporting Specialist:

Support implementation of a deviation investigator training pilot program, incorporating adult learning principles and hands-on application.
Develop training materials (case studies, templates, guidance tools) to improve investigation quality and consistency.
Support execution and delivery of Quality scorecards, dashboards, and KPI frameworks.
Perform data aggregation, trending, and root-cause-oriented analysis across deviations, CAPAs, and change controls.
Assist with process mapping, gap assessments, and continuous improvement initiatives across QMS processes.




What we are looking for in a QMS and Reporting Specialist:

Bachelor's degree in Life Sciences, Engineering, or related discipline.

3-5 years of experience in GxP Quality Systems, QA operations, or QMS program support.

Strong understanding of GMP, GLP, GCP regulations and ICH guidelines
Demonstrated experience with Quality Management System processes
Demonstrated experience with Quality metrics, dashboards, and KPI development
Demonstrated experience with Data analysis and trending (Excel, Power BI, or equivalent tools)

Title: Power BI Data Analyst
Description:
Contract 5 months to start could be temp to perm




This position is responsible for providing support in making strategic and tactical data-related decisions by analyzing, manipulating, tracking, internally managing, and reporting post-market surveillance data and leading and supporting post-market projects through a methodical approach of planning, executing, and tracking project deliverables from start to finish.




About the Role




You will work collaboratively with the Global Product Monitoring leadership team and other functions as needed to address key safety, quality, and compliance questions using the appropriate tools/skills to derive the best answers, to help drive required actions, and ensure project progress and success.




Responsibilities




Design and develop interactive, user-friendly dashboards within Power BI to visualize and explore data patterns, enabling stakeholders to derive actionable insights.
Optimize the process for collecting and analyzing data across multiple systems to provide scheduled and ad hoc analysis and reporting to stakeholders.
Develop and maintain reports and dashboards.
Extract data and perform detailed data analysis.
Produce and deliver reports on a routine basis to support the company’s operational initiatives.




Qualifications




Bachelor's Level Degree




Required Skills




At least 3+ years of demonstrated experience in post-market surveillance, quality assurance, regulatory compliance, data analysis/analytics or data reporting, and/or project management in the medical device and/or life sciences industry.
Proficiency in Power BI, including data modeling, report creation, and sharing insights.
Strong organizational skills.
Ability to analyze problems and develop solutions.




Preferred Skills




Advanced MS Excel skills.
Familiarity with Spotfire.
Excellent writing skills.
Excellent influencing and negotiating skills.
Ability to rapidly learn and take advantage of new concepts, business models, and technologies.
Proficiency with Medical Device regulations, particularly as they relate to post-market surveillance, vigilance, complaint handling, and risk management.




Pay range and compensation package




60 - 65 hr Could be temp to perm. You must be a GreenCard or US Citizen

No OPT or Visa consideration




Equal Opportunity Statement







We are committed to diversity and inclusivity.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
About The Role

The role bridges the gap between complex engineering pipelines and commercial strategy, translating raw database schemas into actionable business intelligence. The successful candidate will own the semantic layer, design core performance dashboards, and deliver deep-dive analyses that influence executive-level product and operational decisions.

Working closely with data engineers and product managers, this position ensures data integrity across business units and democratizes access to data. This is an opportunity to build robust analytics infrastructure and establish quantitative frameworks for a rapidly scaling business.

Key Responsibilities


Design, build, and maintain production-grade dashboards in Tableau or Looker to track key performance indicators across product and operations teams
Write complex, optimized SQL queries to extract, transform, and analyze large datasets from Snowflake or BigQuery data warehouses
Build and maintain dbt (data build tool) models to define clean, version-controlled business logic within the centralized data warehouse
Partner with product and engineering teams to define event tracking schemas and ensure accurate instrumentation of new features
Conduct ad-hoc diagnostic analyses on user retention, acquisition funnels, and unit economics to identify growth opportunities
Present structured data findings and strategic recommendations to non-technical business stakeholders and department heads


What We Are Looking For


2-4 years of experience as a business data analyst, analytics engineer, or in a highly quantitative business intelligence role
Advanced SQL skills, with deep knowledge of window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and database schema design
Hands-on experience with modern BI tools (Looker/LookML, Tableau, or PowerBI) and data transformation frameworks like dbt
Strong proficiency in Python or R for statistical analysis, data manipulation, and automated reporting tasks
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or a highly quantitative field
Bonus: Experience with web analytics platforms (Amplitude, Mixpanel, Segment) or managing data pipelines with Airflow

Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
PMA’s Risk Control Department is seeking a self-motivated, solution-oriented Data Analyst who enjoys building the data models, tools, systems, and processes necessary to enhance and streamline the way data is utilized and processed currently.

As a member of our Risk Control Department, you will have the opportunity to positively impact efficiency and profitability by designing, and re-designing the way data is extracted, transformed, loaded. You will perform assignments, tasks or activities that directly and indirectly contribute to the fulfillment of the corporation's and department's overall business objectives.

Responsibilities


Collect, evaluate, run, and design statistical data/reports on a monthly and ad hoc basis.
Prepare statistical and narrative reports and recommendations and participate in the analysis and interpretation of data as appropriate.
Support various project initiatives
Develop, maintain, and ensure quality control of data sources, queries, models, and results.
Run existing analytical reports and assist with defining and designing new reports to meet business needs.
Utilize data visualization tools to effectively illustrate data trends and patterns.
Participate in the development, implementation, and enhancement of data models and data warehouse tables to ensure that business needs are met.
Develop and implement data query and mining techniques that optimize statistical efficiency and data quality. This may include cleansing and normalizing of data or development of internal control reporting to ensure data validity going forward.
Assists in the application and implementation procedures of data standards and guidelines to ensure access to and integrity of data sets.
Identify, troubleshoot, and remediate issues that are produced from anomalies in the data.
Develop self-service reporting and informational tools for end users through defining business requirements, application and/or query design, testing, solutions implementation and end user training.
Assist in the testing and implementation of new or upgraded software.
Work closely with management and other department heads to prioritize business and information needs.
Provide data consulting in support of business and information technology initiatives to stakeholders to improve database systems.
Assist in the development of a system by which other analysts may be effectively educated on the various data analyst techniques employed.
Collaborate with both internal and external stakeholders to clearly define and document information/data requirements.
Support compliance and audit requirements
Demonstrate commitment to Company's Code of Business Conduct and Ethics, and apply knowledge of compliance policies and procedures, standards and laws applicable to job responsibilities in the performance of work


Requirements


BS in Mathematics, Economics, Business, Risk Management, Computer Science, Information Management, Statistics or related fields
Experience as a data analyst in commercial casualty insurance industry, with emphasis in workers compensation preferred
Adept at query development, report writing and presenting findings
Understanding of data modeling and data cleansing techniques
Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and communicate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy
Excellent visual design sense regarding clear and accurate presentation of data
Ability to interact with business and technology experts to help determine the technical feasibility of requirements
Ability to effectively communicate technical concepts to both technical and non-technical audiences
Ability to organize and balance multiple concurrent timelines and tasks to meet deadlines
Superb business process analysis and interpersonal skills
Strong knowledge of tools such as Excel, Access, Power BI, Visual Basic, database development
Working familiarity with tools such as QlikSense, Tableau, Hyperion, Access, SharePoint, MicroStrategy, SQL, R and Python preferred

Title: Data Analyst - Front Office - Customer Data
Description:
For this U.S. based position, the expected compensation range is $101,600 - $152,400 per year, which includes base pay and short-term incentive.

The compensation range for this full-time position applies to candidates located within the United States. Our salary ranges are determined by reviewing roles of similar responsibility and level. Within the salary range, individual pay is determined by several factors including performance, knowledge, job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Schneider Electric also offers a comprehensive benefits package to support our employees, inclusive of medical (with member reward points), dental, vision, and basic life insurance, Benefit Bucks (credits to apply towards your benefits) flexible work arrangements, paid family leaves, 401(k) + match, well-being and recognition (including service anniversary) programs, 12 holidays per year,15 days of paid time off per year (pro-rated in the first year of employment based on start date), opportunity to purchase company stock (eligibility depends on start date), and military leave benefits.

You must submit an online application to be considered for the position. The Company will accept applications on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

If you believe this job posting is not compliant with applicable state pay transparency laws in the U.S., please notify the Company as soon as possible upon discovery by completing this form Job Posting Compliance Form.

Join the Titan Data Transformation team supporting Front Office (ART 1) initiatives focused on customer data. This role is responsible for enabling the successful migration and transformation of customer master data into SAP S/4 HANA, ensuring alignment across sales, distribution, and commercial processes.

Key responsibilities include:


Working in an agile environment with business and technical team to identifying data migration requirements and dependencies.
Responsible for understanding and executing data mapping and data migrations with business and functional analysts.
Work with the business on data clean up
Work with data conversion and data load teams to drive end-to-end migration activities.
Assist in the development of data quality and readiness checks and identify solution changes which could impact checks.
Trouble shoot data extraction, conversion, and load errors
Validate accuracy of migrated data to expected outcomes
Assist with the development of lifecycle processes


Additional Responsibilities:


Support migration and governance of customer master data across sales and distribution processes
Partner with commercial and sales teams to define customer data requirements and resolve data gaps
Validate customer hierarchies, account structures, and sales area data for S/4 readiness
Identify and remediate inconsistencies impacting order processing and customer lifecycle data


Primary Skills/Qualifications:


Master data management and governance experience
5+ years SE manufacturing process knowledge or working in a manufacturing environment which applies data governance principles
3+ years of hands-on experience working with data, data tables and ETL processes
3+ years of hands-on experience working in the SAP environment
Excellent at solving problems and working through complex analytics
Candidate is proactive, collaborative, and persistent
Organized, detail oriented and rigorous
Advanced Excel skills
Intermediate SQL proficiency


Travel Requirement:

This role may require travel up to approximately 50–75% based on project phase and deployment needs.

Passport Requirement:

A valid passport is required as this role may support cross-border collaboration and deployment activities

Title: Associate Analyst, Insights, Data & Intelligence
Description:
Who We Are

Burson, part of WPP, is the global communications leader built to create value for clients through reputation. With highly specialized teams, industry-leading technologies and breakthrough creative, we help brands and businesses redefine reputation as a competitive advantage so they can lead today and into the future. When you work at Burson, you are part of a global community of lifelong learners who thrive at the edge of innovation.

WPP (LSE/NYSE: WPP) is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit WPP.com

For more information visit bursonglobal.com and follow us on LinkedIn and Instagram.

More About The Role

The Business Insights, Data & Intelligence (BIDI) team sits at the heart of this work—turning data into strategic insight that drives storytelling, campaign performance, and business outcomes.

Due to our expanding client roster and scopes, Burson is seeking to hire a group of entry-level Associate Analysts to join our best-in-class Business Insights, Data & Intelligence (BIDI) team, supporting some of the world’s most recognizable global brands such as The Coca-Cola Company, Verizon and Google. This opportunity is ideal for a recent graduate with internship experience in digital media, marketing, communications, or analytics who is excited to explore the intersection of social media, consumer behavior/social listening, and data-driven storytelling.

As part of a highly collaborative team, you’ll gain hands-on experience uncovering cultural insights, monitoring online conversations, and helping brands make informed decisions through social and digital intelligence. This is a unique opportunity to launch a career in analytics at one of the world’s leading global communications agencies while working across high-profile client accounts.

This role can be based in Atlanta, the New York metro area, or Los Angeles, with a preference for Atlanta-based candidates.

Relocation assistance will be available for candidates open to relocating to Atlanta.

What You'll Do


Support social listening, media monitoring, and reporting initiatives across a portfolio of global brands.
Track cultural moments, emerging conversations, competitor activity, and consumer sentiment across social and digital platforms.
Learn and utilize industry-leading tools such as Brandwatch, Sprinklr, and NewsWhip to uncover actionable insights tied to brand reputation, campaign performance, sustainability, and corporate communications initiatives.
Translate complex social and digital data into clear, compelling narratives and presentations for internal teams and clients.
Assist in developing recurring reports, dashboards, and performance analyses to measure brand health and campaign impact.
Monitor online conversations and media trends surrounding brand, industry, and corporate topics in real time.
Partner with cross-functional teams across strategy, creative, media, and communications in a fast-paced agency environment.
Gain exposure to both agency operations and client-facing work while developing analytical and storytelling skills.


Experience That Contributes To Success


Bachelor’s degree in communications, marketing, journalism, public relations, data analytics, business, or a related field.
Internship or hands-on experience in PR, marketing, social media, analytics, communications, or digital strategy preferred.
Strong curiosity about social media trends, consumer behavior, internet culture, and data-driven insights.
Excellent written, verbal, and presentation skills with the ability to synthesize information clearly and concisely.
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail, with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint; familiarity with pivot tables, Boolean search logic, dashboards, or data visualization tools is a plus.
Comfortable learning new technologies and navigating a dynamic, fast-paced environment.
Collaborative mindset with a willingness to learn, contribute ideas, and work across teams.
Exposure to social listening or analytics platforms such as Brandwatch, Sprinklr, Talkwalker, or Meltwater is a plus.
Additional language fluency in Spanish, Portuguese, or French is a bonus.


Burson is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

Individual compensation varies based on job-related factors, including location, business needs, level of responsibility, experience, and qualifications. We offer a competitive benefits package; click here for more details: https://mybenefits.wpp.com/public/welcome.

The base salary for this position at the time of this posting may range between:

$35,000—$75,000 USD

You Belong At Burson

Our vision is for Burson to be the leading ‘academy company’ for creative communications professionals and emerging leaders. We provide a dynamic environment where ambitious colleagues come to elevate their skills, expand their networks, and ultimately, produce the best solutions for today’s leading brands. Our commitment to your growth is reflected in our robust benefits and people programs, which include professional development opportunities, mentorship programs with industry leaders, a vibrant and inclusive company culture fostering collaboration and innovation and packages designed to reward and retain top talent. We invest in our people because we believe their success is our success.

At Burson, we’re an agency of learn-it-alls. We recognize that the things we do best, creativity and communications, require a level of nuanced understanding. That’s why it’s imperative for us to hire extraordinarily talented people of all backgrounds and identities. Without that we can’t do the essential work of inspiring bold ideas, fostering constructive dialogue, cultivating meaningful experiences, and driving innovation and creativity for our partners. Your unique point of view—your talent—is what makes you a #BursonPerson, and it’s how we deliver exceptional results for our clients, together.

We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office around four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

When you click the "Submit Application" button below, this will send any information you add below to Burson. Before you do this, we think it's a good idea to read through our  Recruitment Privacy Policy. California residents should read our  California Recruitment Privacy Notice. This explains what we do with your personal data when you apply for a role with us, and, how you can update the information you have provided us with or how to remove it.

Title: Data Analyst / SQL / SOQL

This client is seeking a Data Analyst to support a higher education organization in Massachusetts. They are looking for someone with experience in SQL, SOQL, Salesforce, and Snowflake.

This is a great opportunity for individuals who enjoy working with complex data and turning it into meaningful insight that supports important business decisions. The ideal candidate will have a combination of analytical skills and exposure to Salesforce reporting and cloud-based data platforms.

Strong expertise with SQL and SOQL
Understanding of Salesforce reporting and dashboards

Experience in data analysis and reporting

Daily Responsibilities


75% Hands On

20% Team Collaboration

Title: Data & Product Analyst (Benchmarking), CoStar Debt Solutions
Description:
Job Description

Data & Product Analyst (Benchmarking)

Location: Boston, in-office

About The Role

CoStar Debt Solutions is building the next generation of CRE analytics software — a platform where data, benchmarking intelligence, and client workflows come together in a single experience to support how lenders originate, evaluate, and manage risk.

A central part of this vision is our Benchmarking product, which enables lenders to compare loan performance, pricing, and risk characteristics against a trusted market dataset. This product is rapidly evolving, with significant investment in expanding coverage, improving methodologies, and delivering more actionable insights directly into client workflows.

We’re hiring a Data & Product Analyst to help drive the continued growth of this Benchmarking product — both as a data asset and as a highly client-facing solution.

This role combines data analysis, product development, and direct client engagement, with a strong emphasis on supporting client adoption and enabling the sales process. You will work closely with clients and internal sales teams to demonstrate product value, answer analytical questions, and ensure clients can effectively leverage benchmarking insights in their workflows.

In year one, this role will focus on analyzing benchmark data, supporting product enhancements, and actively participating in client meetings, product demos, and sales support activities. Over time, the role expands into shaping benchmark methodologies, influencing product strategy, and helping scale how benchmarking insights are delivered across the platform.

This is a hybrid role at the intersection of data analysis, product development, and client/sales engagement — ideal for someone who can translate complex datasets into clear insights and communicate them effectively to both internal and external audiences.

What You’ll Own


The analysis of complex datasets that power the Benchmarking product — identifying trends, inconsistencies, and opportunities to improve data coverage and quality
The evolution of benchmark methodologies, outputs, and usability in partnership with Product Managers and cross-functional teams
Direct engagement with clients, including leading and supporting client conversations to understand benchmarking use cases, answer analytical questions, and gather actionable feedback
Active support of sales and go-to-market efforts, including participating in product demonstrations, responding to client inquiries, and helping articulate the value of benchmarking insights
Ongoing client support and enablement, ensuring users can interpret and apply benchmark data effectively in their workflows
Collaboration with engineering teams to ensure accurate delivery of benchmark data, calculations, and features
Monitoring product usage and client feedback to identify areas for improvement and drive continuous enhancements
Data validation, quality checks, and troubleshooting to maintain the reliability and integrity of benchmark datasets
Staying current on industry trends and competitive benchmarking solutions to inform product direction


What We Expect You To Bring


Bachelor’s degree from an accredited, not-for-profit, in person, university or college. Ideally in Data Analytics, Data Science, Business Analytics, Finance, Economics, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
2+ years of experience working with data analytics, financial data, or data-driven products
Strong hands-on experience working with large datasets and performing detailed analysis
Strong communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively with both technical stakeholders and clients
Proven ability to support client-facing interactions, including presenting, explaining analytical concepts, and responding to client questions in real time
Experience with analytics or benchmarking products, or similar data-driven solutions
Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem-solving skills
A track record of commitment to prior employers


Nice To Have


Exposure to SQL, Python, or similar languages
Experience with benchmarking, peer comparison, or performance analytics solutions
Experience supporting client-facing products or working in a customer-oriented environment
Familiarity with financial services, lending, or real estate data
Experience with data visualization tools
Understanding of APIs and data integration concepts


The Growth Path

The first year in this role is focused on deeply understanding the benchmark dataset, client needs, and current product capabilities — contributing directly to data analysis, active client engagement, sales support, and incremental product improvements. You will play a key role in client conversations, product demonstrations, and helping drive adoption of benchmarking insights in real-world workflows.

Beyond that, the role expands into a more strategic position:


Shaping how benchmark methodologies evolve as the dataset scales
Driving enhancements to how insights are surfaced within client workflows
Partnering with sales and client teams to improve how benchmarking is positioned, adopted, and delivered
Influencing product direction based on client usage patterns, feedback, and market trends


This role is a strong path into broader product ownership, client-facing product strategy, or advanced analytics leadership, particularly for someone interested in building and scaling data-driven products that directly support client outcomes and business growth.

What’s Unique About This Role

Most data analyst roles focus on generating insights. This role goes further — connecting those insights directly to product decisions and client outcomes.

You’ll Not Only Analyze Data, But Also


Work directly with clients and sales teams to translate insights into adoption and business value
Play a key role in client-facing discussions that shape both product direction and commercial success
Help bridge the gap between data, product, and revenue-generating activities


What’s in it for you?

When you join CoStar Group, you’ll experience a collaborative and innovative culture working alongside the best and brightest to empower our people and customers to succeed. We offer you generous compensation and performance-based incentives. CoStar Group also invests in your professional and academic growth with internal training, and tuition reimbursement.

Our Benefits Package Includes (but Is Not Limited To)


Comprehensive healthcare coverage: Medical / Vision / Dental / Prescription Drug
Life, legal, and supplementary insurance
Virtual and in person mental health counseling services for individuals and family
Commuter and parking benefits
401(K) retirement plan with matching contributions
Employee stock purchase plan
Paid time off
Tuition reimbursement
On-site fitness center and/or reimbursed fitness center membership costs (location dependent)
Access to CoStar Group’s Employee Resource Groups
Complimentary gourmet coffee, tea, hot chocolate, fresh fruit, and other healthy snacks


We welcome all qualified candidates who are currently eligible to work full-time in the United States to apply. However, please note that CoStar Group is not able to provide visa sponsorship for this position.

CoStar Group is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer; we maintain a drug-free workplace and perform pre-employment substance abuse testing

CoStar Group is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer; we maintain a drug-free workplace and perform pre-employment substance abuse testing

Title: Analyst, Insights, Data & Intelligence
Description:
Who We Are

Burson, part of WPP, is the global communications leader built to create value for clients through reputation. With highly specialized teams, industry-leading technologies and breakthrough creative, we help brands and businesses redefine reputation as a competitive advantage so they can lead today and into the future. When you work at Burson, you are part of a global community of lifelong learners who thrive at the edge of innovation.

WPP (LSE/NYSE: WPP) is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit WPP.com

For more information visit bursonglobal.com and follow us on LinkedIn and Instagram.

More About The Role

The Business Insights, Data & Intelligence (BIDI) team sits at the heart of this work—turning data into strategic insight that drives storytelling, campaign performance, and business outcomes.

Due to our expanding client roster and scopes, Burson is seeking to hire a group of Analysts to join our best-in-class Business Insights, Data & Intelligence (BIDI) team, supporting some of the world’s most recognizable global brands such as The Coca-Cola Company, Verizon and Google. This opportunity is ideal for a recent graduate with internship experience in digital media, marketing, communications, or analytics who is excited to explore the intersection of social media, consumer behavior/social listening, and data-driven storytelling.

As part of a highly collaborative team, you’ll gain hands-on experience uncovering cultural insights, monitoring online conversations, and helping brands make informed decisions through social and digital intelligence. This is a unique opportunity to launch a career in analytics at one of the world’s leading global communications agencies while working across high-profile client accounts.

This role can be based in Atlanta, the New York metro area, or Los Angeles, with a preference for Atlanta-based candidates.

Relocation assistance will be available for candidates open to relocating to Atlanta.

What You'll Do


Support social listening, media monitoring, and reporting initiatives across a portfolio of global brands.
Track cultural moments, emerging conversations, competitor activity, and consumer sentiment across social and digital platforms.
Utilize industry-leading tools such as Brandwatch, Sprinklr, and NewsWhip to uncover actionable insights tied to brand reputation, campaign performance, sustainability, and corporate communications initiatives.
Translate complex social and digital data into clear, compelling narratives and presentations for internal teams and clients.
Help lead recurring reports, dashboards, and performance analyses to measure brand health and campaign impact.
Monitor online conversations and media trends surrounding brand, industry, and corporate topics in real time.
Partner with cross-functional teams across strategy, creative, media, and communications in a fast-paced agency environment.
Gain exposure to both agency operations and client-facing work while developing analytical and storytelling skills.


Experience That Contributes To Success


Bachelor’s degree in communications, marketing, journalism, public relations, data analytics, business, or a related field.
1-2+ years of experience in analytics, insights, or intelligence roles working within or for the PR, Communications, or Advertising industry, with a deep understanding of its unique data landscapes, client demands, and strategic challenges.
Strong curiosity about social media trends, consumer behavior, internet culture, and data-driven insights.
Excellent written, verbal, and presentation skills with the ability to synthesize information clearly and concisely.
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail, with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint; familiarity with pivot tables, Boolean search logic, dashboards, or data visualization tools is a plus.
Comfortable learning new technologies and navigating a dynamic, fast-paced environment.
Collaborative mindset with a willingness to learn, contribute ideas, and work across teams.
Exposure to social listening or analytics platforms such as Brandwatch, Sprinklr, Talkwalker, or Meltwater is a plus.
Additional language fluency in Spanish, Portuguese, or French is a bonus.


Burson is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

Individual compensation varies based on job-related factors, including location, business needs, level of responsibility, experience, and qualifications. We offer a competitive benefits package; click here for more details: https://mybenefits.wpp.com/public/welcome.

The base salary for this position at the time of this posting may range between:

$35,000—$75,000 USD

You Belong At Burson

Our vision is for Burson to be the leading ‘academy company’ for creative communications professionals and emerging leaders. We provide a dynamic environment where ambitious colleagues come to elevate their skills, expand their networks, and ultimately, produce the best solutions for today’s leading brands. Our commitment to your growth is reflected in our robust benefits and people programs, which include professional development opportunities, mentorship programs with industry leaders, a vibrant and inclusive company culture fostering collaboration and innovation and packages designed to reward and retain top talent. We invest in our people because we believe their success is our success.

At Burson, we’re an agency of learn-it-alls. We recognize that the things we do best, creativity and communications, require a level of nuanced understanding. That’s why it’s imperative for us to hire extraordinarily talented people of all backgrounds and identities. Without that we can’t do the essential work of inspiring bold ideas, fostering constructive dialogue, cultivating meaningful experiences, and driving innovation and creativity for our partners. Your unique point of view—your talent—is what makes you a #BursonPerson, and it’s how we deliver exceptional results for our clients, together.

We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office around four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

When you click the "Submit Application" button below, this will send any information you add below to Burson. Before you do this, we think it's a good idea to read through our  Recruitment Privacy Policy. California residents should read our  California Recruitment Privacy Notice. This explains what we do with your personal data when you apply for a role with us, and, how you can update the information you have provided us with or how to remove it.

Title: Quality Systems Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Quality Systems Analyst
Location: Boston, MA
Contractor Work Model: Hybrid - 3 days/wk on-site
Hours: M-F 8am-5pm
Compensation: $70.00 - $125.00/hr
Type: Contract, Potential for extension or conversion to permanent

Overview
We are seeking an experienced Quality Systems Analyst to support our GMP biotechnology operations. In this role, you will serve as a hands-on quality partner to Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) across manufacturing, laboratory, and technical functions. Your primary focus will be facilitating the timely and compliant completion of quality records — including change controls, deviations, laboratory investigations, and controlled document revisions — within our electronic Quality Management System (eQMS). This role will assist with the continued quality oversight of GMP production and testing of clinical trial materials at contract development and manufacturing organizations (CDMOs) and contract testing labs (CTLs). In addition, responsible for review of Regulatory submissions and source data verification. The individual will be a collaborative, quality professional with biotech GMP compliance experience, with exceptional communication skills to support a pipeline of gene editing products. This role will be responsible for document review as well as assisting with the maintenance of phase-appropriate quality systems, including GxP compliance, document control, validation, and quality control.

Responsibilities

Assist in Quality oversight for external GMP production and testing activities for clinical stage products and materials
Review master batch records, executed batch records, analytical testing protocols, methods, reports, testing records, stability documentation, and other quality documentation as needed
Support the batch disposition process by ensuring that all required documents are accurately and properly completed, including all batch related deviations.
Perform review of Regulatory submissions and source data verification
Manage review and approval of analytical testing protocols, methods, and qualification/validation reports
Support review of regulatory filing activities as needed
Assist in establishing and maintaining internal procedures in compliance with FDA, ICH, EU, and other country-specific regulations
Support SMEs in managing documents through the review, approval, and release workflow in the eQMS (e.g., Veeva Vault)
Serve as a resource and coach for SMEs navigating quality processes and eQMS workflows
Identify and escalate quality issues or systemic trends to Quality management
Support audit readiness by ensuring quality records are complete, accurate, and on-time
Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the Quality Systems function
Other duties as assigned

Requirements
Advanced skills in MS Office including SharePoint
Experience working with eQMS software
Strong working knowledge of GMP regulations
Strong pharmaceutical and/or biotechnology knowledge
Strong data analysis skills
Excellent writing and communication skills
Ability to guide non-quality personnel through complex quality processes efficiently and without unnecessary burden


System One, and its subsidiaries including Joulé and Mountain Ltd., are leaders in delivering outsourced services and workforce solutions across North America. We help clients get work done more efficiently and economically, without compromising quality. System One not only serves as a valued partner for our clients, but we offer eligible employees health and welfare benefits coverage options including medical, dental, vision, spending accounts, life insurance, voluntary plans, as well as participation in a 401(k) plan.

System One is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, disability, family care or medical leave status, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

Title: Analyst Reporting & Analysis
Description:
Category/Area of Expertise: Data-Science & Analytics

Job Requisition: 504010

Address: USA-MA-Quincy-1385 Hancock Street

Store Code: ADUSA Distribution Services (5172986)

ADUSA Distribution is the distribution company of Ahold Delhaize USA, providing distribution services to one of the largest grocery retail supply chains in the nation. ADUSA Distribution fosters a culture that inspires and empowers our associates to always deliver exceptional service and value. Our team is supporting the supply chain network evolution to an integrated self-distribution model of the future, serving leading omnichannel grocery brands - Food Lion, Giant Food, The GIANT Company, Hannaford and Stop & Shop. To learn more about our company and the other supply chain companies of Ahold Delhaize USA, visit www.adusasc.com.

What You'll Do (Role Overview)

As an Analyst III, Reporting and Analysis, you'll play a critical role in shaping how data drives decisions across our Distribution & Transportation network.

In this role, you'll go beyond building reports you'll unlock insights, influence strategy, and help leaders understand what's happening across the business and what to do next.

You'll work with large, complex data sets, translate them into meaningful stories, and provide clear recommendations that help drive smarter, faster decisions.
